Abs | GECK1 | Abs(x:float) -> float | When given a number, returns the absolute value of the number. | Functions (GECK); Math Functions; Functions (FO3)
Activate | GECK1 | [Object].Activate(ActionRef:ref?, RunOnActivateBlockFlag:int?) | Executes the activation event associated with the calling object. The nature of the activation event depends on the object type and two optional flags. | Functions (GECK); Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions
ActivateAnim | kNVSE | actor.ActivateAnim(anim sequence path:string, bIsFirstPerson:0/1?, iPriority:integer?, bStartOver:0/1?, fWeight:float?, fEaseInTime:float?, time sync sequence path:string?) | Plays the specified anim at the specified priority and weight, with optional parameters. All arguments except the sequence path are optional. This is a low-level animation function that must be used with care. Use PlayAnimationPath, ForcePlayIdle or PlayGroup if you don't know what you're doing. This function plays the specified animation sequence path at the specified priority and weight. The animation will overlay any other animations playing on the actor. Use DeactivateAnim, CrossFadeAnims or BlendToAnim to deactivate or transition the animation to a different one. * 'bIsFirstperson' specifies if the animation should be played on the player character's first person or third person skeleton. If the target actor is an NPC it will default to 0. If no value is specified and the reference is the player character it will default to the current point of view of view of the player. * 'priority' specifies the priority. The default value is 1. * 'bStartOver' specifies if the animation should start from it's "start" key or resume from it's last offset. The default value is 1. * 'fWeight' specifies the final weight of the animation after easing in. Default value is the sequence weight of the NiControllerSequence which can be retrieved with GetAnimationAttributes. * 'fEaseInTime' specifies a time period over which the weight will be ramped up from 0.0 to the weight specified by fWeight. This is used for easing in sequences so that they do not pop when activated over other running sequences. The default value is the max of the animation's 'Blend' and 'BlendIn' text keys divided by 30. If the animation is missing both of those keys, the default ease in time will be 0. For more info see Easing In and Out. * 'time sync sequence path' is an optional sequence with which the time for the activated sequence is synchronized. This synchronization is done via the morph text keys in each sequence. If at least one pair of identical morph text keys is not found in each sequence, this function will return 0. The path of the sequence must be based from 'Meshes\', but should not include it in the string for most anim group based animations but should contain the prefix for idle animations. The anim must be deactivated or otherwise nothing will happen. Animations do not deactivate by themselves after they finish. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
Actor | GECK1 | actorRef.Actor(Actor:ref) -> int | Returns 1 if a specified Actor is a combat target of a calling reference. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
actorEffect | JohnnyGuitar | actorEffect(actorEffect:form) -> int | Returns the type of an actor effect form. Return values are as follows:<pre> -1 - Invalid type 0 - Actor Effect 1 - Disease 2 - Power (unused) 3 - Lesser Power (unused) 4 - Ability 5 - Poison 10 - Addiction</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions

Useful for supporting multiple languages. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; Actor Value Functions; Actor Functions
ActorValueToStringC | NVSE | |returnType = string.ActorValueToStringC(AVCode:int, typeFilter:int?) -> string (alias: AVStringC) | Returns the localization appropriate string representing the actor value, which is specified by its int code. Useful for supporting multiple languages. The 2nd argument '<i>typeFilter</i>' is only available if using JIP LN, and may be used to control the type of string, defined in the corresponding AVInfo entry, that is returned: *0: Default name *1: Full name (localized) *2: Abbreviated (localized) | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; Actor Value Functions; Actor Functions

AddCreatureToLeveledList | GECK1 | AddCreatureToLeveledList(LeveledCreatureID:ref, CreatureOrLeveledCreatureToAddID:ref, iLevel:int) | Adds a form to a LeveledCreature list. This should be used in the RARE case where we want to add an item to a LeveledCreature list that already exists in the ESM. It's primary use it for modifying lists from the base game in downloadable content. In most cases you should add items to form lists in the editor object windows. This is for those special cases where you want to alter the contents of a list at runtime or want to change it without putting it in your ESP/ESM file. Once altered using this function, it will persist in the save game data. (parameters are Base Objects) It can 'not' be undone. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Leveled List Functions; Creature Functions; Actor Functions; Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions

AddFormToFormList | GECK1 | AddFormToFormList(formList:baseForm, form:baseForm/refr) | Adds a form to a FormList. Unlike ListAddForm, this function adds the new form at the 0th index, adding 1 to the indices of the other list entries. This function was originally intended to modify lists from the base game in downloadable content. The change to the list is savebaked. | List Functions; Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions
AddItem | GECK1 | <nowiki>ActorID|ContainerID</nowiki>.AddItem(ObjectID:ref, Count:int, SilentlyAdd:int?) | Adds Count items of object ObjectID to the calling container's inventory. It has two required parameters (the object to add and the quantity) and one optional parameter, SilentlyAdd (if false, will display a message on screen and play a sound). | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; List Functions; Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions
AddItemAlt | JIP | reference.AddItemAlt(item:form, quantity:int, condition:float?, equipItem:0/1?, noMessage:0/1?) | Adds <i>quantity</i> of the specified item to the calling reference's inventory. *Unlike the vanilla AddItem/AddItemHealthPercent functions, this does not cause the infamous "invisible weapon" glitch when used on actors. *The 1st argument '<i>item</i>' may be either an 'Item' form, a 'Leveled Item', or a 'Form List' of items. *The 3rd argument '<i>condition</i>' is optional and sets the item(s) health percent (100% by default). *The 4th argument '<i>equipItem</i>' is optional. If non-zero, and the calling reference is an actor, the added item(s) will be immediately equipped. This is mainly intended to be used with leveled items, where it is not possible to reliably determine which item(s) was added. *The 5th argument '<i>noMessage</i>' is optional (1 by default). If non-zero, the function will not display corner messages. | Functions (JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Object Functions; Leveled List Functions; List Functions; Savebaked Functions

AddPackageAt | NVSE | actor.AddPackageAt(package:baseform, index:int?, actor:reference?) -> int:index | Adds a package to calling actor's AI package list at a specified index. Using -1 as an index appends the package to the end of the list. Existing packages are not replaced when adding at the same index, indices are shifted up instead. <br><br> This only affects the targeted actor ref, so the base actor form and other refs using it are unaffected This is perfect for dynamically adding packages to spawned actors without having a mess of packages with equally messy conditions on the base actor form. | Functions (NVSE); Actor Functions; AI Functions; Package Functions
AddPerk | GECK1 | ActorRef.AddPerk(perk:form, forTeammates:0/1?) | Increments the rank of the specified Perk on the calling Actor. If actor doesn't already have the perk, it will be added with rank 1. *When called on the Player character, this function will add the perk to the player, <u>unless</u> the optional argument '<i>forTeammates</i>' is non-zero, in which case it will add the perk for all teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the 'uNPCPerks' optional [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] feature is toggled in 'Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'. | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Perk Functions; Savebaked Functions; Functions (FO3)

This function will only work on: 1. Activator-type objects - in which case the primitive will function as a cubic/spherical activator (trigger) - or 2. A <b>CollisionMarker</b> - in which case the primitive will function as a collision plane/box/sphere. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP)

The calling reference may be of <i>any</i> type, <u>except</u> pick-able objects (inventory items). 'Note: For safety reasons, any map markers added by this function are <u>removed</u> (and, therefore, must be re-added) once a saved-game is loaded.' | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Map Functions; Map_Marker_Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP)

AdvanceSeed | ShowOff | AdvanceSeed(numIncrement:UInt) -> bool | If a seed has been set for the calling mod, advances that seed's internal numGenerated count by numIncrement. This will make in the next call(s) of RandSeeded/GetRandomPercentSeeded generate the next number(s) in the pseudo-random sequence. Effectively, this discards what would have been returned by X amount of RandSeeded/GetRandomPercentSeeded calls. This function returns 0 (false) if no seed has been set for the calling mod, 1 (true) otherwise. | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
AgeRace | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].AgeRace(AgeValue:int) | AgeRace changes an actor to be an older or younger version of their current race. In Fallout 3, this command is used only to set the player's age from adult to child before the vault tutorial quest and the Tranquility Lane quest, and back to adult again on completion of these quests. Giving this command a positive parameter will change the target actor to be older by that many steps, up to the maximum available. A negative value will make the actor younger by that many steps, down to the lowest available. This is done by having each age step be a separate "race", and having them linked by the "older race" and "younger race" dropdowns in the "General data" tab of the Race form's properties window. With the default Fallout 3 races, only humans have these "age races", so the AgeRace command will not affect Super Mutants, robots, Ghouls, or other non-humans. If you view all "RACE" type objects in the GECK, you will see that there are four race groups that have age subraces: AfricanAmerican, Asian, Caucasian, and Hispanic. These each have three ages, eg: AsianChild, Asian, and AsianOld. This means that the valid parameter values for the default Fallout 3 races are: -2, -1, 1, 2. Trying to set the actor to an age younger than a child will set the actor to be a child; trying to set them older than old will set them to old. They also all have eg "AsianOldAged" versions, but the ~OldAged versions are not chained with the "older"/"younger" dropdowns, so are ignored by the AgeRace command. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Savebaked Functions; Functions (FO3)
AllowAttack | kNVSE | reference.AllowAttack() | Sets the current Anim Action of the calling reference to 0, allowing attack action while another animation action is playing. This function can also used to enable new actions even when other animation actions are in progress, such as reloading while attack animation is playing. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
animName | NVSE | actorRef.animName(animName:string, baseActor:form?) | Removes an animation from the base actor's or creature's animation list. | Functions (NVSE); Animation Functions
animName | NVSE | actorRef.animName(animName:string, baseActor:form?) | Adds an animation to the base actor's or creature's animation list. | Functions (NVSE); Animation Functions
ApplyEasing | ShowOff | ApplyEasing(input:float{0-1}, easingFuncName:string, easingMode:int{0,1,2}) -> float{0-1} (alias: Ease) | Applies an [https://easings.net/ easing function] to an input value (assumed to be between 0-1), to specify the rate of change of that input over time, and returns that eased value. The easing function to use is specified via easingFuncName (which is NOT case-sensitive). Possible values are in 'Notes'. easingMode is used to specify the mode of the easing function. Possible values: <pre> 0 = EaseIn 1 = EaseOut 2 = EaseInOut </pre> Returns -1 if the operation was not successful (example: invalid easingFuncName string). | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Math Functions
ApplyEasingAlt | ShowOff | ApplyEasingAlt(input:float{0-1}, easingFuncCode:int, easingMode:int{0,1,2}) -> float{0-1} (alias: EaseAlt) | Applies an [https://easings.net/ easing function] to an input value (assumed to be between 0-1), to specify the rate of change of that input over time, and returns that eased value. The easing function to use is specified via easingFuncCode. Possible values: <pre> Sine = 0 Quad = 1 Cubic = 2 Quart = 3 Quint = 4 Circ = 5 Expo = 6 Back = 7 Elastic = 8 Bounce = 9 </pre> easingMode is used to specify the mode of the easing function. Possible values: <pre> 0 = EaseIn 1 = EaseOut 2 = EaseInOut </pre> Returns -1 if the operation was not successful (example: invalid easingFuncName string). | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Math Functions
ApplyImageSpaceModifier | GECK1 | ApplyImageSpaceModifier(ImageSpaceModifierID:ref) (alias: imod) | 'ApplyImageSpaceModifier' will play the specified ImageSpace Modifier. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3); Imagespace Functions
ar_All | NVSE | ar_All(source:array, conditionFunction:function/lambda) -> 0/1 | Takes an array and a function and returns true if all of the elements satisfy the function condition. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
ar_Any | NVSE | ar_Any(source:array, conditionFunction:function/lambda) -> 0/1 | Takes an array and a function and returns true if any of the elements satisfy the function condition. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Append | NVSE | Ar_Append(list:array, element:multi) -> None | Appends to the end of an existing regular array (list) a new element. The regular array must have been initialized prior, for example by using Ar_Construct or Ar_List. This function can not be used to add elements to map or stringmap arrays. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
ar_Cat | LU | ar_Cat(baseArray:array_var, toAddArray:array_var, overrideOldKeys:int?) (alias: pinto_Cat) | Concatenates the elements of the second 'array_var' to the first. If either the first or second array are invalid, will do nothing. Will also do nothing if both arrays have different types. For 'Map' or 'StringMap'-type arrays, if 'overrideOldKeys' is set to non-zero, then the values for the keys from the 'baseArray' can be overwritten if the 'toAddArray' has matching keys. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Array Functions
Ar_Construct | NVSE | Ar_Construct(type:string) -> Array | Returns an empty array of the specified type. To assign the function return to a variable, the let command must be used, rather than 'set'. Type is specified as one of three strings; 'array', 'map' or 'stringmap'. This type can later be checked using TypeOf. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions
Ar_DeepEquals | NVSE | Ar_DeepEquals(array1:array_var, array2:array_var) -> bool (alias: Ar_DeepCompare) | Compares the contents of two arrays, including key/element pairs from nested sub-arrays (though the arrays do not need to have sub-arrays). Returns 1 (true) if the arrays have the same contents in the same order, 0 (false) otherwise. If both arrays are null, also returns 1. In xNVSE v6.2.1, with NVSE Expressions enabled via Let, Eval, etc, the Equality/Inequality operators (==/!=) can be used to compare the surface contents of two arrays. However, any sub-arrays are compared via checking their numeric IDs only, and not by checking their contents, so this function is made for comparing potentially multidimensional arrays in an extensive manner. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Dump | NVSE | Ar_Dump(ToDump:array) -> None | Prints the contents of an array to the console in a human readable format. Intended as a debugging tool. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; Debugging; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_DumpF | NVSE | Ar_DumpF(ToDump:array, fileName:string, bAppend:bool?) -> None | Like Ar_Dump, but prints the contents of the array to the specified file (in the Fallout directory). Intended as a debugging tool. If bAppend is omitted or set to 1, the contents of the file are appended to the file's previous text. Otherwise, all previous content is erased. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; Debugging; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Erase | NVSE | Ar_Erase(Parent:array, ToErase:index-or-range?) -> int | Erases the entry at the specified index, if it exists, from the specified array. The index may be specified using range notation to delete all elements with indices within the range, inclusively. Alternatively, the index argument may be omitted to erase all entries in an array. Returns the count of entries erased. The array may be of any type, but the index must be appropriate to it (int or string). Arrays of type "array" (Ar_List) are always indexed consecutively, so any entries above the erased will have their keys shifted down. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Filter | NVSE | Ar_Filter(source:array, filter:function/lambda) -> array | Takes an array and a function and returns a new array that containing elements that pass the test implemented by the provided filter function. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_FindWhere | NVSE | Ar_FindWhere(Source:array, FindFunction:function/lambda) -> multi | Returns the value of the first element in the provided array that satisfies the provided testing function. If no values satisfy the testing function, 0 is returned. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_ForEach | NVSE | Ar_ForEach(source:array, function:function/lambda) | Takes an array and a function and executes the function once for each array element. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Generate | NVSE | Ar_Generate(numElems:int, valueGenerator:function/lambda, keyGenerator:function/lambda?) -> array | Creates a new Array populated with a specified amount of elements. For each element, starting from the first element to the last one, valueGenerator is called and an element is set to that result. <br> If keyGenerator is omitted, then the resulting array will be of 'Array' type, and each value being generated is being appended to that array. <br> Otherwise, each Value being generated is tied to the Key generated by keyGenerator. In the latter case, the type of the returned array will be a 'StringMap' / 'Map', depending on the type of value that the keyGenerator first returns. <br> Key generation and Value generation are evaluated side-by-side, with the valueGenerator function being called first. Returns Ar_Null if the keyGenerator function ever returns a non-string or non-numeric element. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
ar_Init | NVSE | ar_Init(numElems:int, initElem:anyType) -> array | Creates a new (numerically-indexed) Array populated with a specified amount of an element. Every element of that array is set to the provided elem, which can be of any type. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Insert | NVSE | Ar_Insert(Array:array, Index:int, Element:multi) -> bool | Inserts a single element into an Array-type array (list) at the specified index, provided the index is not greater than the current size of the array. Elements at and above the index are shifted up by one index. Usable only with Array-type arrays. Note that this modifies an array in place- it returns a boolean, not a new array. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_InsertRange | NVSE | Ar_InsertRange(Array:array, Index:int, ToInsert:array) -> bool | Similar to Ar_Insert, but allows for multiple new elements to be inserted at once. Inserts a range of elements into an Array-type array (list) at the specified index, provided the index is not greater than the current size of the array. Elements above the insertion index are shifted up by the number of elements inserted. The range is itself passed as an Array, and each element of the range is inserted into the target Array in order. Usable only with Array-type (regular/list) arrays. Note that this function modifies an array in place and returns a boolean, not a new array. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_List | NVSE | Ar_List(entry0:multi, entry1:multi, ...entry19:multi) -> Array | Returns a list array variable with the specified elements as its entries in the same order. Up to 20 elements may specified during creation, although more can be added using alternative functions on subsequent lines. Array elements may be of any type, and in any combination. To assign the function return to a variable, the let command must be used, rather than 'set'. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Map | NVSE | Ar_Map(Key(int/float/string)::Value(multi), ...Key(int/float/string)::Value(multi)?) -> array | Returns a "map" or "stringmap" array variable with the specified key::element pairs as its entries. Up to 20 key::element pairs may be specified during creation, although more can be added using Let or alternative functions on subsequent lines. All keys must be of the same type, either numeric (ints and floats can be mixed) or string, which will cause the function to return an array of the type "map" or "stringmap", respectively. The array's type can be checked using TypeOf. Array elements may be of any type in any combination. To assign the function return to a variable, the <b>Let .. := ..</b> command must be used, rather than '<b>set .. to ..</b>'. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_MapTo | NVSE | Ar_MapTo(Source:array, MapFunction:function/lambda) -> array | Creates a new Array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array. The type of the new array will be the same as the source array. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Range | NVSE | Ar_Range(Start:int, End:int, <i>step</i>:<i>int</i>) -> array | Returns a regular array (list) containing all int values between the specified start and end (inclusive), at the specified step interval. The step argument is optional and defaults to 1. A negative step interval can be used to order elements in reverse. To assign the function return to a variable, the let command must be used, rather than 'set'. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Ar_Unique | NVSE | Ar_Unique(source:array_var) -> array | Returns a (non-deeply) copied version of the source array that has no duplicate values. The first instance of a value in the array is what's kept in the new array; all other duplicate values following that are discarded. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
armor | JohnnyGuitar | armor(armor:baseform, modelID:int) -> array | Returns an array of alternate texture forms of a specified Armor model. Model IDs are as follows:<pre> 1 - Male Biped model 2 - Female Biped model 3 - Male World model 4 - Female World model </pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Armor Functions; Texture Functions

If assertion is false, will print an error message and show the script line that isn't working in console. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; NVSE-Aware Functions

AttachAshPileEx | JIP | reference.AttachAshPileEx(activator:form) | Attaches an 'Ash Pile' to the calling actor. Unlike the vanilla AttachAshPile, this function allows specifying an 'Activator' form to be used for the ash pile model/properties, thus making it possible to create and use custom-made ash piles, in addition to the only two available in the game. *In order to use a custom 'Activator' form as an ash pile, the base form must first be marked as one, using MarkActivatorAshPile. | Savebaked Functions; Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP)
AttachExtraCamera | JIP | reference.AttachExtraCamera(cameraName:string, addOrRemove:1/0, parentNode:string?) -> successful:0/1 | Attaches an extra scene camera at the specified 'NiNode' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The scene, as viewed from the camera, can then be projected onto either another model block, or an 'image'-type UI tile, using 'ProjectExtraCamera'. The function takes 3 arguments, as follows: *'<i>cameraName</i>' - Any unique name that will be used for identifying and referencing the camera. *'<i>addOrRemove</i>' - Pass '1' to attach, or '0' to remove the camera. *'<i>parentNode</i>' - A name (case-sensitive) of a 'NiNode' in the calling reference's 3D model, to which the camera will be attached. ==Notes== *The camera's XYZ position/rotation can be modified using 'SetNifBlockTranslation / SetNifBlockRotation' (the block name is the camera name). *If the camera was successfully attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The function will 'fail' if the calling reference's 3D model isn't loaded. *The camera will be removed, 'a)' if the calling reference's 3D is un(re)loaded, or 'b)' when loading a game. *This function should be considered '<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP)
AttachFormModel | JIPPPLN | AttachFormModel(Target:form, ToAttach:form, addOrRemove:int, targetAndSuffix:formattedString?, formatVars (up to 20):?) -> successful:0/1 | Attaches an entire form’s runtime model (all runtime nodes created via AttachModel/InsertNode or present in the base model) to a selected NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. This function is useful for transferring a set of attached models from one form to another. The function takes '3(+) arguments', as follows: *'<i>refOrForm</i>' – Either an 'object reference' or a 'base form'. In the former case, the models will be attached to the target node in the 3D model of that specific reference only, whereas in the latter case, they will be attached in the 3D model of any reference instance of the base form. *'<i>formToAttach</i>' – A reference or base form whose attached models will be used. All NiNodes and models that were previously attached to this form (via AttachModel or InsertNode) will be copied and attached to the target form. *'<i>addOrRemove</i>' – Either attach or remove the models. Use one of the following values: <pre>0 Remove; Pending reload 1 Attach; Pending reload 2 Remove; Instantaneous 3 Attach; Instantaneous</pre> :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Pending reload'</span> – Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or Update3D is called on it. :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Instantaneous'</span> – Changes will be applied <u>instantly</u>. *'<i>targetAndSuffix</i>' – An 'optional formatted string' containing the name (<u>'case-sensitive'</u>) of an existing NiNode in the target form’s 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '"&#124;"', an optional suffix to append to the name of every block in the attached form. The suffix (maximum 15 characters) is enclosed by asterisks and is useful to prevent name collisions when multiple copies of the same form are attached. If omitted, the function attaches the models to the root NiNode of the target form. *'<i>formatVars (up to 20)</i>' – Optional variables used in the formatted string. | Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions
AttachLine | JIP | reference.AttachLine(targetNode:string, lineName:string, length:float, red:int, green:int, blue:int, alpha:int) -> successful:0/1 | Attaches a line mesh to a selected node in the loaded 3D model of the calling reference. The function takes 7 arguments, as follows: *'<i>targetNode</i>' - The name of the parent node where the line is attached as a child. *'<i>lineName</i>' - The name that is given to the line block (this name can later be used to transform/remove the line using any of the 'NifBlock' functions). *'<i>length</i>' - The initial length of the line, in game units (the length can later be changed using SetNifBlockScale; 'length = scale'). *'<i>red, green, blue & alpha</i>' - The RGB colour and alpha (opacity) of the line; each 0-255. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions
AttachModel | JIP | AttachModel(refOrForm:form, addOrRemove:int, targetAndFile:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> successful:0/1 | Attaches a complete copy of a model from a selected NIF file to an existing NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. The function takes 3(+) arguments, as follows: *'<i>refOrForm</i>' - Either an 'object reference' or a 'base form'. In the former case, the model will be attached to the target node in the 3D model of that specific reference <u>only</u>, whereas in the latter case, it will be attached in the 3D model of <u>any</u> reference instance of the base form. *'<i>addOrRemove</i>' - Either attach, or remove a previously attached model. Use one of the following values: <pre>0&#09;Remove; Pending reload 1&#09;Attach; Pending reload 2&#09;Remove; Instantaneous 3&#09;Attach; Instantaneous</pre> :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Pending reload'</span> - Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or Update3D is called on it. :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Instantaneous'</span> - Changes will be applied <u>instantly</u>. *'<i>targetAndFile</i>' - A 'formatted string' containing the name (<u>'case-sensitive'</u>) of an existing NiNode in the object's 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '"&#124;"', the path of the 'NIF file' containing the model (<u>NOT case-sensitive</u>). Note that the path must be relative to '..\Data\Meshes\' :*<span style="color:#EFEF3F">'Block name suffixes'</span> - The function can <u>optionally</u> append a suffix to the name of every block in the attached NIF. This is useful when attaching multiple copies of the same NIF to a model, and/or for avoiding an outcome where the model contains multiple blocks with the same name (which may cause various issues). Suffixes have a character limit of 15. ::To specify a suffix, add the suffix string immediately before the NIF path, enclosed by asterisks. For example: '"Bip01 R Thigh&#124;*_CPY1*weapons\9mmSMG.nif"'. | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); File Functions; Model Functions
AttachUIComponent | JIP | reference.AttachUIComponent(targetNode:string, component:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> successful:0/1 | Generates UI components from a formatted string and attaches them at the specified 'NiNode' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. ==Notes== *The name of the target 'NiNode' (1st argument) is <u>case-sensitive</u>. *If the components were successfully generated and attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The attached components can be manipulated using any of the available (and applicable) UI functions. *Internally, the components are injected into 'HUDMainMenu' menu root. *The component must be composed of a single 'rect' parent, with all the other components as its children. *To remove the attached components, use UnloadUIComponent. *This function should be considered '<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions
AttachUIXML | JIP | reference.AttachUIXML(targetNode:string, filePath:string) -> successful:0/1 | Generates UI components from an XML file and attaches them at the specified 'NiNode' of the calling reference's loaded 3D model. ==Notes== *The name of the target 'NiNode' (1st argument) is <u>case-sensitive</u>. *The 'XML file' path (2nd argument) is relative to the game's installation path. *If the components were successfully generated and attached, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *The attached components can be manipulated using any of the available (and applicable) UI functions. *Internally, the components are injected into 'HUDMainMenu' menu root. *The XML must be composed of a single 'rect' parent, with all the other components as its children. *To remove the attached components, use UnloadUIComponent. *This function should be considered '<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; File Functions

AuxTimerPaused | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerPaused(timerName:string, setPaused:int, owner:baseForm?) -> wasPaused:bool | Returns 1 if the specified Auxiliary Timer on the specified form is currently paused, 0 otherwise. Afterwards, if setPaused is specified, will set if the timer is paused or not. If the timer does not exist, returns -1. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxTimerStart | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerStart(timerName:string, timeToCountdown:float?, flags:int?, owner:baseForm?) -> success:bool | Creates and starts Auxiliary Timer on an owning form to start counting down from timeToCountdown with the specified flags. If the timer exists already, will instead restart it, and it will be unpaused if it was paused. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. Once the timer for an AuxTimer runs out, it will will trigger the OnAuxTimerStop event. Afterwards, the AuxTimer will be deleted automatically unless the 'Auto Restarts' flag is on. In that case, the timer will restart, triggering the OnAuxTimerStart event. 'AuxTimer Flags:' <pre> Bit# &#09; Value &#09; Description 0 &#09; 1 &#09; Run In MenuMode 1 &#09; 2 &#09; Run In GameMode 2 &#09; 4 &#09; Count In Seconds (if off, counts down in frames) 3 &#09; 8 &#09; Auto-Remove On Load And MainMenu (has no effect on permanent AuxTimers, only temporary ones) 4 &#09; 16 &#09; Auto Restarts 5 &#09; 32 &#09; Is Paused 6 &#09; 64 &#09; Ignore TimeMult In MenuMode (only used for seconds-based timers) 7 &#09; 128 &#09; Don't Run When Paused (won't run in main menu, pause menu, console menu) 8 &#09; 256 &#09; Run OnTimerUpdate Event (see SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler) - v1.65 9 &#09; 512 &#09; Ignore Turbo - If on, seconds-based timers that don't ignore TimeMult won't be slowed down by turbo - v1.80 10 &#09; 1024 &#09; Ignore TimeMult In GameMode (only used for seconds-based timers) - v1.80 </pre> The default-enabled flags are 'Run In MenuMode', 'Run In GameMode', and 'Count In Seconds'.<br> Therefore, default timers obey TimeMult, and are slowed down by the Turbo slow-mo effect (unlike the player). The 'Ignore Turbo' flag is recommended to be enabled if the timer should run according to the player's perspective of time, not the world's. This is because the player isn't slowed down by Turbo, but the rest of the world is. *This flag only has an effect if the 'Ignore TimeMult' flags aren't enabled. Otherwise, it is ignored. For UI mods, it is usually recommended to enable the 'Ignore TimeMult' flags, as most UI features in the vanilla game aren't affected by TimeMult. If the AuxTimer's owner form becomes invalid at any time while it is counting down, the AuxTimer will be deleted. This will NOT fire the OnAuxTimerStop event. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxTimerStop | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerStop(timerName:string, fireEvent:bool?, owner:baseForm?) -> success:bool (alias: AuxTimerErase) | Stops and erases an Auxiliary Timer on a form. This will only fire the OnAuxTimerStop event if fireEvent is set to non-0. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxTimerTimeElapsed | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerTimeElapsed(timerName:string, unused:int, owner:baseForm?) -> timeElapsed:float | Returns the amount of time passed since the specified Auxiliary Timer started. If the timer does not exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1. Does NOT account for time elapsed while the timer is paused, if it becomes paused by AuxTimerPaused. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxTimerTimeLeft | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerTimeLeft(timerName:string, owner:baseForm?) -> timeLeft:float | Returns the time left for an Auxiliary Timer before it stops on its own. If the timer doesn't exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxTimerTimeToCountdown | ShowOff | ownerRefr.AuxTimerTimeToCountdown(timerName:string, owner:baseForm?) -> timeToCountdown:float | Returns the original amount of time that the Auxiliary Timer was ordered to start counting down. If the timer doesn't exist or was fully stopped before this is called, returns -1. If no owner form for the AuxTimer is specified, PlayerRef will be used. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Aux Functions; Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Savebaked Functions
AuxVarGetFltCond | JIP | AuxVarGetFltCond(Quest:string, Integer:int) | A modified form of AuxiliaryVariableGetFloat that works as a Condition. Returns the float value of the element at index 0 of the specified Auxiliary-Variable for the calling ref. The Aux Var's Name is specified in the Parameter window, where one must select a Quest which has a Quest Script that contains a String Variable. The contents of this String will be used as the Name. The Integer field indicates on what index this string variable has in the script. {{Template:ModIndexOverride}} | Functions_(JIP); Auxiliary-Variable Functions (JIP); Aux Functions; Condition Functions

Archetype codes are as follows: <pre> 0: Value Modifier 1: Script 2: Dispel 3: Cure Disease 11: Invisibility 12: Chameleon 13: Light 16: Lock 17: Open 18: Bound Item 19: Summon Creature 24: Paralysis 30: Cure Paralysis 31: Cure Addiction 32: Cure Poison 33: Concussion 34: Value and Parts 35: Limb Condition 36: Turbo </pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions

BlendToAnim | kNVSE | actor.BlendToAnim(source sequence path:string, dest sequence path:string, bFirstPerson:0/1?, fDuration:float?, fDestFrame:float?, fSourceWeight:float?, fDestWeight:float?, iPriority:integer?, time sync sequence path:string?) | Performs a blend transition from the specified source animation sequence to the specified offset in the destination animation sequence over the specified duration (given in seconds). The source sequence must be an actively playing animation. If fDestFrame is 0.0, the blend will be to the first frame of the destination sequence. iPriority specifies the priority at which to (re)activate both sequences. fSourceWeight and fDestWeight specify the final weights to assign to the source and destination NiControllerSequence objects. If a time sync sequence is provided, the destination NiControllerSequence is synchronized to it. This function returns 0 if the blend cannot be successfully started. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
BlendToAnimFromPose | kNVSE | reference.BlendToAnimFromPose(sequence path:string, bFirstPerson:0/1?, fDuration:float?, fDestFrame:float?, iPriority:integer?, time sync sequence path:string?) | Performs a blend transition from the current scene graph state of the character to the specified frame in the destination sequence. Optional fDestFrame argument allows specifying the offset of the animation in which it should start. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
blurRadius | GECK1 | blurRadius(blurRadius:float?, blurIntensity:float?, scanlineFrequency:float?) | Sets parameters for HUD glow. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3); Console Functions
bodyPartData | JohnnyGuitar | bodyPartData(bodyPartData:form, typeID:int, traitID:int) -> string_var | Returns String trait of a Body Part Data form, using body part type ID and trait ID. ==Body-Part Type IDs== <pre>0&#09;Torso 1&#09;Head 1 2&#09;Head 2 3&#09;Left Arm 1 4&#09;Left Arm 2 5&#09;Right Arm 1 6&#09;Right Arm 2 7&#09;Left Leg 1 8&#09;Left Leg 2 9&#09;Left Leg 3 10&#09;Right Leg 1 11&#09;Right Leg 2 12&#09;Right Leg 3 13&#09;Brain 14&#09;Weapon</pre> ==Body-Part Trait IDs== <pre> 1&#09;Part Node 2&#09;VATS Target 3&#09;Start Node 4&#09;Part Name 5&#09;Target Bone</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)
bodyPartID | JohnnyGuitar | Actor.bodyPartID(bodyPartID:int?) | Highlights the body part of an actor. Can be used in scripts, unlike HighlightBodyPart. Like HighlightBodyPart, only a single actor can be highlighted at a time. This function is 'session-persistent'. Body part IDs are:<pre> No Parameter - Everything except Weapon 0 - Torso 1 - Head1 2 - Head2 3 - LeftArm1 4 - LeftArm2 5 - RightArm1 6 - RightArm2 7 - LeftLeg1 8 - LeftLeg2 9 - LeftLeg3 10 - RightLeg1 11 - RightLeg2 12 - RightLeg3 13 - Brain 14 - Weapon 15 - Everything except Weapon</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)
bOn | ShowOff | bOn(bOn:int?) -> bool | Toggles all quest messages on/off, and returns true if a successful change was made. If bOn is left at default or is set to -1, will return if the quest messages were toggled on/off by this function. | Quest Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
book | JohnnyGuitar | book(book:baseform, avCode:int) | Sets skill actor value of a Book. Accepts actor value codes between 32 and 46 (skills), and -1 to make the book not teach any skill. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Book Functions
book | JohnnyGuitar | book(book:baseform) -> avCode | Returns skill actor value that a Book modifies. Returns -1 for non-skill books. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Book Functions

Break | NVSE | Break() | Used only inside of a While or Foreach loop block to immediately exit the loop, bypassing it's subsequent lines and ignoring it's condition. See the loop function pages for further information. | Functions_(NVSE)
bsaName | JohnnyGuitar | bsaName(bsaName:string) -> 0/1 | Returns 1 if a specified BSA archive (relative to Data folder) is currently loaded in the game. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Debugging; File Functions
buttonID | JohnnyGuitar | buttonID(buttonID:int, onOrOff:int) | Toggles a specified Pip-Boy button light. Button IDs are as follows: <pre> 0 - Stats 1 - Items 2 - Data</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); UI Functions; Pip-Boy Functions
Call | NVSE | runOnSubject.Call(UDFList:FormList, bBreakIfFalse:int?) -> int | The condition-function equivalent of Call. Calls every UDF script in the specified 'UDFList' formlist, and returns the value returned by the last-called UDF. If no values are returned, then it returns 0. If the return value is a number (TypeOf == "Number"), then it returns that directly. Otherwise, the value is converted into a boolean. For example, if an array is the last return value, then it returns if the array is valid or not (1 or 0, respectively). The UDFs are executed in the order they are arranged in the formlist. The "Run On" subject for the condition is passed to the UDF, and can be retrieved using GetSelf/This. No function arguments are passed, so the scripts should begin with: <pre> begin Function { } </pre> If 'bBreakIfFalse' is set to non-zero, then if a UDF returns a false-y value during formlist loop, it will prevent further UDFs from being called and return 0. Returns 0 when called from a script (not meant for use there). | Functions_(NVSE); User Defined Functions; Condition Functions
Call | NVSE | reference.Call(function:script, multi:arg(up to 15)?) -> multi | Calls a User Defined Function. Should be followed by a list of arguments matching the types expected by the function. If a calling reference is specified, commands within the function body will operate on that reference. Call returns the value specified by the function. | Functions_(NVSE); User Defined Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
CallAfterFrames | NVSE | reference.CallAfterFrames(frames:int, udf:script, runWhen:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) | Executes a User Defined Function or an inline Lambda script after the specified amount of frames have elapsed in-game Only runs in GameMode by default, or if runWhen is set to 0. If runWhen is set to 1, will 'also' run in MenuMode. If it is set to 2, will 'also' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open (new in 6.3.0). Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. Arguments can be passed like Call to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. | Functions (NVSE)
CallAfterSeconds | NVSE | reference.CallAfterSeconds(seconds:float, udf:script, runWhen:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) (alias: CallAfter) | Executes a User Defined Function or an inline Lambda script after specified amount of seconds have elapsed in-game. Only runs in GameMode by default, or if runWhen is set to 0. If runWhen is set to 1, will 'also' run in MenuMode. If it is set to 2, will 'also' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open (new in 6.3.0). Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that timers created using this function are 'NOT savebaked', which could potentially cause bugs if a timer was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the timer was running. Arguments can be passed like Call to the function (since xNVSE 6.2.6). If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. | Functions (NVSE)
CallForSeconds | NVSE | reference.CallForSeconds(seconds:float, udf:script, runWhen:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) (alias: CallFor) | Executes a User Defined Function or an inline Lambda script each frame until specified amount of seconds have elapsed in-game. Only runs in GameMode by default, or if runWhen is set to 0. If runWhen is set to 1, will 'also' run in MenuMode. If it is set to 2, will 'also' pause the callback while the main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open (new in 6.3.0). Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that timers created using this function are 'NOT savebaked', which could potentially cause bugs if a timer was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the timer was running. Arguments can be passed like Call to the function (since xNVSE 6.2.6). If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. | Functions (NVSE)
CallWhen | NVSE | reference.CallWhen(udf:script, condition:script, flags:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) | Executes a function when a specified condition function returns true exactly once. Condition is evaluated 'each frame' until it becomes true. Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are 'NOT savebaked', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. Runs in both GameMode and MenuMode, unless DontRunInMenuMode and/or DontRunInGameMode flags are enabled (see below). Will also run while paused (meaning while main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open) unless DontRunWhilePaused is enabled. <u>The following features have been added starting from xNVSE 6.2.6.</u> flags is a bitmask, whose bits toggle on/off the following features: <pre> PassArgsToTheCalledFunction = 1 (bit #0) - xNVSE 6.2.6 PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1) - xNVSE 6.2.6 DontRunInMenuMode = 4 (bit #2) - xNVSE 6.2.8 DontRunInGameMode = 8 (bit #3) - xNVSE 6.2.8 DontRunWhilePaused = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0 </pre> For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 (Binary Notation, or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on. By default, all flags are off (flags = 0). Arguments can be passed like Call to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If PassArgsToTheCalledFunction is on, then the Call-like args will be passed to the udf, and if PassArgsToTheConditionFunction is on, they'll be passed to the condition UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. | Functions (NVSE)
CallWhile | NVSE | reference.CallWhile(udf:script, condition:script, flags:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) | Executes a User Defined Function or an inline Lambda script 'each frame' while specified condition UDF (or a Lambda) returns true. If it returns false, then the handler will never be re-evaluated again, unless it is reset by another call to CallWhile. Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are 'NOT savebaked', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. Runs in both GameMode and MenuMode, unless DontRunInMenuMode and/or DontRunInGameMode flags are enabled (see below). Will also run while paused (meaning while main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open) unless DontRunWhilePaused is enabled. <u>The following features have been added starting from xNVSE 6.2.6.</u> flags is a bitmask, whose bits toggle on/off the following features: <pre> PassArgsToTheCalledFunction = 1 (bit #0) - xNVSE 6.2.6 PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1) - xNVSE 6.2.6 DontRunInMenuMode = 4 (bit #2) - xNVSE 6.2.8 DontRunInGameMode = 8 (bit #3) - xNVSE 6.2.8 DontRunWhilePaused = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0 </pre> For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 (Binary Notation, or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on. By default, all flags are off (flags = 0). Arguments can be passed like Call to the function. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If PassArgsToTheCalledFunction is on, then the Call-like args will be passed to the udf, and if PassArgsToTheConditionFunction is on, they'll be passed to the condition UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. | Functions (NVSE)
CallWhilePerSeconds | NVSE | reference.CallWhilePerSeconds(timeInterval:float, udf:script, condition:script, flags:int?, multi:arg(up to 15)?) | Calls UDF every couple of seconds, until the condition UDF returns false. Every queued call is removed when loading the game (GetGameLoaded), so they may need to be reset during then. *NOTE: This means that callbacks created using this function are 'NOT savebaked', which could potentially cause bugs if a callback was expected to keep running after saving and reloading a save while the callback was running. flags is a bitmask, whose bits toggle on/off the following features: <pre> PassArgsToTheCalledFunction = 1 (bit #0) PassArgsToTheConditionFunction = 2 (bit #1) DontRunInMenuMode = 4 (bit #2) DontRunInGameMode = 8 (bit #3) DontRunWhilePaused = 16 (bit #4) - xNVSE 6.3.0 </pre> For example, if flags = 0, then none of these features are on. If flags = 0b11 (Binary Notation, or 3 in decimal), then the first two are on.<br> By default, all flags are off (flags = 0). DontRunWhilePaused means that the callback will be delayed while main menu / pause menu / console menu MenuModes are open. Arguments can be passed to the function, just like Call. If an arg is an array, then its lifetime will be extended until the callbacks have stopped. If PassArgsToTheCalledFunction is on, then the Call-like args will be passed to the udf, and if PassArgsToTheConditionFunction is on, they'll be passed to the condition UDF. Both flags can be on at the same time. | Functions (NVSE)
Casino | VEGAS1 | Casino(Casino:form, minBet:int?, maxBet:int?, maxWinnings:int?) (alias: sbjmp) | Displays the BlackJack menu. Player must meet Casino requirements for the menu to display, such as having Casino Chips in the inventory and not being banned from the casino. | Functions (Vegas); UI Functions; Casino Functions

CastImmediate | JIP | reference.CastImmediate(gameEffect:ref, casterRef:ref?) | An alternate version of the vanilla CastImmediateOnSelf. Instantly invokes the specified 'Game Effect' (<i>Actor Effect</i>, <i>Object Effect</i> or <i>Ingestible</i>) on the calling actor reference. If the second (optional) argument '<i>casterRef</i>' is <u>not</u> omitted, the effects being applied on the calling reference will be registered as if they were inflicted by <i>casterRef</i>. *If any of the 'Effect Items' of the effect are considered hostile, this will count as an assault. *If the effect causes the death of the calling actor, and the caster is the player character, XP will be gained for the kill. 'Note:' For the above to work properly, all the "hostile" 'Effect Items' must have their 'Range' set to '<i>Touch</i>'. *If the target is not a Ghost, the spell's type is NOT Disease, Ability or Addiction, and a casterRef is specified, calling this function can trigger OnHit events for each applicable Effect Item. *If the target reference is not currently loaded, does nothing. | Functions (JIP); Effect Functions (JIP); Game Effect Functions; Savebaked Functions

checkPerks | ShowOff | checkPerks(checkPerks:bool?, checkAssigned:bool?) -> int | Returns the amount of Skill/Perk points (specified by checkPerks) for the active Level-Up Menu. If checkAssigned is true (non-zero), will check for the amount of Skills/Perks that were assigned by the player. Otherwise, will return the <u>max</u> amount of Skills/Perks that can be assigned. Returns -1 if the Perk Menu is not opened. If this is called in an OnMenuClose event, the menu is also considered to be unopened, so it returns -1. Accounts for the 'Adjust Gained Skill Points' Perk Entry Point. Also accounts for lStewieAl's Tweaks's 'bModifySkillPointsEarned' and 'iPerksPerLevel'. | Player Functions; UI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

'Details on caching files:' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Caches should be cleared when they cease to be useful. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

ClickMenuButton | JIP | ClickMenuButton(componentPath:string, clickTimes:int?) -> successful:0/1 | Simulates the user clicking (one or more times) on the specified UI button. *This function is intended for UI buttons performing a particular, hardcoded menu function (<i>Accept</i>, <i>Cancel</i>, <i>Select</i>, <i>Switch</i>, etc.). For miscellaneous UI buttons, see ClickMenuTile. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Input Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

ClimateRemoveWeatherType | JIP | ClimateRemoveWeatherType(climate:ref, weather:ref) -> successful:0/1 | Removes a weather from a climate's weather types. | Functions_(JIP); Climate Functions (JIP)
CloseActiveMenu | JIP | CloseActiveMenu(closeAll:0/1?) | Force-closes the current, topmost active menu or, if the optional '<i>closeAll</i>' argument is non-zero, closes 'ALL' currently open menus. *Unlike Con_CloseAllMenus, which is partially broken, this function should be considered safe to use. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
Command | GECK1 | Command(Command:string, RadioStation:ObjectReferenceID?) (alias: PRAD) | Controls the Pipboy radio. Can switch it on or off and tune into a specific radio station. | Functions (GECK); Radio Functions; Functions (FO3); Pip-Boy Functions
CompareNames | FOSE1 | [firstForm:reference].CompareNames(secondForm:baseForm, firstForm:baseForm) -> float | Compares a form's FullName to another form's FullName. firstForm can be provided as either a reference, or a baseForm, depending on where it is supplied. In either case, the function uses firstForm's BaseForm to get its name. If firstForm and secondForm's names match completely, returns 0. Else, returns either 1 or -1, depending on which form had the longest name. If the firstForm is the longest, will return -1, and if secondForm is longest, will return 1. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Name Functions; Functions (FO3)
CompareScripts | NVSE | CompareScripts(Script:script, Script:script) -> bool | Returns true (1) if the compiled scripts are identical. | Functions_(NVSE); Script Functions
CompileScript | NVSE | CompileScript(path:string, forceRecompile:bool?) -> script:form (alias: GetUDFFromFile) | Returns a compiled script from a text file pointed by the path arg. The path arg is relative to '"data/nvse/user_defined_functions/.."'. If it was formatted like a UDF, then the returned script can be used like a regular UDF, meaning it can be passed to functions like Call. *This makes it a great way to store long lines of code that wouldn't fit in a Lambda due to the line character limit. **Of course, it's only useful if you're running scripts without wanting to use an esp/esm file that could store an actual UDF. The compiled script is cached for performance, so it won't be recompiled after the first call.<br> To "hot-reload" and recompile the script to account for mid-game changes, likely for testing purposes, set forceRecompile to 1. | Functions_(NVSE); Script Functions
CompleteAllObjectives | GECK1 | CompleteAllObjectives(QuestID:ref) | Marks all objectives for a quest as completed. | Functions (GECK); Savebaked Functions; Quest Functions; Functions (FO3)
CompleteAllQuestStages | GECK1 | CompleteAllQuestStages() (alias: caqs) | Marks all stages for all quests as completed. | Console Functions; Savebaked Functions; Quest Functions; Functions (FO3)
CompleteQuest | GECK1 | CompleteQuest(QuestID:ref) | Marks the quest as complete. The only real effect of this is that the quest is will be dimmed and moved below all active quests in the Pip-Boy, and will no longer be selectable as an active quest. The "Quest Completed" UI message is also displayed. | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Savebaked Functions; Functions (FO3)
con_CloseAllMenus | FOSE1 | con_CloseAllMenus() | Closes all open menus. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); UI Functions; Functions (FO3)

con_SaveINI | FOSE1 | con_SaveINI() | Writes all the .ini files. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (FO3)
Con_SCOF | NVSE | Con_SCOF(FileName:string) -> none | Runs the console command SCOF (Set Console Output File) via a script. This saves all subsequent console output during a game session to an external text file in the main game directory. The file name is specified as a string argument, and if it does not exist then it will be created, otherwise it will be appended to. This function can be used to create Debug Dumps. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging
con_SetGameSetting | FOSE1 | con_SetGameSetting(settingName:string, newValue:string) | Sets a game setting. Does not accept variables as arguments. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); GameSetting Functions; Functions (FO3)
con_SetINISetting | FOSE1 | con_SetINISetting(setting:string, newValue:string) | Sets the value of an INI setting. Setting string is of the format "SettingName:CategoryName" where CategoryName is the header under which the setting appears in the 'Fallout.ini' / 'FalloutPrefs.ini' / 'FalloutCustom.ini' files. Does not accept variables as arguments. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (FO3)

ConditionPrint | ShowOff | ConditionPrint() -> 1 | Returns 1 and prints a message to console. Meant for use as a debugging condition, in order to see when a condition is evaluated and also check if previous conditions passed. The printed message includes information about the reference which is passed by the game to the condition function, such as its refID and editorID. The latter only shows up if an editorID was set for the reference and if JohnnyGuitar NVSE's bLoadEditorIDs is set to 1. | Condition Functions; Debugging; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

Continue | NVSE | Continue() | Added by NVSE V4. Used only inside of a While or Foreach loop block to bypass subsequent lines and return code execution to its beginning, repeating the loop if it's condition evaluates true. See the loop function pages for further information. | Functions_(NVSE)
CopyAnimationsToForm | kNVSE | |arguments =.CopyAnimationsToForm(from form:any form, to form:any form) | Copies the kNVSE AnimGroupOverride animations, including conditions and other attributes, from one form to another. This function is useful for transferring animations between different forms, preserving conditions and other related data. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
CopyFaceGenFrom | LU | reference.CopyFaceGenFrom(sourceNPC:form?) | Copies all visual appearance properties from the specified source 'NPC' form to the calling actor. *The function copies race, sex, face geometry (FaceGen data), skin tone, hair (style, length and color), eyes and miscellaneous head/face parts. *The function modifies the calling actor's base form, thus changes will be applied on any instance of the same base form in the game. *When called on the player character, any changes made using this function will be recorded in the game's save file, and will apply on the current game only, whereas changes made to other NPCs' appearance will <u>not</u> be saved, will remain in effect for the duration of the current game session, and will be reset once the game is restarted. *Visual update is already performed by the function, and is therefore not required separately. 'Undoing changes:' *The function keeps an internal backup of the pre-modified properties. *To undo changes, simply call the function omitting the 'sourceNPC' argument. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; FaceGen Functions; Savebaked Functions
CopyIR | NVSE | InvRef.CopyIR(Location:Container) -> Success:bool | Copies an inventory reference to the specified container. The calling object needn't be in a container and remains valid after the command is called. If the calling object is equipped, the copy will not be equipped. Returns true if it succeeds, although it is not necessary to check this. | Functions_(NVSE); Object Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
CopyIRAlt | NVSE | InvRef.CopyIRAlt(Location:Container) -> copiedInvRef:reference | Copies an inventory reference to the specified container. The calling object needn't be in a container and remains valid after the command is called. If the calling object is equipped, the copy will not be equipped. Returns the newly copied inventory reference if it succeeds. | Functions_(NVSE); Object Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
CopyRuntimeNodes | JIPPPLN | CopyRuntimeNodes(toForm:form, fromForm:form, copyMode:int?) -> successful:0/1 | Copies the 'RuntimeNodes' from one form to another. This is much faster than manually looping AttachModel/InsertNode/AttachFormModel. Use it to create an exact copy of a form’s runtime setup, or to concatenate another form’s RuntimeNodes onto the target. | Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions

It is mostly useful for creating a stack of one or more items to be added to a container with CopyIR. | Functions_(NVSE); Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions

CrossFadeAnims | kNVSE | actor.CrossFadeAnims(source sequence path:string, dest sequence path:string, bFirstPerson:0/1?, fEaseInTime:float?, iPriority:integer?, bStartOver:0/1?, fWeight:float?, time sync sequence path:string?) | Triggers a cross-fade transition between two specified animation sequences over a specified duration. The source sequence must be an actively playing anim sequence. Returns 1 if it succeeds or 0 if it fails. This is a low level animation function that should be used with care. * iPriority specifies the new priority for both sequences. * fWeight specifies the weight of the destination sequence at the end of the transition (ramping up from 0.0 at its start). * If a time sync sequence is specified, the destination sequence is synchronized with it. The source anim must be active and destination anim deactivated or otherwise nothing will happen. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions

This function can also be used as a condition. | Functions_(JIP); Condition Functions; Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Crosshair Functions
DamageActorValue | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].DamageActorValue([[ActorValue]]:statName, Value:<nowiki>int|float</nowiki>) (alias: DamageAV) | 'DamageActorValue' is called on a Reference and takes two parameters, an ActorValue and a value. The Function alters the damage modifier of a given actor value. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. DamageActorValue will decrease the modifier by the given amount. | Functions (GECK); Actor Value Functions; Savebaked Functions; Functions (FO3)
DamageActorValueAlt | JohnnyGuitar | [ActorRefID].DamageActorValueAlt(ActorValueCode:int, Value:<nowiki>int|float</nowiki>) (alias: DamageAVAlt) | DamageActorValueAlt alters the damage modifier of a given actor value using a numeric Actor Value code instead of a stat name. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. DamageActorValueAlt will decrease the modifier by the given amount. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Actor Value Functions; Savebaked Functions
DamageObject | GECK1 | [ObjectRefID].DamageObject(Value:float) (alias: DO) | Applies damage to a destructible object which is not already destroyed. Does not work for objects which have health (such as weapons). It is called on a reference and takes a float which is the amount of damage to apply. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Functions (FO3)
DeactivateAllHighlightsAlt | JohnnyGuitar | DeactivateAllHighlightsAlt() | Deactivates all VATS body part highlights. Unlike DeactivateAllHighlights, this can be used in scripts. DeactivateAnim | kNVSE | actor.DeactivateAnim(anim sequence path:string, fEaseOutTime:float?) | Deactivates the specified animation sequence. This is a low level animation function that must be used on an animation path played with ActivateAnim, BlendToAnim, BlendToAnimFromPose or CrossFadeAnims. Do not use it if you don't know what you're doing. Returns 1 if the sequence was successfully deactivated, and 0 otherwise. * Optional argument 'fEaseOutTime' specifies a time period over which the sequence weight will be ramped down from its set value to 0.0. This is used for easing out the sequence to avoid a pop when other sequences are still running. The default value is the max of the NiControllerSequence text keys 'Blend' and 'BlendOut' divided by 30. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
debugMode | JIP | debugMode(debugMode:int) | Used to check if a specific debug mode is toggled on or off. Previously undocumented. Accepts a value from 0-4, which specifies the debug mode to check: <pre>0&#09;ToggleGodMode / TGM 1&#09;ToggleAI / TAI 2&#09;TCAI 3&#09;ToggleCollision / TCL 4&#09;TB </pre> | Functions_(JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); Debugging; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

Scripts will run on disabled references. Ignores Disable's limitation of being unable to disable references that have an Enable Parent. <u>Ignoring this limit has not been solidly tested, so some issues may pop up</u>. Otherwise, the two functions are identical. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

disableOrEnable | JohnnyGuitar | disableOrEnable(disableOrEnable:0/1) | Toggles the display of Level Up Menu. Whenever a save is loaded, the level-up menu will be re-enabled. For this reason, checking GetGameLoaded in a quest script is recommended to continuously prevent the display of the menu. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); UI Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
DisablePlayerControls | GECK1 | DisablePlayerControls(MovementFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, PipboyFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, FightingFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, POVFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, LookingFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, RolloverTextFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, SneakingFlag (0/1) (optional):?) | 'DisablePlayerControls' disables some or all of the player's controls. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: DisablePlayerControls 1 1 1 1 0 0 0 ; Looking, Rollover Text, and Sneaking are still enabled | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Functions (FO3)
DisablePlayerControlsAlt | NVSE | DisablePlayerControlsAlt(MovementFlag (0/1),:?, PipboyFlag (0/1),:?, FightingFlag (0/1),:?, POVFlag (0/1),:?, LookingFlag (0/1),:?, RolloverTextFlag (0/1),:?, SneakingFlag (0/1):?) | Disables some or all of the player's controls. Unlike DisablePlayerControls, the changes are NOT savebaked, and are cleared whenever a save is loaded. * Consider using GetGameLoaded to re-apply changes, or something similar. Furthermore, disabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. *This makes it easier to keep mods that disable controls compatible with each other. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: DisablePlayerControlsAlt 1 1 1 1 0 0 0 ; Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE)
DisablePlayerControlsAltEx | NVSE | DisablePlayerControlsAltEx(flags:int?) | Disables some or all of the player's controls. Unlike DisablePlayerControls, the changes are NOT savebaked, and are cleared whenever a save is loaded. * Consider using GetGameLoaded to re-apply changes, or something similar. Furthermore, disabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. *This makes it easier to keep mods that disable controls compatible with each other. Unlike DisablePlayerControlsAlt, this function takes a single arg, flags. It is a Bitmask, with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the 'Flag Definitions' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: <pre> DisablePlayerControlsAltEx ((1 << 0) | (1 << 2) | (1 << 3) | (1 << 4)) ; Movement, Pipboy, Fighting and POV controls are disabled </pre> If you are unfamiliar with the binary OR operator, <nowiki>"|"</nowiki>, or the binary bit-shifting operator <nowiki>"<<"</nowiki>, see the NVSE Expressions page. This example also makes use of an Inline Expression. | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE); Bit Functions
DisableTrigger | LU | DisableTrigger(trigger:int) | Prevents the game from registering input from a trigger. (Script functions will still register the input.) | Functions_(JIP); Controller_Functions_(JIP); Input Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
DismemberLimb | JIP | reference.DismemberLimb(bodyPartID:int, explode:1/0?) | Dismembers the calling actor's specified limb. If the second, optional argument is non-zero, the limb will explode; otherwise, it will be severed (default). | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP)
DispatchEvent | NVSE | DispatchEvent(eventID:string, infomap:stringmap?, AlternativeSenderString:string?) | Dispatches a User-Defined Event that any mod can register to with SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt. This allows for easy communication between mods. The listening mod receives a string map array in its handler script. By default it will contain the event name under the "eventName" key, and the dispatching mod name under the "eventSender" key, both as strings. The dispatching mod can choose to override this behavior by * setting an optional infomap parameter to a custom stringmap that listening mod will receive. * setting an optional AlternativeSenderString string that will replace "eventSender" key. | Functions_(NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions; Event Handlers
DispatchEventAlt | NVSE | callingRef.DispatchEventAlt(eventName:string, multi:arg(up to 15)?) -> success:bool | Similar to Call, but executes multiple Event Handler UDFs that were registered for the User-Defined Event of a certain name, assuming the filters for those event handlers match with the dispatched arguments. Can dispatch up to 15 arguments of any type (numeric, form, string, array), as well as passing an optional calling reference to invoke the UDFs with. Users can register to these custom events via SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, though SetEventHandlerAlt is more suited for this task, as it allows filters that dictate when the handler should run for each individual arg. | Functions (NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions; Event Handlers
Dispel | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].Dispel(MagicID:ref) | Removes specified Actor Effect that is affecting an actor. | Functions (GECK); Game Effect Functions; Functions (FO3)
