Optional parameter controls whether the function looks for any matching dlls, or only dlls originating from game folder. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Debugging; File Functions; Functions (ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
DonnerReedKuruParty | JIP | reference.DonnerReedKuruParty(isEaten:0/1) (alias: TheOtherOtherWhiteMeat) | Marks the calling actor's 'corpse' as eaten/not eaten (with the 'Cannibal' perk). *Marking a corpse as <i>eaten</i> will block feeding on it. Conversely, marking it as <i>not eaten</i> will enable feeding on it again. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions
Drop | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].Drop(ObjectID:ref, Count:int) | Calling actor drops the specified item(s) in the world at their feet. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Actor Functions; Functions (FO3)
DropAlt | JIP | reference.DropAlt(item/list:ref, quantity:int?, clearOwnership:0/1?) | The calling actor/container will drop the specified number of the specified item(s) in the world, next to it. This function is an improved version of the vanilla Drop: *This function may be called on <i>any</i> container, and is not restricted to actors alone. *A 'FormList' may be passed as parameter (in place of an item). *All stats/attributes (health, script, mods, ownership, etc.) of dropped items are preserved. *This function will trigger the 'OnDrop' script event (where applicable). | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Object Functions; Actor Functions
DropMe | GECK1 | DropMe() | Drops the calling item from its container. If it is not currently in a container, the function will have no effect. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Actor Functions; Functions (FO3)
DropMeAlt | JIP | reference.DropMeAlt(quantity:int?, clearOwnership:0/1?) | Drops the calling 'Inventory Reference' in the world, next to its container. *All stats/attributes (health, script, mods, ownership, etc.) of dropped items are preserved. *This function will trigger the 'OnDrop' script event (where applicable). | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Object Functions; Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
DumpCommandWikiDoc | NVSE | DumpCommandWikiDoc(commandName:string) (alias: DumpWikiDoc) | Dumps GECK Wiki-style documentation for the specified command to 'nvse.log'. Useful for documenting new functions. Note that for certain plugins, the 'origin' name and 'Category:Functions (?)' category name may be incorrect due to naming scheme inconsistencies. In those cases, create an issue for xNVSE on github so that the correct strings may be hardcoded. | Functions (JIP); Functions (NVSE)
DumpDocs | NVSE | DumpDocs(showQuickList:1/0?, startingOpcode:int?, showIfConditionOnly:1/0?, showIfDeprecated:1/0?) | Dumps out all specified registered script functions in a detailed format, or a simpler format if showQuickList is set to 1. Useful for collecting information on functions, for example for making geck script syntax highlighters. Docs are dumped to 'nvse.log'. By default, startingOpcode is set to 5120 to only include functions from NVSE and NVSE plugins. Set this arg to 0 to get functions from the vanilla game as well. | Functions (NVSE); Debugging
DumpEventHandlers | NVSE | DumpEventHandlers(eventNameFilter:string?, handlerFilter:[[UDF]]?, priorityFilter:int?) | Dumps all event handlers matching the filter args (or dumps all event handlers, if unfiltered). <u>Only includes event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. Event handlers will be listed according to the event they belong to and the priority they belong to. Includes event handlers registered from inside NVSE plugins. | Functions_(NVSE); Event Handlers
DumpFormList | ShowOff | DumpFormList(FormList:baseForm, fileName:string?, bAppend:0/1?) (alias: FListDump) | Dumps the contents of a FormList. If fileName is omitted, the contents are dumped into console. Otherwise, it dumps to the specified file (in the Fallout directory). If the file does not exist, it is created. If it exists and bAppend is omitted / is set to 1, the contents are appended to the file. Otherwise, all previous text is erased. | List Functions; Debugging; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
DumpLevList | LU | DumpLevList(levelledList:baseform) (alias: LListDump) | Similar to ar_Dump, outputs all entries of a Levelled List to the console. | Functions_(JIP); Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP); Leveled List Functions
DuplicateAllItems | GECK1 | <nowiki>[ActorRefID|ContainerRefID]</nowiki>.DuplicateAllItems(TargetContainerRefID:ref) | Duplicates all items into the target container. Any scripted item will be duplicated almost identically: *Any script variables will be set to 0 on the duplicate *The duplicate will have a new FormID, meaning 'if (Original == New)' will never be true **More importantly, meaning if any script referenced the original the duplicate will not work. For instance 'if (player.GetItemCount ArmorLeather)' will never be true if the player has the duplicate. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Object Functions; Functions (FO3)
editorID | JohnnyGuitar | editorID(editorID:string) -> ref | Returns the Form ID associated with a specified Editor ID string (case-sensitive). | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); ID Functions; String Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
effectShader | JohnnyGuitar | effectShader(effectShader:baseform, textureID:int) -> string_var | Returns the specified texture path of an 'Effect Shader'. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions
effectShader | JohnnyGuitar | effectShader(effectShader:baseform, textureID:int, path:string) | Sets the specified texture path of an 'Effect Shader'. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions
effectShader | JohnnyGuitar | effectShader(effectShader:baseform, traitID:int, value:float) -> int | Sets the value of a numeric 'Effect Shader' trait, returning 1 on success. Most changes to effect shaders will not take effect while the shader effect is playing. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions
effectShader | JohnnyGuitar | effectShader(effectShader:baseform, traitID:int) -> float | Returns the value of a numeric 'Effect Shader' trait. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Game Effect Functions
enable | GECK1 | enable(enable:int) | This is one of 4 functions which can be used to increase the game's performance in situations where many actors are in combat or otherwise doing complex pathing. Note that the flag is cleared every frame, so this function needs to be called in a script running on a reference in the loaded area. When the SetNoAvoidance flag is set, actors do not perform avoid box movement. Actors do not open doors or angle away from each other. | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Actor Functions
enable | GECK1 | enable(enable:int) | This is one of 4 functions which can be used to increase the game's performance in situations where many actors are in combat or otherwise doing complex pathing. Note that the flag is cleared every frame, so this function needs to be called in a script running on a reference in the loaded area. When the SetAllVisible flag is set, actors do not perform LineOfSight checks. When actors check to see if they can see something, they assume they can see its head. | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Actor Functions
enable | GECK1 | enable(enable:int) | This function is used to simplify processing during complex scenes involving many actors in combat. As such it needs to be used carefully and only under controlled circumstances, as it significantly changes the behavior of the game while it is set. 'Important note:' this flag is cleared every frame, so in order to maintain the "complex scene" flag you need to set it in a script which is running on something in the loaded area (such as a load door or activator). The important changes the "complex scene" flag make are: * animation and sound details are reduced * target disposition checks are not run as a part of "should attack" checks for actors already in combat * combat does not check movement restrictions (actors may sidestep into objects, off ledges, etc) * 'low and middle-low process levels do not update' -- very important! This means that actors which are not in the loaded area WILL NOT UPDATE while this flag is set -- meaning they will not go through load doors, for example. This is the main reason this command needs to be used with caution. * excess dead bodies in the loaded area are cleaned up more frequently * avoidance is disabled (actors won't open doors or angle away from each other) | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Actor Functions
EnableAlt | ShowOff | reference.EnableAlt(FadeIn:Int?) (alias: SnigOn) | Enables the calling reference if it was previously disabled. Enabled references are rendered, and enabled actors will process their AI. Ignores Enable's limitation of being unable to enable references that have an Enable Parent. <u>Ignoring this limit has not been solidly tested, so some issues may pop up</u>. Otherwise, the two functions are identical. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
EnableButton | LU | EnableButton(Button:int) -> none | Re-enables button input disabled via DisableButton. Multiple buttons can be specified at once. | Functions_(JIP); Controller Functions (JIP); Input Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
EnableControl | FOSE1 | EnableControl(controlCode:int) (alias: ec) | Enables the key and/or mouse button bound to a control. *With the [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN NVSE Plugin] installed, this function will also work when using a controller. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Input Functions; Functions (FO3)

EnablePlayerControls | GECK1 | EnablePlayerControls(MovementFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, PipboyFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, FightingFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, POVFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, LookingFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, RolloverTextFlag (0/1) (optional),:?, SneakingFlag (0/1) (optional):?) | 'EnablePlayerControls' enables the player's controls after use of DisablePlayerControls. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to: EnablePlayerControls 1 1 1 1 1 1 1 | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Functions (FO3)
EnablePlayerControlsAlt | NVSE | EnablePlayerControlsAlt(MovementFlag (0/1),:?, PipboyFlag (0/1),:?, FightingFlag (0/1),:?, POVFlag (0/1),:?, LookingFlag (0/1),:?, RolloverTextFlag (0/1),:?, SneakingFlag (0/1):?) | Enables the player's controls after use of DisablePlayerControlsAlt / DisablePlayerControlsAltEx. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. This will NOT override the controls disabled with the vanilla DisablePlayerControls. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having *all* the Flag arguments at 1, meaning it will re-enable all controls. | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE)
EnablePlayerControlsAltEx | NVSE | EnablePlayerControlsAltEx(flags:int?) | Enables the player's controls after use of DisablePlayerControlsAlt / DisablePlayerControlsAltEx. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. This will NOT override the controls disabled with the vanilla DisablePlayerControls. Unlike EnablePlayerControlsAlt, this function takes a single arg, flags. It is a Bitmask, with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the 'Flag Definitions' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having *all* the flag bits at 1, meaning it will re-enable all controls. | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE)

Exists | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].Exists([TARGET]:) -> int | In Combat Dialogue, the target of the conversation is a group member nearby the speaking actor. If no group members are nearby, the target of the conversation is the speaker himself. Because of this, there is a new condition function called the Exists condition function. Exists returns 1 if the Self and Target references for the conditions are different, and 0 if not. This allows the dialogue system to tell whether there is a distinct second person to carry on the conversation. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3); Condition Functions

The result is the same as if one had divided the player's raw fame or infamy value by the given reputation's maximum value, or 'scale'. The second arg determines if infamy (0) of fame (1) is checked. | Functions (Vegas); Reputation Functions; Condition Functions

fatan | JIP | fatan(value:float) -> float | Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the 'arc tangent' (expressed in degrees) of the specified value. *This function is 'significantly faster' than dATan. *'Note:' Due to the sign ambiguity, this function cannot determine with certainty in which quadrant the angle falls only by its tangent value. See fatan2 for an alternative that takes a fractional argument instead. | Math Functions; Functions (JIP); Math Functions (JIP)
fatan2 | JIP | fatan2(y:float, x:float) -> float | Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the principal value of the 'arc tangent' (expressed in degrees) of the specified 'y/x'. *This function is 'significantly faster' than dATan2. *To compute the value, the function takes into account the sign of both arguments in order to determine the quadrant. | Math Functions; Functions (JIP); Math Functions (JIP)

FileExists | JIP | FileExists(filePath:string, isFolder:1/0?) -> exists:0/1/2 | Returns whether or not a file (or a folder) with the specified path (relative to the 'Data' folder) exists. *If the file exists as a 'loose' file, the function returns '1'; If the file is 'packed' in a BSA archive, the function returns '2'. *If the second argument '<i>isFolder</i>' is non-zero, the function checks for a folder, otherwise for a file. *A 'string_var' may be passed, prefixed with the '$' operator. *'Does not work for directories packed inside a BSA archive and will always return 0 when attempting to check for those.' | Functions (JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); File Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

flags | ShowOff | sourceContainer.flags(flags:int, typeCode:int, targetContainer:reference, exceptionFormlist:baseForm) -> 0/1 | An extended version of RemoveAllTypedItems. Removes all items from the calling reference's inventory, with custom exceptions. Returns 0 if some error has recurred, like attempting to call this function without a valid sourceContainer, 1 otherwise. ==Arguments== The first argument, flags, is a bitmask composed with all the boolean flags that can be toggled for this function. All flag values and their significance are listed below, in the Flags section. If typeCode is omitted or set to -1, there is no Form Type ID restriction on what forms can be removed. If set to 0, will do nothing. Otherwise, this determines the Form Type ID of items that should be removed. If targetContainer is specified, the items being removed transferred to that container with all equipment items retaining their current health; otherwise the items are destroyed as they would be via RemoveItem. If exceptionFormlist is specified, any form in that FormList will not be removed. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Inventory Functions; Actor Functions; Bit Functions
flags | JohnnyGuitar | flags(flags:bitmask) (alias: SetDPCHUDFlags) | Sets HUD visibility flags bitmask that will be used when player controls are disabled. HUD Visibility flags are as follows:<pre> AP Meter = 1 (bit 0), HP Meter = 2 (bit 1), Radiation Meter = 4 (bit 2), Enemy Health = 8 (bit 3), Quest Reminder = 16 (bit 4), Region Location = 32 (bit 5), Reticle = 64 (bit 6), Sneak Meter = 128 (bit 7), Messages = 256 (bit 8), Info = 512 (bit 9), Subtitles = 1024 (bit 10), Hotkeys = 2048 (bit 11), XP Meter = 4096 (bit 12), Breath Meter = 8192 (bit 13), Grenade Indicator = 16384 (bit 14), Crippled Limb Indicator = 32768 (bit 15), Hardcore Mode Meters = 65536 (bit 16)</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Bit Functions; UI Functions; Flag Functions
Floor | FOSE1 | Floor(float:float) -> float (alias: flr) | Returns the nearest whole number that is less than a float | Math Functions; Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Functions (FO3)
fmod | FOSE1 | fmod(x:float, n:float, offset:float) -> float | Returns the result of a floating point operated on by a floating point modulous. (As opposed to the arithmetic operator '%' modulo, for integers.) | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Math Functions; Functions (FO3)
ForceActiveQuest | GECK1 | ForceActiveQuest(QuestID:ref) | ForceActiveQuest makes a specified quest the active quest. | Quest Functions; Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3)
ForceActorDetectionValue | JIP | reference.ForceActorDetectionValue(detectionValue:int?, asModifier:1/0?) | 'A)' Forces and locks the calculated 'detection value' (a value that indicates how 'visible' an actor is to another actor) of the calling actor to <u>any</u> other actor, regardless of skill, cover, lighting, perception, etc. A very low, negative value (such as -100) will render the calling actor completely undetectable by other actors, while a high positive value will render it fully detectable by any actor. 'B)' If the 2nd, optional argument is non-zero, the value of the 1st argument will instead be used as a modifier, and will be added/subtracted from the 'detection value' every time it is calculated. Calling the function and ommiting the argument, or passing 0, will restore detection mechanics on the calling actor to normal. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Detection Functions; Detection
ForceActorValue | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].ForceActorValue([[ActorValue]]:statName, Value:int) (alias: ForceAV) | ForceActorValue modifies the current stat to the specified value (not to be confused with SetActorValue, which changes the BASE value of the stat). | Functions (GECK); Actor Value Functions; Functions (FO3)
ForceActorValueAlt | JohnnyGuitar | [ActorRefID].ForceActorValueAlt(ActorValueCode:int, Value:int) (alias: ForceAVAlt) | ForceActorValueAlt modifies the current stat to the specified value (not to be confused with SetActorValue, which changes the BASE value of the stat), using a numeric Actor Value code instead of a stat name. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Actor Value Functions
ForceClimate | JIP | ForceClimate(climate:form?) | Forces the game to use the specified 'Climate' type, blocking transitions to other climate types. *Calling either this function with the <i>climate</i> argument omitted, or calling SetCurrentClimate, will unlock and restore normal climate transitions. | Functions (JIP); Climate Functions (JIP); Functions (ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
ForceFireWeapon | kNVSE | actor.ForceFireWeapon() | Fires the currently equipped weapon of the specified actor. | Functions (kNVSE); Actor Functions; Animation Functions; Weapon Functions
ForceFlee | GECK1 | [ActorRefID].ForceFlee(CellID:ref (Optional)?, ObjectRefID:ref (Optional)?) | Causes the actor to analyze its surroundings for potential threats, and flee if a threat is found. | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Functions (FO3)
ForceHitStaggerReaction | ShowOff | actorReference.ForceHitStaggerReaction(checkForIgnoreCrippledLimbsAV:0/1?) -> 0/1 (alias: ForceHitReaction) | Forces the calling actor reference to play the hit stagger reaction animation for the limb they just got hit on. Only works inside OnHit-style event handlers, and only if GetHitLocation is not -1. It will only work for limb stagger idle anims that check for GetForceHitReaction, which vanilla has only set up for third person. A mod to set them up for first person would be recommended (recommendation pending). If checkForIgnoreCrippledLimbsAV is set to 1, will not play the hit stagger reaction if the actor has the 'Ignore Crippled Limbs' Actor Value set to > 0. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Animation Functions; Actor Functions
ForcePlayIdle | kNVSE | reference.ForcePlayIdle(idleAnim:ref?, playerPOV (optional):1/2?) | Plays an idle animation on an actor immediately. This function works consistently in all script types (and not just effect scripts) and in the in-game console, unlike PlayIdle and PlayIdleEx. An optional player point of view argument can be passed where 1 represents 3rd person and 2 represents 1st person. | Functions (kNVSE); Actor_Functions; Animation Functions
ForceRadioStationUpdate | GECK1 | ForceRadioStationUpdate() (alias: FRSU) | Forces radio update. | Functions (GECK); Radio Functions; Functions (FO3)
ForceRecoilAnim | ShowOff | actorReference.ForceRecoilAnim() -> 0/1 | Forces the calling actor reference to play the weapon recoil animation, which typically only works for melee/unarmed weapons. Does nothing if stagger animation is playing. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Animation Functions; Actor Functions
ForceSave | GECK1 | ForceSave() | Creates a new save in a new slot, as if the player manually did so. | Functions (GECK); Miscellaneous Functions; Functions (FO3)
ForceStopIdle | kNVSE | reference.ForceStopIdle(playerPOV (optional):1/2?) | Forces the currently playing idle animation on the calling reference to stop. This function works consistently in all script types (and not just effect scripts) and in the in-game console, unlike StopIdle. An optional player point of view argument can be passed where 1 represents 3rd person and 2 represents 1st person. | Functions (kNVSE); Actor_Functions; Animation Functions
ForceTerminalBack | GECK1 | ForceTerminalBack() | When called from the result script of a terminal menu item, this script command will force the terminal back one level. If called from the top-level terminal, this will close the terminal and return to normal gameplay. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3)
ForceWeaponJamAnim | ShowOff | actorRefr.ForceWeaponJamAnim() -> bool (alias: ForceJamAnim) | Attempts to force the calling actor to play the jam animation for their drawn-out Weapon. Returns true (1) for success, false (0) for failure. | Actor Functions; Weapon Functions; Animation Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
ForceWeather | GECK1 | ForceWeather(WeatherID:ref, WeatherOverrideFlag:bool?) (alias: FW) | Forces the weather to immediately switch to the specified weather, skipping the normal transition period set by the Trans Delta. If OverrideWeatherFlag is 1, the weather will not change again until ReleaseWeatherOverride is called. Otherwise, the weather will change back in the normal fashion according to the weather system. | Functions (GECK); Weather Functions; Functions (FO3)

fOriginX | JIP | fOriginX(fOriginX:float, fOriginY:float, fOriginZ:float, fTargetX:float, fTargetY:float, fTargetZ:float, iColliderFlag:int) -> float | Returns the distance between Collision and Target X Y Z coordinates. ==Warning== The calculation is not the fastest thing on the planet, and spamming a bunch of casts every frame might be not the healthiest thing for Fallout New Vegas performance | Functions (JIP); Movement Functions

FreezeAmmoRegen | ShowOff | FreezeAmmoRegen(toggle:1/0?) -> wasAmmoRegenFrozen:0/1 | Toggles if ammo regeneration should be frozen, preventing ammo from being regenerated. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Time Functions; Weapon Functions
FreezeTime | JIP | FreezeTime(toggle:1/0?) -> timeFrozen:0/1 | Toggles a special game mode, where: *Game-time ceases to advance. *'All' animations and physics are suspended, <u>except</u> for the player character. The following effects were observed while this mode is enabled: *Game-time <i>does</i> advance when sleeping/waiting, or when fast-travelling. *Actor Effects/ingestibles with a duration are suspended. *Hardcore needs are not incremented. *Quest-/Object-type scripts continue to run as normal; Effect-type script, however, are suspended. *Weapons with an ammo regen rate will still have their ammo regenerate. See FreezeAmmoRegen to counter this. *Neither lighting settings not IMODs are updated when traversing between cells. 'Additional effects are likely, pending further testing.' | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Functions_(Command Extender); Time Functions; Functions (FO3)

fsin | LU | fsin(angle:float) -> float | Returns an approximation (accurate to at least 6 decimal digits) of the 'sine' of the specified angle (expressed in degrees). *This function is 'significantly faster' than either sin or dSin. | Math Functions; Functions (JIP); Math Functions (JIP)
fsqrt | LU | fsqrt(inputValue:float) -> outputValue:float | Very fast floating-point square-root calculation. | Math_Functions; Functions_(JIP); Math_Functions_(JIP)

GetActionRef | GECK1 | Activator.GetActionRef() -> ref (alias: GetAR) | Returns the reference currently interacting with the scripted object. It will only return a value during the frame after the object has been interacted with. This means it is generally only useful inside an OnActivate, OnTriggerEnter, OnTriggerLeave or OnTrigger block. If more than one object is interacting with the object (for instance, several objects colliding with the same trigger zone), only the most recently interacting object will be returned by this function. | Functions (GECK); Reference Variable Functions; Functions (FO3)

GetActorProcessingLevel | JIP | reference.GetActorProcessingLevel() -> processingLvl:int | Returns the current AI/script processing-level of the calling actor. The returned value ranges from 0 to 3: *'0 (High):' The actor is processed every frame. *'1 (Mid-High):' The actor is processed every ~18 seconds. *'2 (Mid-Low):' Unknown at this time. *'3 (Low):' The actor is processed every ~120 seconds. If the actor's Base Process isn't loaded, which can occur if it is disabled, returns -1. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions

GetAllItemRefs | JIP | reference.GetAllItemRefs(typeCode:int?, noNonPlayable:0/1?, noQuestItems:0/1?, noEquipped:0/1?) -> items:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing 'Inventory References' of all the items in the calling reference's inventory. Four optional parameters may be passed for filtering the result: 'typeCode' - Only include inventory references of items of this type (type-codes). Passing 0 will include items of ANY type. 'noNonPlayable' - <u>Do not</u> include items that are non-playable (only applicable to clothing, weapons and ammo). 'noQuestItems' - <u>Do not</u> include quest items. 'noEquipped' - <u>Do not</u> include inventory references that are currently equipped by the calling reference. | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Array Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
GetAllItems | JIP | reference.GetAllItems(typeCode:int?, noNonPlayable:0/1?, noQuestItems:0/1?, noEquipped:0/1?) -> items:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing the Base Forms of all the items in the calling reference's inventory. Four optional parameters may be passed for filtering the result: 'typeCode' - Only include items of this type (a full list of type-codes can be found here). Passing 0 will include items of ANY type. 'noNonPlayable' - <u>Do not</u> include items that are non-playable (only applicable to clothing, weapons and ammo). 'noQuestItems' - <u>Do not</u> include quest items. 'noEquipped' - <u>Do not</u> include items that are currently equipped by the calling reference. | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Array Functions

This is a copy of the local data, so modifying the array contents will not modify the stored data. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions
GetAllPerks | JIP | actorRef.GetAllPerks(forTeammates:0/1?, includeHidden:0/1?) -> allPerks:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing all 'perks' the calling 'Actor' reference has. *When called on 'the player', depending on the function's 1st argument, '<i>forTeammates</i>', the result array will contain either the player's own perks, or the player's perks for active teammates. *When called on any other actor, the result depends on whether the 'uNPCPerks' optional JIP LN feature is enabled or not. **If enabled, the array will contain all the perks owned by the actor (both startup and script-added perks). If a teammate and the argument is non-zero, the player's perks for teammates will also be included. **If disabled, the function will return a non-empty array if and only if the actor is a teammate <u>and</u> the argument is non-zero. *If the 2nd argument, '<i>includeHidden</i>', is non-zero, all applicable hidden perks will be included in the result, as well. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Perk Functions; Array Functions; Perk Functions (JIP)

GetAngleEx | JIP | reference.GetAngleEx(resAngleX:ScriptVar, resAngleY:ScriptVar, resAngleZ:ScriptVar, getLocal:0/1?) | Outputs the XYZ rotation angles ('in degrees') of the calling object reference into the 3 specified 'script variables'. *By default, the function outputs the object's 'extrinsic' (global) rotation, unless the 4th, optional argument, '<i>getLocal</i>', is non-zero, on which case it outputs the object's 'intrinsic' (local) rotation. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
GetAnglesBetweenPoints | JIP | GetAnglesBetweenPoints(x0:float, y0:float, z0:float, x1:float, y1:float, z1:float, resAngX:ScriptVar, resAngZ:ScriptVar) | Calculates the X (pitch) and Z (yaw) angles between two given 3D points in space, and outputs the result angles into the 2 specified 'Script Variables'. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)
GetAngularVelocity | JIP | reference.GetAngularVelocity(nodeName:string, axis:X/Y/Z) -> velocity:float (alias: GetAngVel) | Returns the angular velocity, on the specified 'Axis', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '2&pi; rad/s' (essentially, 'revolutions/second'). | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
GetAngularVelocityAlt | JIP | reference.GetAngularVelocityAlt(nodeName:string, resX:ScriptVar, resY:ScriptVar, resZ:ScriptVar, getGlobal:0/1?) -> success:0/1 | Returns the angular velocity of each of the 3 X/Y/Z axes, of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified 'Script Variables'. *By default, the function returns the velocities relative to the object's 'local axes', unless the 'optional' 5th argument, '<i>getGlobal</i>', is non-zero, on which case the velocities are relative to the world, 'global axes'. *The velocity is in units of '2&pi; rad/s' (essentially, 'revolutions/second'). *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions

GetAnimationAttributes | kNVSE | reference.GetAnimationAttributes(anim sequence path:string) (alias: GetAnimAttrs) | Retrieves attributes and information for the specified animation sequence as a String Map. When called on an actor, retrieves additional data such as the state, current offset, calculated time relative to the actor if it has been played on him. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
GetAnimationsByAnimGroup | kNVSE | reference.GetAnimationsByAnimGroup(anim group id:animation group) | Returns the animation paths, including variants, that would be played if the specified animation group is played on the actor as an Array. The array is sorted where animations with higher priority are at the start of the array, but bear in mind kNVSE condition scripts can skip animations with higher priority, instead playing animations with a lower priority. This function also takes into account any kNVSE overridden animations. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
GetAnimCurrentTime | kNVSE | reference.GetAnimCurrentTime(sAnimPath:string) | This function retrieves the current time of a specified animation. It is functionally equivalent to accessing the calculatedTime field found in the GetAnimationAttributes function. Alternatively, it can be considered as the sum of the offset field in the same function and the value obtained from GetGlobalAnimTime. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions

GetArmourPenetrated | JIP | reference.GetArmourPenetrated() -> hitPenetrated:1/0 | When called from a 'OnHit'/'OnHitWith' script block (or the equivalent NVSE event handlers), or from a 'ScriptEffectStart' block in an 'Ammo' form 'Impact Script', returns whether the total damage delivered by the current hit had penetrated the actor's 'DT', or was fully-absorbed by it. Note that the maximum damage reduction, after deducting DT/DR is, by default, 80% of the total damage, which effectively means that even if a hit was fully-absorbed, a certain fraction of the damage will always pass through. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions

If the <i>getSoldItems</i> parameter is non-zero, the returned array will contain the items set to be <i>sold</i> by the player. GetBaseEquippedWeight | ShowOff | actorRefr.GetBaseEquippedWeight(minWeight:float?, flagsMask:int?) -> float | Returns the total amount of base weight for the items that the calling Actor reference has equipped. This ignores weight modification effects, such as the "Modify Light Items" Perk Entry Point. minWeight is the minimum weight an equipped item must have in order to be included in the total. If omitted, it defaults to 0. flagsMask is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not count duplicates. | Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Condition Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

GetCalculatedAPCost | ShowOff | GetCalculatedAPCost(whatAttack:int, weaponOverride:baseForm?) -> int (alias: GetCalculatedWeaponAttackAPCost) | Returns the Action Point cost for the player doing the specified VATS attack with their currently equipped weapon (or their fists, if no weapon is equipped). Here are the possible values for whatAttack, which determines what VATS attack to query: <pre> DefaultAttack = 0, SpecialAttack1 = 1, (does not work correctly outside of VATS) SpecialAttack2 = 2 (does not work correctly outside of VATS) </pre> If weaponOverride is set to a valid Weapon baseform, will instead use that weapon for the calculation. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Actor Value Functions; VATS Functions
GetCalculatedEquippedWeight | ShowOff | actorRefr.GetCalculatedEquippedWeight(minWeight:float?, flagsMask:int?) -> float | Returns the total amount of Weight for the items that the calling Actor reference has equipped. This accounts for weight modification effects, including: *"Modify Light Items" Perk Entry Point *"Decrease Weight" Weapon Mod effect - note that it does not properly account for it if more than 1 weap. mod with this effect is equipped. *"Adjust Heavy Weapon Weight" Perk Entry Point minWeight is the minimum weight an equipped item must have in order to be included in the total. If omitted, it defaults to 0. flagsMask is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not count duplicates. | Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Condition Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
GetCalculatedItemValue | ShowOff | itemRefr.GetCalculatedItemValue(accountForBarterChanges:bool?) -> float | Returns the item's reference's value modified by condition and Weapon Mods. Returns -1 in case of an error. If accountForBarterChanges is true AND a valid Inventory Reference is passed, will also account for Barter menu sell/buy multipliers. It should return the same value as what appears on screen. *May not work reliably if the function is being used as a Condition; it may be evaluated before the 'Calculate Buy Price' Perk Entry Point effects are applied. | Inventory Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

GetCalculatedWeaponDamage | JIP | reference.GetCalculatedWeaponDamage(weaponForm:ref?) -> weaponDamage:float | Returns the total, calculated attack damage of a 'Weapon', adjusted by any bonuses/penalties from stats, perks, etc. *If called on either an Inventory Reference, or a weapon reference placed in the world, the damage will further be adjusted by the weapon's condition, and by any weapon mods attached to it. *In the case of an 'inventory reference', if the weapon is in the inventory of an 'actor', the damage will be adjusted by the actor's stats/perks. In all other cases, the player's stats/perks will be used. *If the weapon is equipped and uses ammo, the damage will be adjusted by any effects of the equipped ammo type. | Functions (JIP); Weapon Functions (JIP); Weapon Functions

GetChildBlocks | JIP | reference.GetChildBlocks(parentNode:string?, pcModel:1/2?) -> blockNames:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing the names (string_vars) of all the blocks that are descended from the specified parent node, in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 1st argument, '<i>parentNode</i>', is <u>optional</u>. If omitted, or an empty string, the object's root node will be used. *The 2nd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Array Functions; Functions (JIP PP LN)

The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the Actor Value for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing healthThreshold. | Condition Functions; Actor Functions; Actor Value Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

GetDetectionValue | JIP | reference.GetDetectionValue(targetActor:ref) -> detectionValue:int | Returns a value indicating how visible the target actor is to the calling actor. A value lower than zero indicates the calling actor does not detect the target actor, whilst a value higher than zero indicates the target actor is detected by the calling actor. The higher the absolute value, the more the target actor is visible to/concealed from the calling actor. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions; Detection Functions

Unlike GetDistance, this does not cause crashes if the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace. | Math_Functions; Functions_(JIP); Math_Functions_(JIP); Condition_Functions; Movement Functions
GetDistance3D | LU | [Actor, Activator].GetDistance3D(ref:ref) -> float | Faster version of GetDistance. Can also be used as a condition. Unlike GetDistance, this does not cause crashes if the reference(s) are not in the current cell/worldspace. | Math_Functions; Functions_(JIP); Math_Functions_(JIP); Condition_Functions; Movement Functions

Note that, while these weapons <i>do</i> appear in the calling actor's inventory, they are <u>not</u> visible to inventory functions (GetItemCount, RemoveItem, etc.). | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions; Array Functions

The function outputs the X/Y/Z position and rotation values into the 6 'Script Variable' arguments, whereas the cell is the return value of the function. *If the calling reference was not placed in the editor, the function returns 0. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions

If the actor's CarryWeight Actor Value is somehow below 1, this function considers their max carry weight as 1 instead. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Value Functions; Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Condition Functions

GetEquippedItemRefForItem | ShowOff | actorReference.GetEquippedItemRefForItem(itemOrFormlist:BaseForm) -> itemRef:ref | If the calling actor has the specified item BaseForm currently equipped, then it returns that equipped item as an 'Inventory Reference'. Otherwise, returns 0. ===FormList Support (New in 1.51)=== If a FormList of items is passed instead, and at least one of those items is equipped, will return the first item in the list which is equipped as an 'Inventory Reference'. Otherwise, returns 0. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Inventory Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
GetEquippedItemRefs | ShowOff | ActorRefr.GetEquippedItemRefs(filterFlags:int?) -> array | Returns an array containing the calling Actor reference's equipped items (as Inventory References). When called without specifying any arguments, will return all equipped Playable Items. Otherwise, filters and effects can be used to determine what items will be added to the array. ==Parameters== 'flagsMask': A bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not return duplicates. | Array Functions; Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Inventory Reference Functions
GetEquippedItems | ShowOff | ActorRefr.GetEquippedItems(filterFlags:int?) -> array | Returns an array containing the calling Actor reference's equipped items (as BaseForms). When called without specifying any arguments, will return all equipped Playable Items. Otherwise, filters and effects can be used to determine what items will be added to the array. ==Parameters== 'flagsMask': A bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not return duplicates. | Array Functions; Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

Note that certain items can occupy multiple equip slots. Non-playable items are checked. If called from console, will display the return value in hexadecimal format. | Condition Functions; Actor Functions; Armor Functions; Weapon Functions; Inventory Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

If no weapon is equipped, defaults to 0. GetEventHandlers | NVSE | GetEventHandlers(eventNameFilter:string?, handlerFilter:[[UDF]]?, priorityFilter:int?) -> array | Returns an array containing all event handlers matching the filter args (or all event handlers, if unfiltered). <u>Only includes event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. If 'not' filtering by event name, will return a StringMap, where the keys are the event names and the values are arrays of handlers (potentially sorted by priority). <br>Otherwise, filtering by event name will just return the array of handlers for that event. 'An array of handlers sorted by priority' is a Map Array, where the keys are priority values and the values are arrays of handlers for that priority. <br>If a priority filter is used, then this array is skipped, and the array of handlers for the priority is used directly instead. An 'array of handlers' is a regular array containing an array for each handler. A 'handler array' contains: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a StringMap containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. <u>These strings may be empty.</u> *Index [1]: A StringMap of all the filters used by the handler. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; Event Handlers
GetExcludedCombatActions | JIP | actorRef.GetExcludedCombatActions() -> excludeMask:int | Returns a 'bitmask' specifying combat-AI action types the calling actor is excluded from performing in combat (see 'SetExcludedCombatActions'). | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions

GetFilesInFolder | LU | GetFilesInFolder(folderpath:string, filter:string?) -> array (alias: GetFiles) | Returns an array of filenames (string_vars) in a subdirectory relative to the ".\Data" folder. GetFirstRef | FOSE1 | GetFirstRef(FormTypeID:int, CellDepth:int, IncludeTakenRefs:bool) -> reference | Returns the first reference in the player's current cell. A type can optionally be supplied by its ID code to return only references matching that type. For example, you can pass 200 for actors and 201 for inventory items. Returns nothing (zero) if there is no ref matching the type in the cell. This function will normally be used only prior to a loop including GetNextRef to scan actors in a cell. An optional cell depth can be supplied to specify the number of adjacent cells to scan in exteriors: a cell depth of 1 scans the player's current cell plus 8 adjacent cells, a depth of 2 scans the player's cell plus 25 adjacent cells. By default, inactive references to items which were previously picked up by an actor are ignored; passing 1 for the third parameter will force those references to be included. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Functions (FO3)
GetFirstRefForItem | NVSE | container.GetFirstRefForItem(Type:object) -> ref | Returns the first entry in an array of inventory references to all objects of the specified type in the calling container. | Functions_(NVSE); Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions

Note that if the specified cell is not loaded in memory, this function will only return persistent references. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Cell Functions; Functions (FO3)

The type of gamesetting to include is filtered by gmstType. gmstType is a Bitmask with the following flags: <pre> 1 = Float/Int-value settings (starts with "i"/"f") 2 = String-value settings (starts with "s") </pre> It is set to 3 if not specified (all gamesettings included). | Functions_(JIP); GameSetting Functions; Bit Functions

GetHasContact | JIP | reference.GetHasContact(objectRefOrList:ref) -> hasContact:0/1 | Returns whether the calling reference has 'physical contact' (i.e. is "touching") with the collision geometry of the specified object/actor reference (or, if a 'Form List' is passed, with any reference in the list). | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Condition_Functions

It is not called on a Reference and it takes one parameter, the note to test for. If the calling reference is not an actor, will just return 0. | Functions (GECK); Condition Functions; Movement Functions; Movement Functions; Functions (FO3)

GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers | NVSE | GetHigherPriorityEventHandlers(eventName:string, handler:[[UDF]], startPriority:int, scriptsToIgnore:scriptOrFormlist?, pluginsToIgnore:array?, pluginHandlersToIgnore:array?) -> array | Returns a Map Array with all the event handlers that have a 'higher or equal' priority than the specified handler at startPriority. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers. Each key in the map is a priority, and each value is an array representing a handler. A 'handler array' is a regular array containing: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a StringMap containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. <u>These strings may be empty.</u> *Index [1]: A StringMap of all the filters used by the handler. If the handler could not be found at startPriority, returns Ar_Null. <u>Only includes event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; Event Handlers
GetHit3DData | JIP | reference.GetHit3DData(dataType:int) -> hit3DData:array_var | When called from a 'OnHit'/'OnHitWith' script block (or the corresponding NVSE event handlers), or from a 'ScriptEffectStart' block in an 'Ammo' form 'Impact Script', will return an 'array_var' containing the exact world X/Y/Z coords and/or angles where the target was hit. The second argument <i>'dataType'</i> determines which values are returned in the array: <pre>0&#09;X/Y/Z coords (elements 0-2) 1&#09;X/Y/Z angles (elements 0-2) 2&#09;X/Y/Z coords (elements 0-2) AND angles (elements 3-5)</pre> | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Movement Functions; Array Functions

To determine which limb was hit, use GetHitLocation. GetHitLocationLingering | ShowOff | reference.GetHitLocationLingering() -> int | Unlike the GetHitLocation condition function, the hit location returned by this condition function is allowed to linger after the initial hit. Useful as a condition for the 'Calculate Weapon Damage' Perk Entry Point, since otherwise a regular GetHitLocation condition doesn't linger long enough to alter the damage. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Actor Functions

GetINIFloat | LU | GetINIFloat(keystring:string, filename:string?) -> float | Returns the value of an INI key found in a file (relative to <u>"Data\Config"</u>) as a float. | Functions_(JIP); INI_Functions_(JIP); File Functions; INI Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached | ShowOff | GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached(sectionAndKey:string, iniPath:string?, createValue:float?, comment:string?) -> float (alias: GetINIFltOrCreateC) | Returns the float value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, createValue is returned and is written in the INI file cache. 'Reads/writes the INI file data from a memory cache.' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *To save changes to the actual file, use SaveCachedIniFile. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached | ShowOff | GetINIFloatOrCreate_Cached(sectionAndKey:string, iniPath:string?, createValue:string?, comment:string?) -> string (alias: GetINIStrOrCreateC) | Returns the string value of an INI key. If it does not exist or could not be found, createValue is returned and is written in the INI file cache. 'Reads/writes the INI file data from a memory cache.' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *To save changes to the actual file, use SaveCachedIniFile. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

If it does not exist or could not be found, defaultValue is returned. 'Reads the INI file data from a memory cache.' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions
GetINISection | LU | GetINISection(section:string, filename:string?, getNumeric:1/0?) -> array | Returns a 'StringMap' of all keys in the specified INI section, and their values, with 64k buffer. *INI files are read from and written to in the ..\Data\Config\ folder (as in MCM's INI functions). [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/INI_file#Example Format Example] *The 2nd argument 'filename' is optional. If omitted, the name of the mod the function is called from is used. *The 3rd argument 'getNumeric' is optional. If non-zero, the 'values' of the keys in the section will be returned as floats (rather than as strings). | Functions_(JIP); INI_Functions_(JIP); File Functions; INI Functions; Array Functions

GetIsLAA | JIP | GetIsLAA() -> isLAA:0/1/2 | *Checks whether the game's executable has the LAA (Large Address Aware) flag enabled, which indicates whether or not the game is patched to be able to use up to 4GB of memory. *Performs an allocation test to determine whether the game's process can actually handle large addresses. <u>Returns</u>: <pre>0&#09;Executable is NOT LAA enabled 1&#09;Executable is LAA enabled; allocation test FAILED 2&#09;Both tests successful</pre> | Functions (JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); File Functions; Debugging; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
GetIsLockBroken | GECK1 | reference.GetIsLockBroken() -> bool | Returns 1 if the lock on the calling reference has been broken, 0 otherwise. If the player has the "Ignore Broken Locks" Perk Entry Point set to a non-zero value and they have broken the lock for the first time, they still have a second chance to lockpick, hence GetIsLockBroken will return 0. | Functions (GECK); Condition Functions; Lock Functions; Functions (FO3)

For example, races such as Ghouls, and specifically made "Raider races" will return a 0 value as not being playable, while the "normal races" will return a 1. Does not take a base form parameter; it only checks if the reference npc's race is playable. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3); Condition Functions

If ignoreGodMode is set to true (non-0), will ignore the player's GodMode status to determine if they are overencumbered. By default, if they are in GodMode, they cannot be considered overencumbered. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Player Functions; Inventory Functions; Condition Functions

GetItemHotkeyIconPath | ShowOff | GetItemHotkeyIconPath(item:baseForm, bOnlyReturnOverride:int?) -> string | Returns the item's hotkey override texture path set by SetItemHotkeyIconPath, if present. If not, will return the regular path (i.e what GetTexturePath would return). If bOnlyReturnOverride is 1, will return the override texture path set by SetItemHotkeyIconPath, or an empty string if no override was made. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Inventory Functions; Texture Functions; File Functions; String Functions
GetItemRefCurrentHealth | JIP | reference.GetItemRefCurrentHealth(percOrBase:0/1/2?) -> health:float | Returns the health of the calling item. Unlike GetEquippedCurrentHealth, this function can also called on an <i>inventory reference</i>. The calling item reference does not have to be equipped. The function can return the health value in one of three ways, depending on the optional argument '<i>percOrBase</i>': <pre>0&#09;Current Health (default) 1&#09;Current Health Percent (0.0 to 1.0) 2&#09;Base Health (incl. bonuses from weapon mods)</pre> | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions

The velocity is in units of '0.1 Meter/sec', or 7 'game Units/sec'. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Movement Functions; Model Functions
GetLinearVelocityAlt | JIP | reference.GetLinearVelocityAlt(nodeName:string, resX:ScriptVar, resY:ScriptVar, resZ:ScriptVar, getLocal:0/1?) -> success:0/1 | Returns the linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z 'global axes', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified 'Script Variables'. *The velocity is in units of '0.1 Meter/sec', or '7 game units/sec'. *If the 'optional' 5th argument, '<i>getLocal</i>', is non-zero, the result velocities are relative to the object's 'local axes'. *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions

GetLinkedRef | GECK1 | reference.GetLinkedRef() -> reference | 'GetLinkedRef ' gives the reference that is currently linked to the current reference. For example, if a switch opens a door, you can link the switch to the door and if the switch is activated you get the door reference and activate it. It is called on a reference and takes no parameters. | Functions (GECK); Condition Functions; Reference Variable Functions; Functions (FO3)

GetLoadedType | LU | GetLoadedType(outlist:formList, typeID:int, index:int?, noClear:bool?) (alias: GLT) | Empties a form list and then fills it with every form of the given Form Type ID from all loaded plugins. 'Optional Index parameter': Passing a value for the 'index' parameter loads only forms from the plugin at that mod index (default: -1 for all plugins). 'Optional NoClear parameter': Passing a 1 for the 'noclear' parameter prevents the function from emptying the list before adding forms to it. | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); File Functions; List Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

GetLocked | GECK1 | ref.GetLocked() -> int | The 'GetLocked' script function gets the access state of terminals and locks (Doors or Containers). Note that in vanilla Fallout 3 and New Vegas, hacking a terminal does not change the access state as far as GetLocked is concerned, and unless unlocked via script, GetLocked will return 1 for any terminal that is or was previously locked (fixed by JIP LN version 54.80 in New Vegas and in GetLockedAlt from ButcherPete FOSE in Fallout 3). | Functions (GECK); Lock_Functions; Functions (FO3)

GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers | NVSE | GetLowerPriorityEventHandlers(eventName:string, handler:[[UDF]], startPriority:int, scriptsToIgnore:scriptOrFormlist?, pluginsToIgnore:array?, pluginHandlersToIgnore:array?) -> array | Returns a Map Array with all the event handlers that have a 'lower or equal' priority than the specified handler at startPriority. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers. Each key in the map is a priority, and each value is an array representing a handler. A 'handler array' is a regular array containing: *Index [0]: Either a script handler, or an array representation of a plugin handler. **The latter is a StringMap containing the plugin name at ["Plugin"] and the handler name at ["Handler"]. <u>These strings may be empty.</u> *Index [1]: A StringMap of all the filters used by the handler. If the handler could not be found at startPriority, returns Ar_Null. <u>Only includes event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. | Functions_(NVSE); Array Functions; Event Handlers

GetMenuItemFilter | JIP | GetMenuItemFilter(menuCode:int, useMenuTarget:0/1?) -> filterCode:int | Returns a code indicating the current item-filter option selected in the specified menu. If <i>useMenuTarget</i> is non-zero, the function returns the filter selected for the target reference (right side). Otherwise, it returns the filter selected for the player (left side). <u>Filter codes</u>: <pre>0&#09;None 1&#09;Weapons 2&#09;Apparel 3&#09;Aid 4&#09;Misc 5&#09;Ammo</pre> | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions
GetMenuItemListRefs | JIP | GetMenuItemListRefs(inclFiltered:0/1?, rightPanel:0/1?) -> menuItems:array_var | Returns a Map Array containing, as '<i>values</i>', the Inventory References associated with the 'UI ListBox' items of the current active menu. The '<i>keys</i>' of the map-array are the indices of their paired items in the '<u>un-filtered</u>' 'UI ListBox'. These indices may be used for easy access to the 'UI Tile' connected to the list item, for retrieving/modifying its properties. Example: <pre>GetUIFloatAlt "ContainerMenu/NOGLOW_BRANCH/CM_ItemsRect/CM_Items_InventoryList/*:24/x"</pre> Supported menus: <pre>1002&#09;Pip-Boy Inventory Menu 1008&#09;Container Menu 1035&#09;Repair Menu 1053&#09;Barter Menu 1058&#09;Repair Services Menu</pre> The function takes 2 optional arguments: *'<i>inclFiltered</i>' - If non-zero, <u>all</u> list items will be returned in the array, including those not shown. Otherwise, only list items that are shown and selectable will be returned. *'<i>rightPanel</i>' - In menus where there are two panels (left and right), passing this argument as non-zero will return an array of the right-panel items. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); Inventory Reference Functions; Array Functions; Inventory Functions
GetMenuTargetRef | JIP | GetMenuTargetRef(menuCode:int) -> menuTarget:ref | Returns the reference from which the specified menu is derived (if currently open). The returned reference varies, depending on the menu: <pre>1008&#09;The container/actor inventory currently open. 1009&#09;The actor being spoken to. 1014&#09;The door/container being picked. 1016&#09;The underlying, source item stack (Inventory Reference). 1023&#09;The Map Marker on which the mouse is over. 1027&#09;The Perk on which the mouse is over. 1035&#09;The item being repaired (Inventory Reference). 1053&#09;The actor being bartered with. 1055&#09;The terminal being hacked. 1056&#09;The current target in VATS target-selection. 1057&#09;The terminal being used. 1058&#09;The actor (vendor) whose skills are used for repairing. 1061&#09;The weapon being modded (Inventory Reference). 1075&#09;The actor whose companion-wheel is being used. 1077&#09;The actor whose skills are used for crafting. 1083&#09;The opponent actor in a Caravan game. 1084&#09;The Perk on which the mouse is over.</pre> | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions

This command should only be used within a loop that executes within a single frame. Returns 0 (nothing) if there are no new references left to return that frame. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Functions (FO3)

GetNifBlockFlag | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockFlag(blockName:string, flagID:int, pcModel:1/2?) -> flagSet:0/1 (alias: GetNifFlag) | Returns whether the specified flag is set on the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 3rd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> ==Nif Block Flag IDs== <pre>0&#09;Hidden 1&#09;Selective Update 2&#09;Selective Update: Transforms 3&#09;Selective Update: Controller 4&#09;Selective Update: Rigid 5&#09;Display (UI?) Object 6&#09;Disable Sorting 7&#09;Selective Update: Transforms Override 8&#09;??? 9&#09;Save External Geometry Data 10&#09;No Decals 11&#09;Always Draw 12&#09;Mesh LOD 13&#09;Fixed Bound 14&#09;Top FadeNode 15&#09;Ignore Fade 16&#09;No Anim Sync (X) 17&#09;No Anim Sync (Y) 18&#09;No Anim Sync (Z) 19&#09;No Anim Sync (S) 20&#09;No Dismember 21&#09;No Dismember Validity 22&#09;Render Use 23&#09;Materials Applied 24&#09;High Detail 25&#09;Force Update 26&#09;Pre-Processed Node 27&#09;??? 28&#09;(Reserved) 29&#09;(Reserved) 30&#09;(Reserved) 31&#09;(Reserved)</pre> | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Flag Functions; Functions (JIP PP LN)
GetNifBlockParentNodes | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockParentNodes(blockName:string, pcModel:1/2?) -> parentNames:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing the names (string_vars) of all the parent nodes of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The first element of the array is the name of the direct parent of the NIF block, the second is its grandparent, and so on, all the way to the top root node. *The 2nd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Array Functions
GetNifBlockRotation | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockRotation(blockName:string, whichRotation:int?, pcModel:1/2?) -> rotation:array_var (alias: GetNifRot) | Returns a 3-element 'array_var' containing the XYZ rotation (yaw, pitch and roll) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, '<i>whichRotation</i>', determines the type of rotation that is returned: <pre>0&#09;(Default) EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node. 1&#09;EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world. 2&#09;INTRINSIC (local), relative to parent node. 3&#09;INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to the world.</pre> *The 3rd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> *If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns 'ar_Null'. | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Model Functions; File Functions; Array Functions
GetNifBlockRotationAlt | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockRotationAlt(blockName:string, resX:ScriptVar, resY:ScriptVar, resZ:ScriptVar, whichRotation:int?, pcModel:1/2?) -> success:0/1 (alias: GetNifRotAlt) | Returns the XYZ rotation (yaw, pitch and roll) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified 'Script Variables'. *The 5th argument, '<i>whichRotation</i>', determines the type of rotation that is returned: <pre>0&#09;(Default) EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node. 1&#09;EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world. 2&#09;INTRINSIC (local), relative to parent node. 3&#09;INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to the world.</pre> *The 6th argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> *If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns 0, and the script variables are unchanged. | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Model Functions; File Functions; Array Functions
GetNifBlockScale | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockScale(blockName:string, pcModel:1/2?) -> scale:float (alias: GetNifScale) | Returns the scale of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Scale Functions
GetNifBlockTranslation | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockTranslation(blockName:string, getWorld:0/1?, pcModel:1/2?) -> translation:array_var (alias: GetNifTrans) | Returns a 3-element 'array_var' containing the XYZ translation of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 2nd argument, '<i>getWorld</i>', is <u>optional</u>. If non-zero, the returned array will contain the XYZ world position of the block. *The 3rd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> *If the 3D model does not have the specified block, the function returns 'ar_Null'. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Model Functions; File Functions; Array Functions; Functions (JIP PP LN)
GetNifBlockTranslationAlt | JIP | reference.GetNifBlockTranslationAlt(blockName:string, resX:ScriptVar, resY:ScriptVar, resZ:ScriptVar, getWorld:0/1?, pcModel:1/2?) -> success:0/1 (alias: GetNifTransAlt) | Returns the XYZ translation of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The result XYZ are placed into the 3 specified 'Script Variables'. *The 5th argument, '<i>getWorld</i>', is <u>optional</u>. If non-zero, the returned array will contain the XYZ world position of the block. *The 6th argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. GetNumBrokenEquippedItems | ShowOff | ActorRefr.GetNumBrokenEquippedItems(healthPercThreshold:int?, flagsMask:int?) -> int (alias: GetNumBrokenEq) | Returns the number of broken items the calling actor has equipped. By default, the "isBroken" check is performed by checking if the health for the item's condition is at or below 0%. This minimum health percentage can be modified by changing healthPercThreshold. *'BUG: Does not currently account for weapons which have the "Increase Max Condition" Weapon Mod.' flagsMask is a bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. | Condition Functions; Actor Functions; Armor Functions; Weapon Functions; Inventory Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
GetNumCombatActorsFromActor | ShowOff | actorRefr.GetNumCombatActorsFromActor(maxDistance:float?, flagsBitMask:int?) -> numActors:int | Returns the amount of in-combat actors that are allies/enemies to the calling actor reference. If the first argument (maxDistance) is omitted, it defaults to 0. If set to a number above 0, the distance (in game Units) between the calling actor and each combat actor is checked; if the distance is smaller or equal to maxDistance, the combat actor can make its way into the resulting numActors count. The type of actor to be counted can be optionally filtered via the flagsBitMask argument (bit values are written under 'Notes').<br> If flagsBitMask is omitted, it defaults to 0. If left at 0, flagsBitMask is internally set to 3, meaning both combat allies and targets are counted. | Actor Functions; Bit Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
GetNumCrippledLimbs | ShowOff | ActorRefr.GetNumCrippledLimbs(healthThreshold:int?) -> int | Returns the amount of crippled limbs the calling actor has. The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the Actor Value for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing healthThreshold. | Condition Functions; Actor Functions; Actor Value Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

GetNVSEVersionFull | JIP | GetNVSEVersionFull() -> fullVersion:float | Returns the full, extended 'NVSE' version (<i>version</i>, <i>revision</i> and <i>beta</i>) loaded in the current game session. *This function can also safely be used as an NVSE check, since it is registered in an unused vanilla function opcode that otherwise returns zero. If the user does not have NVSE and JIP LN running, it will simply return 0. If JIP LN and NVSE are running, the full NVSE version is returned, rather than having to concatenate GetNVSEVersion and GetNVSERevision. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); File Functions

Possible return values and their meaning: <pre>-1&#09;N/A (Main Menu / Load Screen) 0&#09;HIDDEN 1&#09;CAUTION 2&#09;DETECTED 3&#09;DANGER </pre> Note that the player does not have to be sneaking/crouched for this function to return results such as 0 (HIDDEN). | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Condition_Functions; Detection Functions; Player Functions

GetPerkModifier | JIP | reference.GetPerkModifier(entryFunction:int, baseValue:float, form1:ref?, form2:ref?) -> perkModifier:float | Given a <i>baseValue</i>, returns it modified by the calling actors' active perk effects of a given <i>entryFunction</i>, as per GetNthPerkEntryFunction. The calling actor is taken as the reference on which the conditions for the 'Perk Owner' tab are run onto.<br> Optionally up to two additional forms are to be supplied, for entry functions which use additional condition tabs. The final result is thus the base value modified by all the actors' active perk effects of that entry function which can apply to the forms provided. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Perk Functions (JIP); Perk Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

Returns <b>-1</b> if the perk argument or calling reference is null. Returns <b>0</b> if the player does not have the perk, otherwise returns an int with the rank starting at <b>1</b>. | Perk Functions; Functions_(NVSE); Condition Functions

When passing a reference as parameter, returns true if the reference is flagged as a quest item. | Debugging; Functions_(NVSE); Condition Functions
GetPermanentActorValue | GECK1 | GetPermanentActorValue(statName:string) -> float (alias: GetPermAV) | Returns the permanent actor value with no bonus from situational perks, equipped items, drugs, etc. Behaves like GetBaseActorValue, except items (e.g. skill books) that add permanent Actor Value modifiers are included. *While the effects of the 'Tag!' Perk are accounted for, that is solely because it uses the AddTagSkills function to permanently modify the chosen AV. Other perks that use Perk Entry Points to modify the effective value of an AV are NOT accounted for. | Functions (GECK); Actor Value Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)

When used as a Condition Function, returns either 0 if there is no radio station currently playing, or 1/0 depending on if the Talking Activator is the same as the reference passed to the function or not (respectively). | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Radio Functions; Pip-Boy Functions; Condition Functions

GetPlayerCamFOV | JohnnyGuitar | reference.GetPlayerCamFOV(int:Integer) -> float | Returns a float of the player's camera FOV. If worldOr1stOrScene is set to 0, will return the World FOV (3rd person FOV). If it is 1, will return the first-person FOV. If it is 2, will return the FOV according to the current Scene being displayed by the SceneGraph. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)
GetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems | ShowOff | GetPlayerCanPickpocketEquippedItems() -> bool (alias: GetPCCanStealEqItems) | Returns 1 if the player can pickpocket equipped items, 0 otherwise. | Player Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Condition Functions
GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt | NVSE | GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt(disabledHow:int?, MovementFlag (0/1),:?, PipboyFlag (0/1),:?, FightingFlag (0/1),:?, POVFlag (0/1),:?, LookingFlag (0/1),:?, RolloverTextFlag (0/1),:?, SneakingFlag (0/1):?) -> bool | Returns true (1) if any of the specified player controls are currently disabled, and false (0) otherwise. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. The disabledHow arg can be used to check how the controls were disabled. Here are its possible values and uses: <pre> If 0, check if controls are disabled by calling mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only) If 1, ... disabled by any mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only) If 2, ... disabled by any mod OR disabled by vanilla DisablePlayerControls </pre> Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having disabledHow at 0, and *all* the Flag arguments at 1, meaning it will check if any of the flags are disabled by the calling mod. | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE)
GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx | NVSE | GetPlayerControlsDisabledAltEx(disabledHow:int?, flags:int?) -> bool | Returns true (1) if any of the specified player controls are currently disabled, and false (0) otherwise. Disabling and enabling is stored on a per-mod basis, so if two mods disable a control at some point, it will be disabled until both of them re-enable it, or until the save is reloaded. The disabledHow arg can be used to check how the controls were disabled. Here are its possible values and uses: <pre> If 0, check if controls are disabled by calling mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only) If 1, ... disabled by any mod (DisablePlayerControls*Alt/AltEx* only) If 2, ... disabled by any mod OR disabled by vanilla DisablePlayerControls </pre> Unlike GetPlayerControlsDisabledAlt, this function takes a single arg, flags. It is a Bitmask, with each bit representing a flag for a control to disable. See the 'Flag Definitions' section for more info. Default (no parameters) is equivalent to having disabledHow at 0, and *all* the flag bits at 1, meaning it will check if any of the flags are disabled by the calling mod. | Player Functions; Functions (NVSE)
