GetRandomInRange | JIP | GetRandomInRange(min:int, max:int) -> randNum:int | Similar to the vanilla function GetRandomPercent, except allows specifying a range. Returns a random integer in the interval '<i>min</i> <= x < <i>max</i>'. | Functions (JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); Math Functions
GetRandomizerSeed | ShowOff | anyReference.GetRandomizerSeed() -> int (alias: GetSeed) | Returns the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Random_seed randomizer seed] (unsigned int) for the calling mod. If no seed is set, returns -1 (no seed can be set to a negative value). *'BUG: If no seed is set, currently tries to return NAN, but that can't be represented as an int. So this behavior will be changed!' | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
GetRandomPercent | GECK1 | GetRandomPercent() -> int | Returns a random integer between 0 and 99 inclusive. | Functions (GECK); Math Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
GetRandomPercentSeeded | ShowOff | GetRandomPercentSeeded() -> int | Returns a random number between '1-100', which is based upon the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Random_seed randomizer seed] set for the calling mod. Note that GetRandomPercent generates from 0-99. If no seed is set for the calling mod, this first generates a 'default seed' (value of 5489). *'NOTE: since the default seed is constant, it will always generate the same sequence of random numbers.' (Behind the scenes, this advances the seed's internal numGenerated count so as to make the next call of RandSeeded/GetRandomPercentSeeded generate the next number in the pseudo-random sequence.) | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

The function returns either a temporary Inventory Reference, or a 'Base Form', depending on the menu type: 'Inventory Reference': *'Inventory Menu' (1002) *'Container Menu' (1008) *'Pipboy Repair Menu' (1035) *'Barter Menu' (1053) *'Vendor Repair Menu' (1058) *'Item Mod Menu' (1061) 'Base Form:' *'Pipboy DATA Menu' (1023) - Quest/Note/Challenge *'Pipboy STATS Menu' (1003) - Perk | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
GetSelf | GECK1 | GetSelf() (alias: this) | Returns the RefID of the calling reference. GetSelf is only useful when this information is accessed externally, or when the scripted reference's RefID needs to be passed as a parameter to a function like PushActorAway Returns null if there is no valid calling reference, OR if the calling reference happens to be a non-Persistent Item. This latter property can cause issues with scripts that run on items; see GetSelfAlt for a safer alternative. | Functions (GECK); Reference Variable Functions; ID Functions; Functions (FO3)
GetSelfAlt | NVSE | GetSelfAlt() (alias: ThisAlt) | Returns the calling reference, or a null form if there is none. Only useful when this information is accessed externally, or when the scripted reference needs to be passed as a parameter to a function like PushActorAway Unlike GetSelf, this will always return the calling reference, even if it happens to be a non-Persistent Item. If that check is not needed, then this is a strictly faster version of that function. | Functions (NVSE); Reference Variable Functions; ID Functions

GetSpellUsageNumEx | ShowOff | Actor.GetSpellUsageNumEx(Ingestible:form) -> float | Similar to the vanilla function GetSpellUsageNum - returns the number of times an Ingestible has been used by an actor. The function works on ingestibles that have a Usage Monitor Effect attached to them, and return value is multiplied by effect magnitude. However, unlike the vanilla version, which only returns a value while the ingestible's effect is active, this function allows checking the value at any time. It also correctly accounts for any Usage Monitor Effect set to decrease with time. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Game Effect Functions; Condition Functions
GetStage | GECK1 | GetStage(questID:form) -> int | Gets the highest completed quest stage. For example, if stages 10, 30, and 75 were completed, GetStage would return 75. Even when stage 30 is completed after stage 75, GetStage will still return 75 (i.e. the highest one). If you need to know if a certain quest stage is completed, use GetStageDone instead. | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
GetStageDone | GECK1 | GetStageDone(questID:form, questStage:int) -> bool | Returns 1 if the specified stage for the specified Quest has been completed, 0 otherwise. | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
GetStartingAngle | GECK1 | [Object].GetStartingAngle(axis:) -> float | Returns the original world rotation angle of the object on the selected axis (X, Y, or Z) in float. The angle is the angle the object was placed in the editor, not it's current game state. | Functions (GECK); Movement Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)

The returned position is the position the object was placed in the editor, not it's current game state. | Functions (GECK); Movement Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)

The ':' seems to be expected by the engine. | Functions_(NVSE); File Functions; INI Functions

GetTempEffects | JIP | reference.GetTempEffects() -> tempEffects:array_var | Returns a two-dimensional 'array_var' containing all temporary effect-entries (i.e. with limited duration) currently active on the calling actor, and their attributes. The elements in the main array are sub-arrays. Each sub-array contains the attribute values of one effect-entry, and has a total of 6 elements, ordered as follows: <pre>Index&#09;Attribute -----&#09;--------- 0&#09;Parent Effect-Form (Object Effect/Actor Effect/Ingestible) : ref 1&#09;Base Effect form : ref 2&#09;Magnitude : float 3&#09;Time Left : float 4&#09;Effect "caster" (the actor who applied the effect) : ref 5&#09;Duration : float</pre> | Functions_(JIP); Effect Functions (JIP); Game Effect Functions; Array Functions

The elements in the array are structured as follows: <pre>Index&#09;Attribute -----&#09;--------- 0&#09;Ingestible: form 1&#09;Time Left : float 2&#09;Duration : float</pre> | Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)

Returns 0 if the actor is invalid, it isn't dead, or if it isn't loaded / has been disabled.<br> If the actor gets disabled after dying, it loses its info about when it has died, meaning GetTimeDead returns 0 even if the actor is re-enabled. | Functions (GECK); Condition_Functions; Time Functions; Functions (FO3)

The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns -999 if UI trait was not found. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); UI Functions; Functions (FO3)
GetUIFloatAlt | NVSE | GetUIFloatAlt(traitName:string) -> float | Looks up a UI trait by name and returns its numeric float value. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns -999 if UI trait was not found. Supersedes GetUIFloat, which is bugged for templated children tiles. | Functions (NVSE); UI Functions

The returned StringMap has the following key/value pairs, all of which are integers: *Year (1601 - 30827) *Month (1-12) *DayOfWeek (1:Sunday - 7:Saturday) *Day (1-31) *Hour (0-23) *Minute (0-59) *Second (0-59) *Millisecond (0-999) | Functions_(NVSE); Time Functions; Array Functions

GetVATSTargetable | ShowOff | reference.GetVATSTargetable() -> 1/0 | Returns 1 (true) if the calling reference is targetable in VATS, 0 (false) otherwise. Note that this accounts for more than just if the reference was set to be non-targetable via SetVATSTargetable; it checks if the reference could be targeted in general. For example, if passing a disabled reference, this function returns false. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Actor Functions; VATS Functions

GetVATSValue | GECK1 | GetVATSValue(query:string, parameter:varies?) -> [varies] | The GetVATSValue condition function is available for use either when selecting a camera path, or within the Calculate To-Hit Chance entry point. Outside of VATS playback mode or Calculate To-Hit Chance, or if used in the console, the function will not return a meaningful value. When you select GetVATSValue, you have one or two additional parameters to select, the Query and an optional parameter to the Query. When used within the Calculate To-Hit Chance entry point, remember that the command has not actually taken place yet, so things like Success/Failure, critical strike info, dismembered/exploded limb or total damage are not calculated yet. However, the weapon, target, and targeted limb are available. | Functions (GECK); Condition Functions; VATS Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)

This function is viable for use in scripts, but further testing is needed to determine whether it can be used well after the actor has been consumed. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3)

'Reads the INI file data from a memory cache.' *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

HasPerk | GECK1 | ActorRef.HasPerk(perk:form, forTeammates:1/0?) -> hasPerk:0/1 | Returns whether or not the calling Actor has the specified Perk. *When called on the Player character, this function will return whether the player has the perk, <u>unless</u> the optional argument '<i>forTeammates</i>' is non-zero, in which case it will return whether the player's teammates have the perk. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the 'uNPCPerks' optional [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] feature is toggled in 'Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'. *When called via the console, this function will also print the current perk rank. | Functions (GECK); Condition Functions; Player Functions; Perk Functions; Functions (FO3)
HasPerkRank | LU | reference.HasPerkRank(perk:form, forTeammates:1/0?) -> rank:int | Returns the rank of the specified perk on the calling actor, or 0 if the actor does not have the perk. *When called on the 'player character', this function will return the perk rank on the player, <u>unless</u> the optional argument '<i>forTeammates</i>' is non-zero, in which case it will return the perk rank for teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the 'uNPCPerks' optional JIP LN feature is toggled in 'Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Perk Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Perk Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

Unlike ModelHasBlock, this function does not load or scan the full NIF; it operates on the runtime data created by AttachModel and InsertNode. It supports three modes: attached models, inserted nodes, and sparse node paths. | Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions

Omitting the optional arguments will cause the item to be hidden entirely from the barter menu. HighlightAdditionalReferenceAlt | ShowOff | reference.HighlightAdditionalReferenceAlt(checkInvisibility:1/0?, setFlashing:1/0?) -> 1/0 | Draws VATS-like flickering highlight over the calling reference. Unlike HighlightAdditionalReference, this can be called from scripts directly. Also unlike HighlightAdditionalReference, this function checks if the target is invisible and the player can't detect them via Improved Detection Perk Entry Point, unless checkInvisibility is set to 0. There is also an option to prevent setting a flashing ref by setting setFlashing to 0. This is useful for highlighting multiple references and not having any be flashing more than any other. This function is 'session-persistent'. Multiple actors can be highlighted this way, unlike with HighlightBodyPart. The last actor to be highlighted will be flashing.. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

InsertNode | JIP | InsertNode(refOrForm:form, addOrRemove:int, targetAndName:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> successful:0/1 | Inserts a 'NiNode' as either a child or as the parent of an existing NiNode in the 3D model of the specified object form. The function takes 3(+) arguments, as follows: *'<i>refOrForm</i>' - Either an 'object reference' or a 'base form'. In the former case, the NiNode will be inserted into the 3D model of that specific reference <u>only</u>, whereas in the latter case, it will be inserted into the 3D model of <u>any</u> reference instance of the base form. *'<i>addOrRemove</i>' - Either insert a new, or remove a previously inserted NiNode. Use one of the following values: <pre>0&#09;Remove; Pending reload 1&#09;Insert; Pending reload 2&#09;Remove; Instantaneous 3&#09;Insert; Instantaneous</pre> :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Pending reload'</span> - Changes will be applied once either the object's 3D is (re)loaded by the game, or Update3D is called on it. :*<span style="color:#3FFF3F">'Instantaneous'</span> - Changes will be applied <u>instantly</u>. *'<i>targetAndName</i>' - A 'formatted string' containing the name of an existing NiNode in the object's 3D model and, separated by a vertical bar '"&#124;"', the name of the new inserted NiNode. If the name of the new node is prefixed by a caret '"^"', it will be inserted as the <u>parent</u> of the target node. Otherwise, it will be inserted as the last <u>child</u> of the target node. <u>Names are case-sensitive</u>. | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions
interfaceID | JohnnyGuitar | interfaceID(interfaceID:int) -> hex | Returns the global 'RGB color' of the specified interface. <pre>1&#09;HUD - Main 2&#09;HUD - Alt. 3&#09;Terminal 4&#09;Pip-Boy 5&#09;Main/Pause Menu</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); UI Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)

If the specified width is smaller than the minimum width to represent the number, will use the minimum width. The maximum width is 32 bits. If AddPrefix is set to true (non-zero), will prepend "0b" to the string. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions

IsEventHandlerFirst | NVSE | IsEventHandlerFirst(eventName:string, handler:[[UDF]], startPriority:int, scriptsToIgnore:scriptOrFormlist?, pluginsToIgnore:array?, pluginHandlersToIgnore:array?) -> bool | Returns 1 (true) if there are no event handlers at a 'lower or equal' priority than the specified handler at startPriority. This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers. If the handler could not be found at startPriority, returns 0 (false). <u>Only accounts for event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. | Functions_(NVSE); Event Handlers

This is for debugging priority conflicts between handlers. If the handler could not be found at startPriority, returns 0 (false). <u>Only accounts for event handlers registered using SetEventHandler / SetEventHandlerAlt, or are generally managed by NVSE.</u> *Does NOT include the LN event handlers, such as OnCellEnter, as those handlers aren't managed by NVSE. *Events such as SetOnHitEventHandler are not managed by NVSE. | Functions_(NVSE); Event Handlers

IsFormValid | FOSE1 | IsFormValid(refVar:ref) -> bool | Returns True (1) if the object passed in the ref variable is valid, meaning it holds either a Reference or a Base Form. Returns False (0) if the object stored in the variable is not currently loaded in memory (for instance, a non-persistent reference) or if the variable doesn't hold any object. This is useful in cases where an invalid ref variable will cause the script or game to crash. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; Functions (FO3)

IsJumping | ShowOff | actorRefr.IsJumping() | Returns true if the calling actor reference is jumping, 0 if not. Unlike with checking if the actor is playing a jumping animation with IsAnimPlayingEx, this function will return 0 immediately when the actor lands, which allows detecting continuous jumps by holding down spacebar. <u>'Broken'. This function returns true even if the player never pressed the jump control, meaning it functions equivalently to IsInAir despite checking for a different flag.</u> | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Actor Functions; Movement Functions

IsLimbCrippled | ShowOff | ActorRefr.IsLimbCrippled(LimbIndex:int?, healthThreshold:int?) -> isCrippled | Returns 1 if a limb (specified by LimbIndex) is crippled. If no LimbIndex is specified, or if it is set to -1, instead returns 1 if any limb is crippled. The "isCrippled" check is performed by checking if the Actor Value for that limb's condition is at or below 0. The minimum amount of health for a limb to be considered "crippled" by this function can be modified by changing healthThreshold. | Condition Functions; Actor Functions; Actor Value Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

IsMusicPlaying | JIP | IsMusicPlaying(musicType:form?) -> musicState:int | Returns the playback state of the game's current background music: <pre>0&#09;Not Playing 1&#09;Paused 2&#09;Playing</pre> The '<i>musicType</i>' argument may be either a 'Music Type' form, a 'Media Location Controller' form, or it may be entirely omitted. *If specified, the function will return the playback state of that particular music type. *If omitted, the function will return the state of the currently playing music (if any). | Functions (JIP); Sound Functions (JIP); Sound Functions; Music

IsOutsideMainThread | ShowOff | IsOutsideMainThread() -> bool | Returns true if the function is being called from outside the game's main thread. This can be used to avoid calling code that may be unsafe when outside the main thread. For example, when outside the main thread, the Print functions can cause a crash if ToggleDebugText is on. In most cases, the only way to run code from outside the main thread is by using certain Event Handlers, which may be invoked from code residing in a helper thread. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Debugging

IsOwner | GECK1 | [RefID].IsOwner(<nowiki>ActorRefID|FactionID</nowiki>:ref (optional)?) -> int | Returns 1 if the calling object is owned by the specified faction/NPC. This only checks actual ownership on the reference not the inherited ownership from the cell. If called without parameter, IsOwner returns 1 if the calling object is owned by the player. Note that IsOwner returns 0 if the owner is set to NONE, so it cannot be used to detect public ownership. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Condition Functions; Ownership Functions; Functions (FO3)

IsPlayerInRegion | VEGAS1 | IsPlayerInRegion(:RegionID) -> int | Returns 1 if the actor is currently in the specified region. Always returns 0 if the actor is in an interior cell. | Functions (Vegas); Actor Functions; Player Functions
IsPlayerLookingAround | ShowOff | IsPlayerLookingAround() -> 1/0 | Returns 1 (true) if the player is moving the camera around, 0 (false) otherwise. Accounts for DisablePlayerControls's option to disable camera-changing inputs. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Player Functions
IsPlayerMovingIntoNewSpace | GECK1 | IsPlayerMovingIntoNewSpace() -> int | Returns 1 if the player is in the process of moving into a new space, i.e. fast traveling or moving through a load door. The use of this function is a bit arcane, but you probably want to put it on any follow package which is conditionalized by the player's location. Say you have an NPC whose follow package on the player is conditionalized to not go into interiors or outside of Megaton. Trouble is, followers get moved before the player arrives, so they don't reevaluate that the target is in an interior or outside of Megaton until they and the player are already there -- so you'd get the NPC fast traveling with you, and then walking all the way back to Skingrad from whereever you happened to go. This function returns true when the player is in the middle of loading into a new cell or fast travelling -- so if you put IsPlayerMovingIntoNewSpace == 0 as a condition on the NPC's follow package, he reevaluates during the load and won't follow the player outside of Megaton or into interiors. | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
IsPlayerSwimming | NVSE | IsPlayerSwimming() -> bool | Returns true if the player is swimming. | Functions_(NVSE); Functions_(Command Extender); Actor Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)
IsPlayerTagSkill | GECK1 | IsPlayerTagSkill([SkillName]:) -> int | Returns 1 if the specified skill is in any of the player's tag skill indexes. | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Condition Functions; Actor Value Functions; Functions (FO3)
IsPleasant | GECK1 | IsPleasant() -> int | Returns 0 if the current weather is not classified as pleasant. Greater than 0 and less than 1 means the weather is transitioning to a pleasant weather. 1 means that the weather is stable and pleasant. | Functions (GECK); Functions (GECK); Weather Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (FO3)

If you are calling LeveledListAddForm with a leveled list passed as the formToAdd argument, you should pass in 0.0 as the health argument. This matches with leveled lists in vanilla. Otherwise, you may see decreased spawn rates. | Functions_(JIP); Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP); Leveled List Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions_(FO3)

Returns the number of entries removed. | Functions_(JIP); Leveled_List_Functions_(JIP); Leveled List Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions_(FO3)

levelOverride | ShowOff | levelOverride(levelOverride:int?) -> int (alias: GetCalculatedSkillPointsEarnedPerLevel) | If the player is not in the Level-Up Menu, returns the amount of Skill points they would get from their next level-up. Otherwise, returns the max amount of Skill points the player can allot from their current level-up. If specified and set to a non-zero value, the levelOverride optional argument can be used to replace the player's current level in the equation with a theoretical level. Note that if this change is made before the adjustment for not being in the Level-Up menu (which adds +1 level). Accounts for the 'Adjust Gained Skill Points' Perk Entry Point. Also accounts for lStewieAl's Tweaks's 'bModifySkillPointsEarned'. Does NOT account for the limit that occurs when the player is close to maxing out every Skill (they receive less Skill points to prevent softlocking). | Player Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
levelOverride | ShowOff | levelOverride(levelOverride:int?) -> int (alias: GetCalculatedPerkPointsEarnedPerLevel) | If the player is not in the Level-Up Menu, returns the amount of Perk points they would get from their next level-up. Otherwise, returns the max amount of Perk points the player can allot from their current level-up. If specified and set to a non-zero value, the levelOverride optional argument can be used to replace the player's current level in the equation with a theoretical level. Note that if this change is made before the adjustment for not being in the Level-Up menu (which adds +1 level). Accounts for the iLevelsPerPerk gamesetting. *Used to account for lStewieAl's Tweaks's 'iPerksPerLevel', but the support was scrapped since support for multiple Tweaks ini files was added. **As such, 'this function can only return 1 or 0'. Does NOT account for the reduction that occurs when the player is running out of perks to choose (they receive less Perk points to prevent softlocking). | Player Functions; Perk Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

ListAddArray | ShowOff | ListAddArray(formlist:baseform, array:array_var, index:int) -> success:bool (alias: AddArrayToFormList) | Adds the forms from an array to the FormList at the given index (or at the end if not provided). The array's contents are appended in the array key order. Returns 1 for success, 0 if an error is encountered. This does the same changes to the formlist as multiple ListAddForms would, and is therefore subject to the same warnings as that function. This also means the changes are not savebaked. | List_Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
ListAddForm | FOSE1 | ListAddForm(formlist:baseform, formToAdd:form, index:int?, checkForDupes:bool?) -> int | Adds the form to the list at the given index (or at the end if not provided, or if -2 is the given index). If the function fails to do anything, returns -1, otherwise returns the index for where the form was added. Added in xNVSE v6.2.5, the checkForDupes arg can set to 1 (true) to prevent adding duplicate forms to the formlist. This function is subject to warnings concerning being used on non-empty lists. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); List_Functions; Functions_(FO3)
ListAddList | ShowOff | ListAddList(destinationList:baseform, sourceList:baseForm, index:int) -> success:bool (alias: AddFormListToFormList) | Adds the forms from sourceList to destinationList at the given index (or at the end if not provided). The contents are appended in the order they appear in sourceList. Returns 1 for success, 0 if an error is encountered. This does the same changes to the formlist as multiple ListAddForms would, and is therefore subject to the same warnings as that function. This also means the changes are not savebaked. | List_Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

MarkActivatorAshPile | JIP | MarkActivatorAshPile(activator/list:form) | Marks the specified 'Activator' base form(s) as 'Ash Pile'-type, so that the game treats and handles it accordingly. The form can then be attached to actor references using AttachAshPileEx. *This remains in effect for the duration of the current game session. markerReforList | JohnnyGuitar | markerReforList(markerReforList:form, iconPath:string) | Sets the custom map marker icon of a Map Marker reference or formlist of references using a specified path, relative to Data/Textures. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Functions (ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3); UI Functions; Map Functions; Texture Functions; File Functions; String Functions
MarkForDelete | GECK1 | [ObjectRefID].MarkForDelete() | 'MarkForDelete' allows a script to set the delete flag on a disabled reference. The reference is placed in a queue for deletion a few frames later. Whenever an object is disabled, it remains in memory. With items that can be dropped from inventory but work on their own, every drop and every pickup is a disable. Players could wrack up several hundred useless disabled objects in memory by the end of the game, each running a script (yes, disabled objects still run their scripts). This function will mark an object for future deletion. When the system does it's normal clean up, the object will be truly deleted, and it's script will no longer be active. References created during gameplay, such as those dropped by an actor or something created with the PlaceAtMe function, are truly deleted. They no longer exist in any way. References that are in the ESM or an ESP file can be marked for deletion, but are not truly deleted. However, the script on such objects is removed and no longer runs. | Functions (GECK); Designer Debug Tools; Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions
MatchFaceGeometry | GECK1 | [targetActor:ref].MatchFaceGeometry(sourceNPC:ref, matchPercent:float) | Changes an actor's face geometry to match another actor's face. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Functions (FO3)
MatchRace | GECK1 | Actor.MatchRace(ActorRefID:ref) | Changes an actor's race to match another actor's current race. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Functions (FO3)

The operation to apply is specified via the operator string. See 'Notes' for valid operator strings. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Matrix Functions; Math Functions; Array Functions

Matrix_Dump | ShowOff | Matrix_Dump(matrix:array_var) (alias: Mat_Dump) | Prints the given Matrix array in console, in Matrix notation. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Matrix Functions; Math Functions; Array Functions
Matrix_IsMatrix | ShowOff | Matrix_IsMatrix(array:array_var) -> bool (alias: Mat_IsMat) | Returns true if an array can be considered a mathematical Matrix, false otherwise. Expects that either all elements in the main array are sub-arrays (for two-dimensional arrays) containing numbers, or are numbers. The array must have the same number of elements in each row. Empty or invalid arrays are not considered valid matrices. Two-dimensional and one-dimensional arrays can both be matrices if they follow the above rules. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Matrix Functions; Math Functions; Array Functions

maxRange | ShowOff | maxRange(maxRange:float?, flags:int?) -> numCompassHostiles | Returns the amount of hostile compass targets, optionally filtered by maxRange and flags. If maxRange is unspecified / set to 0, no additional distance check is performed. Otherwise, the actor will have to be within the specified distance (in game Units) to be counted. flags is a bitmask. If flags is unspecified / set to 0, nothing else happens. Otherwise, additional filters will be applied to the count if certain bits are set. <br> Possible filter values: <pre> 1 No invisible actors </pre> | Actor Functions; Bit Functions; Condition Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

MessageBoxEx | NVSE | MessageBoxEx(string:formatable) -> none | Similar to ShowMessage but accepts a string directly rather than via a message form. Variables can be passed using string formatting, or string variables via ToString($). The message will be displayed in a box that prompts for user confirmation. <br>Supports up to 10 custom buttons separated by "<nowiki>|</nowiki>". To determine which button was selected, GetButtonPressed can be used. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; Message Functions; UI Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
MessageBoxExAlt | JIP | MessageBoxExAlt(callbackScript:ref, msgStrings:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) | An improved version of the NVSE function MessageBoxEx. *Does not require using GetButtonPressed for message boxes with multiple buttons, but instead passes the selected button index via a UDF script, which is invoked when a button is pressed. *Allows up to '255' buttons (instead of 10 max). *Allows setting the message box title (optional). The title text must be passed at the beginning of the formatted string, enclosed by circumflexes: '^'<i>title</i>'^' (see example, below). The function takes two(+) arguments: *'<i>callbackScript</i>' - A 'UDF Script' (as explained above. Also see example script, below). *'<i>msgStrings</i>' - A 'formatted string' containing the message box title, text and buttons (all optional). Up to 20 variables may be passed for format specifiers. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Message Functions

The icon and played sound are both optional, and are passed as part of the formatted string, before the actual message text, each suffixed with a bar "&#124;" symbol (see examples below). *The icon is a path to a DDS image file (relative to ..\Data\Textures). *The played sound is the 'Editor ID' of a Sound form, and must be prefixed with a '$'. | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Message Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)

(alias: MoveToFade) | Moves the object with fade to the specified reference's location with optional axis offset parameters. (alias: sgtm) | SetGlobalTimeMultiplier Sets the time multiplier. 1.0 is standard time, 0.5 is slow mo, and 0.25 is even slower. If keepSoundUnchanged is set to 1 (true), then sounds will not be sped up/slowed down when the time mult is changed. | Functions (GECK); Time Functions

Various actor values have flags specifying their minimum and maximum values, such as 1 and 10 for Charisma or 0 and 100 for Barter, even though these stats can have values outside of this range. The game uses thresholded actor values for calculations, such as detection formula. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Actor Value Functions

Unlike them, this is session persistent as opposed to savebaked. Sets the ownership of the calling reference or Cell to the specified NPC or Faction BaseForm. If SetOwnership/SetCellOwnership were previously called on a form, those changes are savebaked, so SetOwnershipTemp's changes won't be applied when a save is loaded where those changes have been baked. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Object Functions; Ownership Functions

numPerksOverride | ShowOff | numPerksOverride(numPerksOverride:int?, menuTitleOverride:string?) -> bool | Opens the Level-Up menu (menu ID = 1027) to the Perk allotment page. The "Back" button is also hidden, meaning the player cannot choose to go back to the Skills page. Does NOT cause an increase in level. By default, the amount of perks to allot is the same as what the player would receive for their current level. *For example, calling this when the player is at level 1 and iLevelsPerPerk is 2 (default) means that the player does not receive any perks. *The 'iPerksPerLevel' setting from lStewieAl's Tweaks affects the default amount of perks for this function. ==Parameters== The amount of perks received can be overridden by specifying a value for numPerksOverride that is greater than -1. *This value overrides the 'iPerksPerLevel' setting from lStewieAl's Tweaks. The title of the Level-Up menu can be changed by specifying a string for menuTitleOverride. *The default title for the menu is specified by the sLevelUpTitleText gamesetting. ==Return Value== Returns true (1) if the Perk menu is successfully shown, false (0) otherwise. | Perk Functions; Player Functions; UI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
NumToHex | NVSE | NumToHex(ToConvert:int, WidthToPadTo:int?, AddPrefix:bool?) -> string (alias: IntToHex) | Converts a specified integer to a hexadecimal string, optionally padded to the specified width. If the specified width is smaller than the minimum width to represent the number, will use the minimum width. The maximum width is 8 characters. If AddPrefix is set to true (non-zero), will prepend "0x" to the string. * This arg is added in xNVSE 6.2.9. The IntToHex alias was added in xNVSE 6.2.9. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions

PlayAnimationPath | kNVSE | actor.PlayAnimationPath(anim sequence path:string, bIsFirstPerson:0/1?) | Plays the specified animation sequence path. The animation will automatically deactivate when it reaches the end key, unlike ActivateAnim which requires manual deactivation. This function is safer to use than ActivateAnim for most animation needs. PlayAnimationPath is a good option for activating additive animations. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
PlayAnimSequence | JIP | reference.PlayAnimSequence(sequenceName:string, nodeName:string?) -> successful:0/1 | Plays the specified 'animation sequence' on the calling reference. The sequence must be defined in a 'NiControllerManager', in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. The 2nd argument, '<i>nodeName</i>', is optional, and may be used for directing the function to a specific node in the 3D model, where the 'NiControllerManager' is found. If omitted, the function will look for it in the root node. When called on an actor, this function can play <i>any</i> variant of <i>any</i> Animation Group that is available for that actor. This is done by passing the path to the '.kf' animation file (relative to the 'meshes\' folder) as the sequence name. This function differs from PlayGroup in that: *It enables to play <u>any</u> defined animation sequence, and is not restricted to sequences matching one of the Animation Group. *It enabled to specify a node other than the root node. | Functions (JIP); Animation Functions; Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions

PlayGroup | GECK1 | Actor.PlayGroup(AnimGroup:string, InitFlag:int) | 'PlayGroup' plays an animation group on the calling actor. See AnimGroups for a list of possible animations. A flag must be used to control initialization of the group. InitFlag :0 = Normal - The current animation will finish it's full cycle, and the new animation will start from its beginning. :1 = Immediate Start - The current animation will stop regardless of the frame it is on, and the new animation will start from its beginning. :2 = Immediate Loop -The current animation will stop regardless of the frame it is on, and the new animation will start at the beginning of its loop cycle. | Functions (GECK); Animation Functions; Actor Functions; Functions (FO3)
PlayHolotape | JohnnyGuitar | PlayHolotape(form:Note, playStartStopSound:Integer) | Plays the specified holotape. Optional argument plays the "Start/Stop" Sound. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Note Functions
PlayIdleEx | JIP | reference.PlayIdleEx(idleAnim:ref?) | An alternate, extended version of the vanilla function PlayIdle - causes the calling actor to play a specified 'Idle Animation'. Unlike the former, which takes a string argument comprising the name of the 'Idle Animation' to be played, this function takes an 'Editor ID'. Moreover, the behavior of this function is determined by the passed argument: *If a "parent" animation (i.e. grouping one or more "child" animations, or sub-parents), the function will randomly pick and play the first animation whose conditions (if any) evaluate as <i>true</i>. *If a "child" animation (i.e. not grouping other animations), the function will force-play that animation, ignoring conditions. *If the argument is entirely omitted, the function behaves exactly the same as PickIdle. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions; Animation Functions; Functions (ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)

Visuals will play at least one cycle, regardless of duration, and if no duration is specified they play indefinitely or until the art stops animating. | Functions (GECK); Game Effect Functions; Functions (FO3)

posX | JohnnyGuitar | posX(posX:float, posY:float, posZ:float?, skipDisabled:int(0/1), zLimit:float?) -> coords:array_var | Given coordinates within currently loaded Navmesh, returns an array containing coordinates of the center of the Navmesh triangle, and distance to it, as follows: <pre>Index&#09;Attribute -----&#09;--------- 0&#09;X - float 1&#09;Y - float 2&#09;Z - float 3&#09;Distance - float </pre> Two additional arguments allow skipping disabled navmesh triangles and (optionally) limit the search on Z-axis. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Navmesh Functions

PreloadMagicEffect | GECK1 | PreloadMagicEffect(effectID:baseform) | This function will queue up the particles for a spell effect, if they aren't already loaded. The function will do nothing if the particles are already loading. It will be helpful only when a non-actor reference casts a spell, as actors preload their own spells automatically. | Functions (GECK); Game Effect Functions; Functions (FO3)
Print | NVSE | Print(string:expression) -> none | Prints a string expression to the console. This is mainly a convenience function to eliminate the need for temporary string variables when printing complex strings using PrintToConsole, as it supports string concatenation and the ToString function, also making string formatting mostly unnecessary, although it can still be helpful to do things like ending the line with "%r". Like Let and if eval, NVSE expressions and nested function calls are supported without compiler override. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; NVSE-Aware Functions
PrintActiveTile | FOSE1 | PrintActiveTile() | Prints name of highlighted UI component for debug purposes | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; Functions (FO3)
PrintDebugF | NVSE | PrintDebugF(OutputFileName:string, OutputText:string) -> none (alias: PrintDF) | As PrintF, but only outputs if the calling mod has Debug Mode enabled. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; File Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
PrintF | NVSE | PrintF(OutputFileName:string, OutputText:string) -> none | Similar to Print, but outputs the string to the specified file (in the Fallout directory) instead of the console. If the file exists it will be appended, otherwise created. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; File Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
PrintToConsole | FOSE1 | PrintToConsole(Message:string, Formatting ...:|Optional = optional) (alias: PrintC) | Prints a string to console. Optionally with up to 20 format specifiers. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; Functions (FO3)
PrintVar | NVSE | PrintVar(variableName:variable, otherVariableNames:variable(up to 14)?) -> none (alias: PrintV) | Prints the given variable's name and value to the console. Primary use case is debugging while scripting. Since xNVSE 6.3.0, up to 14 additional variables can be passed to the function. This will act the same way as calling 'PrintVar' multiple times with those different variables. | Functions_(NVSE); Debugging; NVSE-Aware Functions
ProjectExtraCamera | JIP | reference.ProjectExtraCamera(cameraName:string, targetGeometry:string, FoV:float, pixelSize:int?, grayscale:0/1?) -> successful:0/1 | Projects the current scene frame, as viewed from the specified camera, onto either a 'NiTri'-based geometry block, or an 'image'-type UI tile. The function takes up to 5 arguments, as follows: *'<i>cameraName</i>' - The name of a camera previously attached using AttachExtraCamera. *'<i>targetGeometry</i>' - Either **'a)' A name (case-sensitive) of a 'NiTriShape/Strips' block in the calling reference's 3D model. The block '<u>MUST</u>' have either 'BSShaderNoLightingProperty' or 'BSShaderPPLightingProperty' property; or **'b)' The full path of an 'image'-type UI tile, <u>prefixed with an asterisk</u>. *'<i>FoV</i>' - The camera's 'field-of-view' angle. The lower this value, the more zoomed-in the rendered image will be (it is recommended not to use FoVs lower than 1, or greater than 10). *'<i>pixelSize</i>' (optional) - The resolution (and hence quality) of the rendered image. 256x256, by default. It is not recommended (and, in most cases, not necessary) to use resolutios greater than 1024x1024, especially when calling this function every frame. *'<i>grayscale</i>' (optional) - If present and non-zero, the rendered image will be generated in grayscale, otherwise (default) in full-colour. ==Notes== *The camera 'MUST' be within the current loaded cells grid, or in the same interior cell as the player. *If an image was successfully rendered from the camera and projected onto the target, the function returns 1 (otherwise, 0). *A calling reference is not required when projecting onto a UI tile. *When projecting onto a NiTri block, the image will be wraped according to the mesh UV map. *This function should be considered '<u>EXPERIMENTAL</u>', and may change considerably (or be scrapped, altogether) in the future. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP)
projectile | JohnnyGuitar | projectile(projectile:form, soundID:int(1-3), sound:form) | Sets new sound for a specified Projectile. Sound IDs are as follows: <pre> 1 - Projectile sound 2 - Countdown sound 3 - Disable sound</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Sound Functions
PurgeCellBuffers | GECK1 | PurgeCellBuffers() (alias: PCB) | Forcibly unloads all unattached cells in cell buffers. | Functions (GECK); Cell Functions; Functions (FO3)
PushActorAway | GECK1 | [source:ref].PushActorAway(target:ref, force:int) | 'PushActorAway' Knocks down (ragdolls) one actor and pushes it away from another with a given force. | Functions (GECK); Movement Functions; Functions (FO3)
PushActorAwayAlt | JIP | actor.PushActorAwayAlt(force:int, posXmod:float?, posYmod:float?, posZmod:float?) | An alternate version of the vanilla PushActorAway: Knocks down (ragdolls) the calling actor, and either pushes it away from, or pulls it towards a 3D coordinate point of 'origin', relative to the actor's world position. *The 1st argument specifies the Havok force applied on the actor. A 'positive' value pushes the actor <u>away</u> from the 'origin', whereas a 'negative' value pulls it <u>towards</u> the 'origin'. *Arguments 2 through 4 (origin X/Y/Z modifiers) are all optional and default to 0. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Movement Functions
PushActorNoRagdoll | JIP | actor.PushActorNoRagdoll(force:int, directionAng:float, originRef:ref?) | An alternate version of PushActorAway/PushActorAwayAlt. Pushes the calling actor in a specified direction, <u>'without'</u> knocking it down or ragdoll-ing it. The function takes 3 arguments: *The 1st argument specifies the Havok 'force' applied on the actor. A 'positive' value pushes the actor <u>away</u>, whereas a 'negative' value pulls it <u>towards</u> the opposite direction. *The 2nd argument specifies the 'direction', as a global 'Z-angle'. *The 3rd argument (optional) may alternatively be used to specify the direction as relative to the position of an 'object reference'. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Movement Functions
PushObject | JIP | reference.PushObject(posXmod:float, posYmod:float, posZmod:float, force:float, originRef:ref?) | Similar to PushActorAway, but intended for non-actor, movable objects. "Pushes" the calling reference in a vector-like force and direction specified by the passed arguments. *By default, the 'origin' of the vector is the calling reference's world position. The 1st through 3rd arguments allow to adjust the origin relative to that position. *The 4th argument specifies the Havok force applied on the object. A 'positive' value pushes the object <u>away</u> from the origin, whereas a 'negative' value pulls the object <u>towards</u> the origin. *The 5th argument is optional. If not omitted, the specified reference will be used as the vector 'origin', instead of the calling reference. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
Quaternion_GetMatrix | ShowOff | Quaternion_GetMatrix(quaternion:array_var) -> array (alias: Quat_GetMat) | Returns the 3x3 Rotation Matrix associated with the given Quaternion. The quaternion array must be laid out as such: [w, x, y, z]. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Matrix Functions; Math Functions; Array Functions

questname | GECK1 | questname(questname:EditorID) | Stops the quest, which means: * Any attached dialogue will be disabled. * The quest script, if any, will stop running after its current iteration (see note below). | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Savebaked Functions
questname | GECK1 | questname(questname:EditorID) | Starts the quest, which means: * Any attached dialogue will be enabled. * The quest script, if any, will begin running. | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Savebaked Functions

race | JohnnyGuitar | race(race:form, flagID:int(1/4)) -> int | Returns the specified flag of a Race. Flag IDs are as follows:<pre> 0 - Playable 1 - Child</pre> | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Flag Functions; Actor Functions

Rand | NVSE | Rand(Minimum:float, Maximum:float) -> float | Returns a random float between specified minimum and maximum. | Functions_(NVSE); Math Functions
RandSeeded | ShowOff | RandSeeded(Minimum:UInt, Maximum:UInt) -> int (alias: GenerateSeededRandomNumber) | Returns a random number between specified Minimum and Maximum, which is based upon the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Random_seed randomizer seed] set for the calling mod. Unlike Rand and GetRandomInRange, the generated number is in the interval 'min <= x <= max' (includes max). Minimum must be <= to Maximum. Returns -1 if this assertion fails. *'NOTE: for now this check isn't actually in place; the code will just crash if assertion fails. Will be added later though.' If no seed is set for the calling mod, this first generates a 'default seed' (value of 5489). *'NOTE: since the default seed is constant, it will always generate the same sequence of random numbers.' (Behind the scenes, this advances the seed's internal numGenerated count so as to make the next call of RandSeeded/GetRandomPercentSeeded generate the next number in the pseudo-random sequence.) | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
ReadArrayFromFile | JIP | ReadArrayFromFile(filePath:string, transpose:1/0?) -> array_var | Reads data from the specified file and stores it in an array variable. If the file contains a single line of data, the function returns a standard, list-type array. Otherwise, it returns a matrix, the layout of which depends on the 2nd, optional argument, '<i>transpose</i>': *'0' (default) - The 1st index is the 'column' number and the 2nd index is the 'row' number (someArray[column][row]). *'1' - The 1st index is the 'row' number and the 2nd index is the 'column' number (someArray[row][column]). <u>The file must be formatted accordingly</u>: <ol type="1"><li>Values must be separated with tabs (one or more).</li> <li>When using non-numeric types as values:</li> *Strings must be prefixed with <b>'$'</b>. Example: <b>$Brave men run, in my family.</b> *Forms must be prefixed with <b>'@'</b>, followed by the source file name, then <b>':'</b>, and finally the six-digit formID (i.e. without the mod index). Example (for Craig Boone): <b>@FalloutNV.esm:096BCE</b></ol> | Functions_(JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); File Functions; Array Functions
ReadFromJSON | ShowOff | ReadFromJSON(filepath:string, jsonPointer:string?, parserMode:int?, useCache:bool?) -> anyType | Parses a JSON file at the given filepath and returns it converted as a script value. See 'Examples' section to see how JSON value types are translated to a script variable type, as well as how to handle all possible return type if it can't be predicted. jsonPointer determines the path in the JSON hierarchy to read from; pass '""' (empty string) to get the root value (everything). It follows the [https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901#section-3 standardized syntax for JSON Pointers]. filepath is relative to root folder (where FalloutNV.exe lies). parserMode determines which JSON-like parser to use, which determines how the file's contents will be interpreted. *Modes are listed in the 'Notes' section. If useCache is true, then the parsed JSON will be stored in session-persistent memory. *This is useful to avoid the performance cost of re-parsing the file, but at the cost of memory. *To clear the cache, use ClearFileCacheShowOff. | File Functions; JSON Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
ReadStringFromFile | JIP | ReadStringFromFile(filePath:string, startAtLine:int?, lineCount:int?) -> string_var | Reads the contents of the specified file and stores them in a string variable. *If <i>startAtLine</i> is specified, the file will be read beginning from that line number (otherwise, it will be read from line 1). *If <i>lineCount</i> is specified, only that number of lines will be read. | Functions_(JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); File Functions

RefillPlayerAmmo | ShowOff | RefillPlayerAmmo(numAmmoToAdd:int) | A debug-oriented function to give the player more ammo for their currently equipped weapon. If a specific type of ammo is equipped, will add that ammo type instead of the weapon's default ammo type. Additionally, this ammo will be used to refill the player's clip as much as possible, with the rest ending up in the backup ammunition count. This is useful to avoid potentially long reload animations. Will not work with equipped weapons that have regenerating ammo. This acts as a shortcut to using AddItem to recover ammo for your current weapon. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Player Functions; Inventory Functions; Debugging

RegisterSRScript | JIP | RegisterSRScript(filePath:string) -> successful:0/1 | Registers a source file to be handled by 'Script Runner'. The main purpose of this function is to make it possible to register SR script files selectively and conditionally, on game restart. *This function may only be called from a 'Game Restart' ('gr_' prefix) 'SR' script, and will have no effect otherwise. *The '<i>filePath</i>' string argument should be the path to the source file, relative to '..\Data\NVSE\plugins\scripts\', and must include <u>at least</u> one subfolder. i.e. The file must be located in a subfolder of '..\scripts\'. *The file name should follow the standard naming convention of 'SR' files - the prefix will determine when the script is executed. *If successful, the function returns '1'. The function will return '0' if: **The file could not be accessed, or **The source script failed to compile, or **A file with the same name was already registered. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script Functions

'Details on caching INI files:' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

RemoveEventHandler | NVSE | RemoveEventHandler(eventID:string, FunctionScript:ref, Filter1:pair?, Filter2:pair?, Priority:pair?) -> bool | Removes the specified script as an event handler for the specified event. Two optional filter arguments can be supplied as 'key::value' pairs to filter events according to the target and/or object. If both filters are omitted, all event handlers matching the script and event will be removed. If only one filter is omitted, all handlers matching the script, event, and the other filter will be removed. An optional priority argument can be supplied as '"priority"::value'. More information is in the 'Priority System' section. Returns true if at least one matching event handler was found and removed, false otherwise. | Functions_(NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions; Event Handlers

RemoveMeIRAlt | JIP | reference.RemoveMeIRAlt(quantity:int?, clearOwnership:0/1?, targetRef:ref?) | Removes the calling 'Inventory Reference' from its containing object. *If <i>quantity</i> is not specified, or is <= 0, the whole count of the <i>Inventory Reference</i> will be removed. *If <i>clearOwnership</i> is not specified, or is not 0, ownership of the item will be cleared. *If <i>targetRef</i> is specified, the <i>Inventory Reference</i> will be moved to the target container, preserving all its stats/attributes (health, script, mods, etc.). Otherwise, it will be destroyed. | Functions (JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Object Functions; Inventory Functions; Inventory Reference Functions
RemoveMerchantContainer | GECK1 | RemoveMerchantContainer(actor:ref) | 'RemoveMerchantContainer' clears an existing merchant container off of an actor. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions
RemoveModLocalData | NVSE | |arguments = {{FunctionArgument.RemoveModLocalData(Key:string) -> Existed:bool | Deletes the Mod Local Data entry associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. Returns false (0) if no data existed for the specified key, otherwise true (1). | Functions_(NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions

If -1 is used as an index, the package is removed from the end of the list. Indices are shifted when removing the package, and new package at the same index is returned. | Functions (NVSE); Actor Functions; AI Functions; Package Functions
RemovePerk | GECK1 | ActorRef.RemovePerk(perk:form, forTeammates:1/0?) | Removes the specified Perk from the calling Actor. *When called on the Player character, this function will remove the perk from the player, <u>unless</u> the optional argument '<i>forTeammates</i>' is non-zero, in which case it will remove the perk from all teammates. *When called on any actor other than the player, this function will work correctly only if the 'uNPCPerks' optional [https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/58277 JIP LN] feature is toggled in 'Data\NVSE\plugins\jip_nvse.ini'. | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Perk Functions

reputation | JohnnyGuitar | reputation(reputation:baseform, tier:int(1-4), iconPath:string) -> success(0/1) | Sets a custom icon path for the icon that appears in the message box when player's reputation tier changes. Changes are unique to the reputation form passed as an argument. Reputation icons have 4 tiers used to indicate changes (tier names are derived from default icons): <pre>1&#09;in pain 2&#09;sad 3&#09;neutral 4&#09;very happy</pre>Changes affect corner messages for reputation as well - tier 2 is used for negative changes, and 4 for positive. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); UI Functions; Reputation Functions
ReputationID | VEGAS1 | |arguments =.ReputationID(ReputationID:editorID, Mixed/Good/Bad:int) | Returns the current reputation with a certain faction. Its two parameters are the reputation ID in question and an integer to denote mixed, good, or bad where 0 = mixed, 1 = good, and 2 = bad. The player is only ever on one of these sliders at a time. If GetReputationThreshold always returns a value between 0 and 6. If the function is called with a faction which the player has both fame and infamy equal to zero, it will return a value of 1 in all three cases of checking the mixed, good, and bad axes. If the player has a higher fame than infamy, GetReputationThreshold will return a number between 2 and 6 on the fame axis depending upon how high the player's fame is, and 0 for both mixed and infamy reputations, and so on. | Functions (Vegas); Reputation Functions; Condition Functions

Note that this does <i>not</i> reset the object's 3d position in the world if it has been moved. | Functions (GECK); Miscellaneous Functions

ResetFallDamageTimer | GECK1 | ref.ResetFallDamageTimer() | Resets the fall damage timer. When the timer is reset, calling object will take damage as if it just started falling. | Functions (GECK); Miscellaneous Functions

Note that the vanilla function ResetFallDamageTimer, which claims to have the same effect, is unfortunately broken. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions; Time Functions; Functions (ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)

ResetPipboyManager | GECK1 | ResetPipboyManager() | After the player equips a PipBoy, this must be called in order to use it. If not, the PipBoy will behave like an NPC PipBoy with no access to the inventory, stats, or data. In addition to (re-)initializing menu interactivity, this function also resets the PipBoy's external appearance to default, disabling the PipBoy's "LightOn" glow texture if currently active. | Functions (GECK); UI Functions; Pip-Boy Functions

RestoreActorValue | GECK1 | ref.RestoreActorValue(actorValue:string, value:float) (alias: RestoreAV) | 'RestoreActorValue' is called on a Reference and takes two parameters, an ActorValue and a value. The Function alters the damage modifier of a given actor value. The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. RestoreActorValue will increase the modifier by the given amount or to 0. | Functions (GECK); Actor Value Functions

The damage modifier is always a negative number, or zero if there is no damage to the actor value. RestoreActorValueAlt will increase the modifier by the given amount. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Actor Value Functions

Return | GECK1 | Return() | 'Return' is used to force a script to stop processing from that line on. It does not JUST terminate the current block, it terminates everything within that script that is placed below the line in which 'Return' is called, including any blocks. | Functions (GECK); Miscellaneous Functions
RewardKarma | GECK1 | RewardKarma(value:int) | Awards/removes Karma Points to/from the player. It is not called on a reference and the parameter is an integer which represents the amount of Karma to add to the player. If set to a negative value, will remove Karma. The function accounts for iKarmaMax and iKarmaMin game settings, meaning that the resulting Karma will not go over or under their values. | Functions (GECK); Player Functions; Karma Functions
RewardKarmaAlt | JohnnyGuitar | RewardKarmaAlt(value:int) | Same as vanilla RewardKarma except it will not display a corner message or play a sound when used. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Player Functions; Karma Functions

Uses script variables as inputs and outputs. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)

RunBatchScript | JIP | reference.RunBatchScript(filePath:string) -> success:0/1 | Executes the 'script source' in the specified file. *The source file path should be relative to the game's <u>root folder</u> (..\Fallout New Vegas\). *The script is executed immediately; The calling script will be halted until the invoked script is processed. *The script source should follow the same structure rules as 'Result Scripts' (of quest stages, dialogue topics, etc.), and may include local 'variables' and 'if/else' statements. *When using forms/references in the script source, the 'Form ID' must be specified (<u>not</u> the 'Editor ID'), enclosed in double quotation marks. Example: <pre>ref rItem set rItem to "00020420" ; ArmorCombat AddItem "00E3778" 1 1 ; WeapNV9mmPistol if "0010D8DF".GetPlayerTeammate ; RexREF endif</pre> *If the function is 'called on a reference' (implicitly/explicitly), the script will be called on that 'same reference' (passed as "this" and returned by 'GetSelf'). Otherwise, it will be called on 'the player' (see examples, below). *If the source file was successfuly opened and read, and the script was compiled and executed with no errors, the function returns 1. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script Functions
RunScript | NVSE | RunScript(Script:Script-or-ScriptedForm) -> bool | Runs a script, which may be passed literally or attached to a form. This allows scripts to be run without a reference. <br>Returns true or false depending on what the script returns. *Prior to xNVSE 6.2.9, this function did not return a value, as it instead printed its result in console. | Functions_(NVSE); Script Functions
RunScriptSnippet | JIP | reference.RunScriptSnippet(delayTime:int, scriptSource:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) | Constructs, compiles and runs the script source in the formatted string. *The script is executed immediately or, if the '<i>delayTime</i>' argument is non-zero, is deferred for the specified number of frames. *The script should follow the same structure rules as 'Result Scripts' (of quest stages, dialogue topics, etc.), and may include local 'variables' and 'if/else' statements. *If the function is 'called on a reference' (implicitly/explicitly), the script will be called on that 'same reference' (passed as "this" and returned by 'GetSelf'). Otherwise, it will be called on 'the player' (see examples, below). *When used skillfully, this function can generate and run fully customizable scripts. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script_Functions

SaveCachedIniFile | ShowOff | SaveCachedIniFile(iniPath:string?) -> bool (alias: SaveCachedIni) | Saves (writes) the contents of a cached INI file to the actual file. This recreates it entirely, and as such can be a bit costly to do constantly; one should only call this function after finishing consecutive changes. 'Details on caching INI files:' *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

The function returns 0, so setting a variable equal to the return value will not do anything. To set up an action for after the line is spoken, see SayToDone, which is a block that is triggered when the SAYTO is complete. | Functions (GECK); Dialogue Functions; Actor Functions

ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds | GECK1 | ScriptEffectElapsedSeconds() -> float | Available only in Effect Scripts. Returns seconds elapsed since the last magic effect update. ScriptWait | JIP | ScriptWait(waitTime:int) | Delays the processing of the script code immediately following this function call, in the current 'BEGIN' block. While waiting, the script will skip processing the waiting 'BEGIN' block (the rest of the script will be processed normally); When the time expires, the script will continue from the wait point. *The 'waitTime' is represented in 1 wait unit = 1 processing cycle, which is once every frame (60 times/second) for Object-type scripts, and determined by the 'Script Processing Delay' setting for Quest-type scripts. *Any running script may only have one <u>active</u> waiting block at one time; calling this function from a 'BEGIN' block while another block is waiting will stop the latter from waiting. *This function cannot be used in Effect-type scripts, UDF scripts, or result scripts. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script_Functions
search | LU | search(name:string, type:int?) | Outputs information about all forms that match the provided string to the console. (This is intended to partially emulate the way the 'help' command works in the Skyrim console.) * The 'optional' type parameter filters output by Form Type IDs. * The type parameter can be the four character code of the type, as output in the Search command (AMMO, ARMO, NPC_, etc.) * Use quotation marks around strings that contain a space. | Functions_(JIP); Functions_(Command Extender); Console_Functions; Debugging

SetActorsAI | GECK1 | [actor:ref].SetActorsAI(processAI:bool) | 'SetActorsAI ' turns on and off the processing of an actor in the world. See Stats List for a list of stats that can be accessed using SetActorValue. See ActorValue to understand how Actor Values are calculated. | Functions (GECK); Actor Value Functions
SetActorValueAlt | JohnnyGuitar | Actor.SetActorValueAlt(ActorValueCode:int, Value:int) (alias: SetAVAlt) | SetActorValueAlt sets the stat's base value to the new value using a numeric Actor Value code instead of a Stat name. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Actor Value Functions
SetActorVelocity | JIP | reference.SetActorVelocity(axis:X/Y/Z, velocity:float) | Sets the calling actor's current 'Velocity' vector in the specified 'Axis'. *The change applies only to the current frame. *The sign of the velocity value indicates direction: A negative Z velocity equals speeding downwards. *Actors on the ground may be impossible to move horizontally, due to friction. *'Gravity' is also applied to the actor: you must supply a velocity equal to at least (GetLocalGravity '<i>axis</i>') in order to cancel it out. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Movement Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
SetActorVoiceType | JIP | reference.SetActorVoiceType(newVoice:ref, baseForm:ref?) -> voiceType:ref | Sets an actor's <i>Voice Type</i>. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor_Functions

SetAlert | GECK1 | actor.SetAlert(value:bool) | Sets whether the actor is in an "alert" state (weapon drawn). This is purely an animation state, and does not affect their actual combat status. | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Actor Functions; Savebaked Functions
SetAlly | GECK1 | SetAlly(faction1:baseform, faction2:baseform, F1toF2Flag:int?, F2toF1Flag:int?) | Sets a friend/ally relationship between two factions. It is not called on a reference. Will add the second argument faction to the first argument faction's reaction list if it is absent from the list. | Functions (GECK); Faction Functions

SetAmmoName | ShowOff | ammoRefr.SetAmmoName(newName:string, nameTypeToChange:int, form:baseform) | Sets the name of the Ammo BaseForm. If a reference is passed, its BaseForm will be modified. The nameTypeToChange arg allows specifying which type of name to change for the ammo: <pre> FullName = 0, ShortName = 1, AbbreviationName = 2 </pre> | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Inventory Functions; Name Functions

SetAngle | GECK1 | [Object].SetAngle(Axis:char, Angle:float) | Sets the angle ('in degrees') of the calling object relative to the world on the specified axis (X, Y, or Z). | Functions (GECK); Movement Functions
SetAngleEx | JIP | reference.SetAngleEx(angleX:float, angleY:float, angleZ:float, method:int?) | Modifies the XYZ rotation angles ('in degrees') of the calling reference. This function sets the angles of all three axes, and is faster (and possibly less glitchy) than calling SetAngle for each axis, individually. *The 4th argument, '<i>method</i>', is <u>optional</u>, and can be used for specifying how the rotation is modified: <pre>0&#09;(Default) Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation. 1&#09;Set the INTRINSIC (local) rotation. 2&#09;Rotate the INTRINSIC (local) rotation by the specified XYZ values. 3&#09;Rotate the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation by the specified XYZ values. 4&#09;Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, such that the reference will face the specified XYZ (given as COORDINATES - not angles!).</pre> | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
SetAngularVelocity | JIP | reference.SetAngularVelocity(nodeName:string, axis:X/Y/Z, velocity:float) (alias: SetAngVel) | Sets the angular velocity, on the specified 'Axis', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '2&pi; rad/s' (essentially, 'revolutions/second'). | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
SetAngularVelocityEx | JIP | reference.SetAngularVelocityEx(nodeName:string, velocityX:float, velocityY:float, velocityZ:float, setGlobal:0/1?) -> success:0/1 | Sets the angular velocity of each of the 3 X/Y/Z axes, of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *By default, the function sets the velocities relative to the object's 'local axes', unless the 'optional' 5th argument, '<i>setGlobal</i>', is non-zero, on which case the velocities are set relative to the world, 'global axes'. *The velocity is in units of '2&pi; rad/s' (essentially, 'revolutions/second'). *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions
SetAnimAdditive | kNVSE | actor.SetAnimAdditive(additive anim path:string, first person:0/1?, reference pose anim path:string?, reference pose time point:float?) | Sets an additive animation for an actor, providing a reference pose animation and an optional time point for blending. This function is used to apply a secondary animation on top of an actor's base animation. Returns 1 if the additive animation is successfully applied, and 0 otherwise. * The argument 'first person' controls whether the animation should apply to the first-person model (if applicable). If not specified and the actor is the player, the player's current perspective is used. * The argument 'reference pose anim path' allows specifying a reference pose animation to blend from. If not provided, a default idle animation will be used. * 'reference pose time point' specifies a time point within the reference pose animation to blend from, allowing fine control over the blending process. Defaults to 0.0 if not specified. To play the anim use ActivateAnim or any other of kNVSE's low level animation functions. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
SetAnimCurrentTime | kNVSE | reference.SetAnimCurrentTime(anim sequence path:string, fCurrentTime:float) | Sets an current time of the specified animation sequence. The current time allows you to control the time for the animation, effectively shifting it forward or backward in time. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
SetAnimObjectIdle | LU | SetAnimObjectIdle(animObject:form, idleAnim:form) (alias: SetANIOIdle) | Sets the 'Idle Animation' of the specified 'Anim Object'. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Animation Functions
SetAnimSequenceFrequency | JIP | reference.SetAnimSequenceFrequency(sequenceName:string, frequency:float) (alias: SetAnimSeqFreq) | Sets the 'Frequency' (animation play speed) of the specified 'NiControllerSequence' in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *For actor animations, including idle animations, the sequence name should be a path to the animation's .kf file, including the 'Meshes\' sub-folder. *Passing "*" as the '<i>sequenceName</i>' argument will set the frequency of '<u>all</u>' the sequences to the specified value. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Animation Functions; Model Functions; File Functions
SetAnimTextKeys | kNVSE | reference.SetAnimTextKeys(anim path:string, text key times:array, text key values:array) | Sets text keys for the specified animation sequence. Text keys allow you to define specific points in the animation timeline and associate text values with those points. These text values can be used for synchronization, labeling, or other purposes. Requires a reference if called after the game is loaded for the first time. If called from the main menu before the game is loaded, all actors using this animation will inherit the text keys from the animation. This function does nothing if the actor is currently playing the animation. To get the current text keys and values, use GetAnimationAttributes. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
SetAnimWeight | kNVSE | actor.SetAnimWeight(anim sequence path:string, weight:float) | Sets the weight of the specified animation sequence. The weight affects the influence or contribution of the animation sequence to the overall animation blend. A higher weight value means the animation has a stronger influence on the character's animation. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
SetArmorArmorRating | NVSE | reference.SetArmorArmorRating(newDR:int, Armor:form?) -> none (alias: SetArmorAR) | Sets the Damage Resistance (Armor Rating) of the specified armor BaseForm.<br> The newDR argument is 100x the value of the effective DR. Example: to set the armor to 20 DR, the newDR int must be 2000. If called on an armor reference, will modify that armor's BaseForm. | Functions_(NVSE); Functions_(Command Extender); Armor Functions
SetArmorAudioTemplate | JIP | SetArmorAudioTemplate(armorForm:ref, audioTemplate:ref?) | Sets the 'Armor Form' used as an 'Audio Template' by the specified 'Armor Form'. If the second argument is omitted, the audio template will be set to <i>null</i>. | Functions (JIP); Armor Functions (JIP); Armor Functions
SetArmorClass | JIP | SetArmorClass(armor:form, class:int) | Sets the 'armor class' of the specified 'Armor' form. | Functions (JIP); Armor Functions (JIP); Armor Functions
SetArmorConditionPenalty | JIP | SetArmorConditionPenalty(multiplier:float?) -> multiplier:float | Overrides the hardcoded 'Multiplier' (1.0, by default) defining the rate and maximum penalty to armor DT/DR when item condition percent falls below the 50% threshold. Above the 50% threshold, this formula is ignored and the 'PenaltyMult' is just 1.0. The formula: <pre>PenaltyMult = 1.0 - (0.5 - CondPercent[0, 1.0]) * Multiplier</pre> *The function may be called with the argument omitted, in which case it will return the current value of the multiplier without modifying it. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Armor Functions (JIP); Armor Functions
SetArmorDT | NVSE | reference.SetArmorDT(DT:float, Armor:form?) -> none | Sets the damage threshold of the specified armor or calling reference. | Functions_(NVSE); Armor Functions

If no BaseForm is specified, will instead set the attack damage of the calling reference's Base Form. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Weapon Functions

The '<i>buyOrSell</i>' argument determines which modifier is set: *0 - Bought items (must be >= 1.0) *1 - Sold items (must be <= 1.0) | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions

SetConditionDamagePenalty | JIP | SetConditionDamagePenalty(multiplier:float) | Overrides the hardcoded 'Multiplier' (0.67, by default) defining the rate and maximum penalty to weapon damage when item condition percent falls below the 75% threshold. Above the 75% threshold, this formula is ignored and the 'PenaltyMult' is just 1.0. The formula: <pre>PenaltyMult = 1.0 - (0.75 - CondPercent[0, 1.0]) * Multiplier</pre> | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Weapon Functions_(JIP); Weapon Functions
SetConsoleEcho | NVSE | SetConsoleEcho(EchoState:bool) -> PreviousEchoState:bool | Toggles whether the engine echoes to the console and returns the previous state. This is essentially a script version of Verbose. | Functions_(NVSE)

This function, along with the 'fDamageToArmorPercentage' GMST, can be used for tweaking and balancing armor condition damage. *This setting is saved in the game. *The function may be called with the argument omitted, in which case it will return the setting's current value, without modifying it. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Armor Functions (JIP); Armor Functions
