SetEquippedData | LU | reference.SetEquippedData(equippedData:array_var, noUnequip:0/1?, noMessage:0/1?) (alias: SetEqData) | Equips an item on the calling actor using an 'Equipped Data array_var' returned by GetEquippedData. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Array Functions
SetEquipType | JohnnyGuitar | SetEquipType(item:ref, equipType:int) | Sets the Equip Type of a BaseForm. If item is passed as a reference, will derive its BaseForm. | Functions_(JohnnyGuitar NVSE)
SetEventHandler | NVSE | SetEventHandler(eventID:string, functionScript:form, filters(up to 2):pair?, priority:pair?) -> success:bool | Registers a User-Defined Function (UDF) as an Event Handler for the specified event. This means that whenever the specified event occurs in-game, if a UDF was set as an Event Handler for that event, the UDF's script will run, allowing for instant reactions to certain events. Note that Event Handlers only stay set for the current session, and are not tied to saves in any way. This means that in most cases, it should be reset each time a new session starts. See GetGameRestarted for further details on sessions and how new ones can be detected. To track if an event has been set for the session, consider using a Temporary Auxiliary Variable, which is also tied to the session. | Functions_(NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnButtonDown:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnButtonDown:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) -> int | Event block type that calls the UDF when the gamepad button is pressed. The pressed button code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any Button code after the ":" delimiter. Button code int can be a bitmask of more than one button. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnButtonUp:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnButtonUp:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) -> int | Event block type that calls the UDF when the gamepad button is released. The released button code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any Button code after the ":" delimiter. Button code int can be a bitmask of more than one button. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnControlDown:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnControlDown:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) | Event block type that calls the UDF when the control is pressed, <u>even if the control has been disabled with DisableControl</u>. The pressed control code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any control code after the ":" delimiter. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnControlUp:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnControlUp:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) | Event block type that calls the UDF when the control is released. The released control code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any control code after the ":" delimiter. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOff"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) -> ref | Event block type that calls the UDF when the current ref is no longer in the crosshair. Crosshair (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or list, or Form Type. OnCrosshairOn and OnCrosshairOff can also be filtered by form type via "OnCrosshairOff:25" etc. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers; Crosshair Functions
SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnCrosshairOn"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) | Event block type that calls the UDF when the a new ref is found in the crosshair. Crosshair (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or list. Can also be filtered by Form Type via "OnCrosshairOn:25" etc. This event functions similarly to the GetCrosshairRef function - it only tracks refs within activation distance. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers; Crosshair Functions
SetEventHandler "OnKeyDown:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnKeyDown:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) | Event block type that calls the UDF when the key is pressed. The pressed key code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any DirectX scancode after the ":" delimiter. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnKeyUp:int" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnKeyUp:int"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) | Event block type that calls the UDF when the key is released. The released key code (int) is passed to the UDF. Use any DirectX scancode after the ":" delimiter. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnPlayerGrab"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) -> ref | Event block type that calls the UDF when the when the player picks up (Control Code 27 ) the form or form in the formlist. Grabbed (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or formlist. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Player Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease" | LU | SetEventHandler "OnPlayerRelease"(myUDF:userDefinedFunction) -> ref | Event block type that calls the UDF when the when the player releases (drops the grabbed ref) (Control Code 27 ) the form or form in the formlist. Released (ref) is passed to the UDF. Can be filtered with a form or formlist. | Functions_(JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Input Functions; Player Functions; Event Handlers
SetEventHandlerAlt | NVSE | SetEventHandlerAlt(eventName:string, handlerFunction:[[UDF]], filters(up-to-16):pair?, priority:pair?) -> success:bool | Allows up to 16 value filters of any type (numeric, form, string, array), as well as array-of-filters filters. Also allows specifying the event handler's priority amongst other handlers, more information in the 'Priority System' section. Details on how filter types are handled are available here: New xNVSE Event Filtering System. 'CAUTION:' <u>'All' event handlers registered with this function will be 'cleared/flushed on load'.</u> <u>LN events CANNOT be used with this function, since xNVSE does not directly manage those events</u>. For example, OnCellEnter is an invalid event to use for this function. Each filter arg is a pair. In this case, the numeric index of the nth argument to filter is paired with the actual filter. Example: <pre> 1::"I am a string filter" </pre> Assuming that the first arg dispatched is a string, then this will filter that arg; if the two strings match, then this event handler is dispatched, otherwise it isn't. Index 0 is a special case; it is reserved to filter the calling reference, also known as what is returned by GetSelf / GetSelfAlt. The other 15 value filters (indexes 1-15) are for filtering the args that are passed. Lastly, a priority value can be specified, see the 'Priority System' section for more information. Multiple event handlers can be set for the same script UDF, though one should avoid having redundant handlers, otherwise the handlerFunction could be called multiple times for the same event dispatch. To remove these handlers, use RemoveEventHandler, which now supports numeric-type arg indexes. Like SetEventHandler, this works with 'User-Defined Events', which can be dispatched via DispatchEvent and DispatchEventAlt. | Functions (NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions; Event Handlers
SetExcludedCombatActions | JIP | actorRef.SetExcludedCombatActions(excludeMask:int) | Excludes specific combat-AI action types from being performed by the calling actor ref, when in combat. The function takes a single argument, which is a 'bitmask' composed of up to 27 optional bits (see table below). A bit set to '1' will exclude the associated combat action from being performed. *For clarity and convenience, it is recommended to use a 'Binary Notation' for the bitmask argument. ==Combat Action Bits== <pre>Bit #&#09;Action -----&#09;------ 0&#09;ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE 1&#09;ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE_FROM_COVER 2&#09;ATTACK_RANGED 3&#09;ATTACK_RANGED_FROM_COVER 4&#09;ATTACK_GRENADE 5&#09;ATTACK_GRENADE_FLUSH_TARGET 6&#09;ATTACK_GRENADE_FROM_COVER 7&#09;ATTACK_MELEE 8&#09;ATTACK_HAND_TO_HAND 9&#09;MOVE 10&#09;MOVE_AND_SWITCH_TO_MELEE 11&#09;MOVE_AND_ATTACK_RANGED 12&#09;MOVE_AND_ATTACK_RANGED_EXPLOSIVE 13&#09;MOVE_AND_ATTACK_GRENADE 14&#09;DRAW_WEAPON 15&#09;SWITCH_WEAPON 16&#09;AVOID_THREAT 17&#09;SEARCH 18&#09;INVESTIGATE 19&#09;DODGE 20&#09;IGNORE_BLOCKED_TARGET 21&#09;FLEE 22&#09;ACTIVATE_COMBAT_ITEM 23&#09;USE_COMBAT_ITEM 24&#09;ACQUIRE_LINE_OF_SIGHT 25&#09;HIDE 26&#09;APPROACH_TARGET</pre> | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions
SetExplosionFlag | JIP | SetExplosionFlag(explosion:ref, flagID:int, newValue:0/1) -> flagValue:0/1 | Sets an explosion's flag value (determined by <i>flagID</i>). | Functions_(JIP); Explosion Functions (JIP); Flag Functions

Whoever is hit by the Explosion will consider that reference as their attacker. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Explosion Functions

SetForceDrawHitscanProjectiles | ShowOff | SetForceDrawHitscanProjectiles(forceDraw:int?) -> 1/0 | If forceDraw is set to 1, forces hitscan projectiles to be rendered, similar to how they are in VATS. If forceDraw is set to 0, will go back to default vanilla behavior. If the forceDraw arg is omitted or set to -1, will do nothing. This is useful for just checking if hitscan projectiles are forced to be drawn. Returns if hitscan projectiles were being forced to be drawn. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Projectile Functions

SetFormFlag | LU | SetFormFlag(form:form, flag:int, value:bool) | Sets a flag on a form. A flag can function differently depending on if it is active in a Reference or in a BaseForm. | Functions (JIP); Flag Functions; Functions (FO3); Functions (ButcherPete FOSE)
SetFullNameAlt | JIP | reference.SetFullNameAlt(newName:string, baseForm:ref?) (alias: SetActorFullNameAlt) | An alternate version of the vanilla functions SetActorFullName and SetCellFullName. Takes a 'String', instead of a 'Message' form, as argument. The name change is <u>permanent</u> and is saved in the game. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Name Functions_(JIP); Actor_Functions; Name Functions; String Functions; Savebaked Functions
SetFunctionValue | NVSE | SetFunctionValue(UDFReturnValue:multi) -> None | Used only within the 'Function' block type of a User Defined Function, sets the return value to that specified. A UDF may return only a single value, but it may be of any type, including an array. If the function is called multiple times in the same function, the most recent is used by the call; this function is not a substitute for the keyword 'return', script execution will continue to the end of the block as normal. In another script, to bind the UDF function value to a variable, Let must be used rather than <b>set</b>, and to use in a conditional, if eval rather than just <b>if</b>. | Functions_(NVSE); User Defined Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
SetGameDifficulty | JIP | SetGameDifficulty(difficulty:int) | Sets the current difficulty-level of the game, ranging from 0 for 'Very Easy', to 4 for 'Very Hard'. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Functions_(Command Extender)
SetGameHour | JIP | SetGameHour(gameHour:float) | Provides a safe and convenient way to set the 'GameHour' global and/or advance game time, without the risk of corrupting the other game time globals. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Time Functions; Functions_(Command Extender)
SetGameMainLoopCallback | JIP | reference.SetGameMainLoopCallback(callbackScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, callDelay:int?, modeFlag:int?) (alias: SGMLC) | Registers/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a callback that will be repeatedly and independently called by the game, with the specified delay between calls. The function takes four arguments: *1. 'callbackScript' - A UDF script. The script should take <u>no arguments</u> (empty braces &#123;&#125;). *2. 'setORremove' - (1) Register, or (0) un-register the callback. *3. 'callDelay' (optional) - The delay time between calls, in 'frames'. The default is 1 (<u>every</u> frame). *4. 'modeFlag' (optional) - A <u>bitmask</u>; enables to restrict the calls to a specific mode, etc. The default is 3. <pre>1&#09;Run on GameMode 2&#09;Run on MenuMode 8&#09;Auto-remove on load game/main menu</pre> If the function is 'called on a reference' (implicitly/explicitly), the UDF script, whenever called, will be called on that 'same reference' (passed as "<i>this</i>"). Otherwise, it will be called on 'the player' (see examples, below). | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Bit Functions; Event Handlers
SetGameVolume | JIP | SetGameVolume(volumeType:int, volumeLevel:int?) | Sets the level (0-100) of the specified game volume type. *The 2nd argument 'volumeLevel' is optional and may be omitted, in which case the specified volume type's level will be reset to the value defined in the game's INI file. | Functions (JIP); Sound Functions (JIP); Sound Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
SetGhost | GECK1 | [Actor].SetGhost(ghostState:0/1) | Sets the actor reference to/from the "ghost" state. The changes are save-persistent. | Functions (GECK); Actor State Functions
SetGlobalAnimTime | kNVSE | reference.SetGlobalAnimTime(timePassed:float, bFirstPerson:0/1?) | Sets the time passed value on the actor, which affects all animations they are currently playing. This function provides a way to control and manipulate the playback of animations. You can use it to pause, reverse, or alter the speed of animations, allowing for dynamic animation control to alter the playback of animations. | Functions (kNVSE); Animation Functions
SetGlobalRef | JIP | reference.SetGlobalRef(global:ref, anyForm:ref?) | Stores a 'reference'-type value in a 'Global' variable (which normally would accept only numeric values). In order to retrieve a reference from a global, GetGlobalRef must be used. | Functions (JIP); Global Variable Functions (JIP); Functions_(Command Extender)

SetINIInteger_Cached | ShowOff | SetINIInteger_Cached(sectionAndKey:string, newValue:int, iniPath:string?, comment:string?) -> int (alias: SetINIIntC) | Sets the integer value of an INI key. 'Only affects a cached version of the INI file!' To save the changes to the actual file, SaveCachedIniFile must be called. *If the file was not previously cached, then it becomes cached. *Caching eats memory, but allows faster access to the data. *Once the cache is no longer needed, ClearFileCacheShowOff should be called to free the memory. If an error has occured, will return -1. Otherwise, returns 1 if the key existed previously and was updated, or 2 if it was inserted. If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
SetINISection | LU | SetINISection(section:string, keysValuesArrayID:array, filename:string?) | Sets the values of multiple keys in an INI section using a stringmap array. JIP NVSE Plugin v40 merged Lutana NVSE plugin v19. 'As of JIPv40, This function's syntax was changed from Lutana v19, requiring that you pass the arrays via their IDs (set to integers) if you use the script compiler override (CO).' | Functions_(JIP); INI_Functions_(JIP); File Functions; INI Functions; Array Functions

'Only affects a cached version of the INI file!' If an error has occured, will return -1. Otherwise, returns 1 if the key existed previously and was updated, or 2 if it was inserted. If the value is created and comment is not an empty string, will add the comment above the created key:value pair. | File Functions; INI Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Cached File Functions (ShowOff NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions

When interaction is disabled, the player will not be able to "activate" the object, and no rollover-text will be shown when the object is pointed at with the crosshair. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP)

This will signal the game to not render the object's 3D model, which will therefore not be visible. *By omitting the 2nd argument, the function can be used to return whether the specified object is an internal marker, without making any changes. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)

SetLevel | GECK1 | [actor:ref].SetLevel(newLevel:int, levelToPCFlag:int, minLevel:int?, maxLevel:int?) | In theory, if the LevelToPCFlag is set to 1 then the level provided is an offset to the player's level, which will not take their level above or below the provided maximum and minimum levels. In practice, the function does not work correctly when called on NPCs- SetLevel will often (if not always) set their level to the provided MinLevel, disregarding any level offset, maximum or anything else. This function can be called on the player and will change the player's level, but it will not change not any of his stats (and the world will not "level up" with the player), so this is not recommended. Use RewardXP instead. | Functions (GECK); Actor State Functions

SetLevelUpMenuCanExitEarly | ShowOff | SetLevelUpMenuCanExitEarly(canExitEarly:int(1/0)) -> 1/0 | If passing 1 to the function, the level up menu can be exited early without requiring all points in the current page to be allotted. Returns 1 for success (level up menu was open), 0 otherwise. 'NOTE:' Trying to revert the change by calling this function with canExitEarly = 0 does not currently work, though this usually isn't desired anyways. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); UI Functions

SetLinearVelocity | JIP | reference.SetLinearVelocity(nodeName:string, velX:float, velY:float, velZ:float, setLocal:0/1?) | Sets the linear velocity on each of the 3 X/Y/Z 'global axes', of the collision object (if any) attached to the specified node in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The velocity is in units of '0.1 Meter/sec', or '7 game units/sec'. *If the 'optional' 5th argument, '<i>setLocal</i>', is non-zero, the function sets the velocities relative to the object's 'local axes'. *If the 3D model does not have the specified node, or the node does not have a mobile collision object, the function returns 0. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Model Functions

SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB | LU | SetLoadScreenTypeTextRGB(loadScreenType:baseform, red:float, green:float, blue:float) | Sets the color values of text in a loading screen type form. | Functions_(JIP); LoadScreen_Functions_(JIP); UI_Functions_(JIP); UI Functions; Loading Screen Functions
SetLocalGravityVector | JIP | SetLocalGravityVector(xGravity:float, yGravity:float, zGravity:float) | Sets the X, Y, and Z components of local acceleration (in Game Units per second squared) due to 'gravity' for the current cell. X and Y accelerations in the game are always zero, by default. Modifying them to non-zero values may, theoretically, be used to cause gravity to act in a non-vertical direction. Negative values are the norm, causing objects to accelerate downward, but positive values are also acceptable. Changing the gravity for an exterior cell changes it for <u>all exterior cells</u> until it is reset by another call to this function. Changes to gravity in an interior cell affect <u>only that cell</u>, and the change will be reset once the cell is reloaded. | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Functions_(Command Extender)
SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly | GECK1 | SetLocationSpecificLoadScreensOnly(flag:int) | This puts the game into a state where only load screens specifically associated with the current cell will be displayed. | Functions (GECK); UI Functions; Loading Screen Functions

Doors, Containers, and Terminals can be locked. | Lock_Functions; Lock_Functions_(JIP); Functions_(JIP); Misc_Reference_Functions_(JIP)

SetMaxAniso | GECK1 | SetMaxAniso(FilterLevel:int) | Sets Anisotropic Filtering level. Acceptable values are: 1, 2, 4, 8, 12 and 16. Other values will get clamped to the nearest lower value. | Functions (GECK)

SetMessageDisabled | JIP | SetMessageDisabled(messageOrList:ref, disable:0/1) | Disables/enables the specified 'Message' form. A disabled message will never be displayed by the game. Either a single 'Message' or a 'Form List' may be passed as the first parameter (in the latter case, all messages contained in the list will be disabled/enabled). | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Message Functions

SetModelPath | NVSE | reference.SetModelPath(NewPath:string, form:baseForm?) -> 1/0 | Sets the model of the specified BaseForm. *If a calling reference is passed, then its BaseForm will be used instead. *This change is NOT savebaked. *The file path is relative to '..\Data\Meshes\'. *If the replaced model is already loaded, then it will need to be reloaded via functions like ReloadEquippedModels or Update3D. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; File Functions; Model Functions
SetModLocalData | NVSE | |arguments = {{FunctionArgument.SetModLocalData(Key:string, Data:multi) -> Success:bool | Sets the Mod Local Data value associated with the specified key for the mod to which the calling script belongs. The key must be a string; the data can be a string, number or object, but not an array. Keys are case-insensitive and unique - only one data value can be associated with a particular key. Returns true if the function succeeds, although it should not necessary to check this. | Functions_(NVSE); NVSE-Aware Functions
SetMoonTexture | JIP | SetMoonTexture(moonType:int, newPath:string) | Sets the texture path of the 'Moon', specified by <i>moonType</i>: <pre>0&#09;Full 1&#09;Three Wan 2&#09;Half Wan 3&#09;One Wan 4&#09;No Moon 5&#09;One Wax 6&#09;Half Wax 7&#09;Three Wax</pre> | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Texture Functions
SetMusicState | JIP | SetMusicState(musicState:int) | Sets the playback state of the game's current background music: <pre>0&#09;Stop Playback 1&#09;Pause Playback 2&#09;Resume Playback</pre> *'Note:' The 'Stop Playback' state will stop music played by PlayMusic, otherwise it will replay the current music from the beginning. | Functions (JIP); Sound Functions (JIP); Sound Functions; Music

SetNifBlockFlag | JIP | reference.SetNifBlockFlag(blockName:string, flagID:int, doSet:0/1, pcModel:1/2?) (alias: SetNifFlag) | Sets/Un-sets the specified flag on the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 4th argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> You can see available flags in NIF NiAVObject Flags | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Flag Functions
SetNifBlockRotation | JIP | reference.SetNifBlockRotation(blockName:string, rotX:float, rotY:float, rotZ:float, method:int?, pcModel:1/2?) (alias: SetNifRot) | Modifies the XYZ rotation (pitch, roll and yaw) angles (in degrees) of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 5th argument, '<i>method</i>', determines how the rotation is modified: <pre>0&#09;(Default) Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to parent node. 1&#09;Rotate the INTRINSIC (local) rotation by the specified XYZ values. 2&#09;Set the INTRINSIC (local) rotation, relative to parent node. 3&#09;(Reserved) 4&#09;Rotate the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation by the specified XYZ values. 5&#09;Set the EXTRINSIC (global) rotation, relative to the world, such that the block will face the specified XYZ (given as COORDINATES - not angles!).</pre> *The 6th argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions; Model Functions
SetNifBlockScale | JIP | reference.SetNifBlockScale(blockName:string, scale:float, pcModel:1/2?) (alias: SetNifScale) | Sets the scale of the specified NIF block in the calling reference's loaded 3D model. *The 3rd argument, <i>'pcModel'</i>, is <u>optional</u>, and may be used when calling the function on the player. The player character has two, completely separate 3D models for 1st and 3rd person. This argument enables to explicitly target one of the models. <pre>1&#09;3rd Person Model 2&#09;1st Person Model</pre> | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions; File Functions; Scale Functions

(alias: SetOnTimerStartHandler) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Auxiliary Timer owned by a specified form is (re)started. 'Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.' *Consider using GetGameLoaded to (re)set the event handler. If no form is specified, will assume PlayerRef. When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the AuxTimer string name, and the owner form. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Event Handlers; Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)

(alias: SetOnTimerStopHandler) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Auxiliary Timer owned by a specified form is stopped. 'Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.' *Consider using GetGameLoaded to (re)set the event handler. If no owner form is specified, will assume PlayerRef. When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the AuxTimer string name, and the owner form. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Event Handlers; Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)
SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler | ShowOff | ownerRefr.SetOnAuxTimerUpdateHandler(setORremove:1/0, handlerScript:ref, timerName:string, ownerForm:ref?) (alias: SetOnTimerUpdateHandler) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked when a specified Auxiliary Timer owned by a specified form is updated. This happens every frame that it is running. <u>'NOTE': this event will only run if the AuxTimer has the 'Run OnTimerUpdate Event' flag enabled; see AuxTimerStart.</u> 'Handlers will be removed when the game is loaded or sent to the Main Menu.' *Consider using GetGameLoaded to (re)set the event handler. If no form is specified, will assume PlayerRef as the AuxTimer owner. When invoked, the script is passed with three arguments: the AuxTimer string name, the owner form, and the time passed since the last update (float). *The time passed will always be 1 for frame-based timers, otherwise it will be a delta in seconds. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Auxiliary-Timer Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Event Handlers; Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE)

When invoked, the script is passed with the 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"), and a single argument: an integer indicating which limb was crippled: <pre>0&#09;Head 1&#09;Torso 2&#09;Left Hand 3&#09;Right Hand 4&#09;Left Leg 5&#09;Right Leg 6&#09;Brain</pre> | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Actor_Functions; Actor Value Functions; Event Handlers
SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler | JIP | SetOnCriticalHitEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, target:ref?, source:ref?, weapon:ref?) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event an 'Actor' is struck by a 'Critical Hit'. Any combination of the three optional arguments may be used for filtering events: *'<i>target</i>' - The target 'Actor' (taking the hit). *'<i>source</i>' - The source 'Reference' (striking the hit). *'<i>weapon</i>' - The 'Weapon' dealing the hit. 'Note:' Due to GECK limitations, the function cannot accept '0'-valued arguments. As a workaround, when required, pass 'XMarker' to indicate a filter should be ignored (see examples, below). When invoked, the script is passed with the struck 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"), and two arguments: the source 'Reference' and the 'Weapon'. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnDialogTopicEventHandler | JIP | SetOnDialogTopicEventHandler(handlerScript:form, setORremove:1/0, topicOrInfo:form) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified 'Dialog Topic'/'Topic Info' is spoken by an actor. When invoked, the script is passed with the speaking 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"), and a single argument: the 'Topic' or the 'Info' (depending on what the event was set for) spoken. *When using a 'Topic Info' for the 3rd argument, use the hexadecimal 'Form ID' (Topic Infos have no Editor IDs). | Functions (JIP); Functions (JIP PP LN); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers

When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Marker reference traveled to, which may or may not be an actual Map Marker base object (hardcoded form ID 10). | Functions_(JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler | JIP | SetOnFireWeaponEventHandler(handlerScript:form, setORremove:1/0, actorOrList:form?) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified 'Actor' fires a round with a ranged-weapon. *The 3rd argument, '<i>actorOrList</i>', is <u>optional</u>. Either a single reference, or a 'FormList' of references may be passed. If omitted, the script will be invoked when 'ANY' actor fires a weapon. When invoked, the script is passed with the 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"), and a single argument: the 'Weapon' (base form) that was fired. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Actor_Functions; Weapon Functions; Event Handlers
SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler | JIP | SetOnHealthDamageEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, actorOrList:ref) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified 'Actor' takes Health damage. When invoked, the script is passed with the 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"), and two arguments: the amount of damage taken and, if the damage resulted from being hit by another actor, the reference of that actor, otherwise (if the damage resulted from falling, for example) 0. The amount of damage taken is a negative value. | Functions_(JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Actor_Functions; Actor Value Functions; Event Handlers

The optional argument may use an 'Actor' or 'Form List' for filtering events: *'<i>target</i>' - The target 'Actor' (taking the hit). *'<i>target</i>' - The target 'Form List' (taking the hit). When invoked, the script is passed with the struck 'Actor' as the calling reference ("<i>this</i>"). | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers

SetOnMenuClickEventHandler | JIP | SetOnMenuClickEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, tilePath:string) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified 'UI Tile' is clicked/pressed. The 'tilePath' argument may specify a menu name and target a specific tile by its '<id>' trait: <pre>"InventoryMenu#8"</pre> Note the menu name, immediately followed by a '#' symbol, then followed by a number matching the '<id>' trait of the tile. This is equivalent to "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_MainRect/IM_ButtonRect/IM_RepairButton". The 'tilePath' argument may be the full-path of a tile, such as: <pre>"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Aid" (clicking on Aid tab) "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Apparel" (clicking on Apparel tab) "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline/Weapons" (clicking on Weapons tab) "InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_MainRect/IM_InventoryList/IM_InventoryListTemplateRect" (clicking on any inventory item)</pre> In the above cases the handler will be invoked only when the specific tile is clicked. It may be a partial path, such as: <pre>"InventoryMenu/GLOW_BRANCH/IM_Tabline"</pre> In which case the handler will be invoked when ANY tile beginning with that path is clicked. Note that the path, either full or partial, must begin with a valid menu name. When invoked, the script is passed with three arguments: *'(int) Menu Mode ID' - 1002/1009/1075 etc. *'(int) Tile ID' - Only for tiles with a <id> property. Otherwise -1 is passed. *'(string_var) Tile Name' - Without the full-path, and <u>in lower-case</u> (for example: "apparel"). For template ListBox tiles, the name is suffixed with the tile's index in the list (for example: "stats_list_template_item:26") | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler | JIP | SetOnMenuCloseEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, menuID:int?) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified MenuMode is closed (MUST be a Specific menu, NOT a Menu Type). If the <i>menuID</i> parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked when ANY menu mode closes. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler | JIP | SetOnMenuOpenEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, menuID:int?) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the specified MenuMode is opened (MUST be a Specific menu, NOT a Menu Type). If the <i>menuID</i> parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked when ANY menu mode opens. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler | JIP | SetOnMouseoverChangeEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0, menuID:int?) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event the mouseover-ed, or highlighted selection in the specified 'Menu Mode', is changed. If the <i>menuID</i> parameter is omitted, the script will be invoked in ANY menu mode. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the Menu Mode ID. | Functions_(JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Event Handlers (JIP); Event Handlers
SetOnNoteAddedEventHandler | JIP | SetOnNoteAddedEventHandler(handlerScript:ref, setORremove:1/0) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked in the event a new 'Note' is added to the player. When invoked, the script is passed with a single argument: the 'Note' form that was added. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Note Functions; Event Handlers

Can be filtered by a specified 'Emotion ID'. When invoked, the script is passed with five arguments: the response string, voiceline sound path, emotion id, emotion value, and response number. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Event Handlers (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Event Handlers; Dialogue Functions

The '<i>skipScript</i>' argument is optional, and if non-zero will effectively <u>prevent</u> the 'result script' (if any) of the specified stage from being executed. When invoked, the script is passed with two arguments: the 'Quest' and the 'Stage' index. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Event Handlers (JIP); Script_Functions; Quest Functions; Event Handlers

The actor will act and react as if he is in the player faction for the purposes of combat. Other actors will treat the actor as is he were in the player faction. SetPosEx | JIP | reference.SetPosEx(posX:float, posY:float, posZ:float, translate:0/1?) | Sets the XYZ position of the calling object reference. This function sets the position on all three axes, and is faster than calling SetPos for each axis, individually. It also ignores the FormType limitations imposed on 'SetPos'. *If the 4th, optional argument, '<i>translate</i>', is non-zero, instead of flat-setting the object's position, the function will move the object to the specified XYZ point, relative to its 'current position and local rotation'. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Movement Functions

SetProjectileTracerChanceOverride | ShowOff | SetProjectileTracerChanceOverride(chanceOverride:int?) -> int | Sets or removes an override determining when to draw projectile tracers. Possible values for chanceOverride: If -1, remove override (returns to default chance-based behavior. If 0, never draw tracers. If 1, always draw tracers if the base Projectile has a tracer chance > 0 to start with. Calling this function with no args or with a value of -2 or lower will produce no changes. Returns the status of the tracer chance override BEFORE the function was called. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Projectile Functions

SetPutdownSound | LU | SetPutdownSound(object:form, sound:form) | Sets the putdown sound of a form. | Functions_(JIP); Sound_Functions_(JIP); Sound_Functions
SetQuestDelay | GECK1 | SetQuestDelay(quest:baseform, questDelay:float) | SetQuestDelay modifies the processing time delay for a script. The change is save-baked. By default, quest scripts process every 5 seconds (this is based on an INI setting). This is to prevent the quest scripts from taking up an excessive amount of processor time. Unless you have a good reason, you should leave the quest script processing every 5 seconds. Good reasons include: *The quest script is running a timer which requires precise timing (e.g. for dialogue). *The quest script needs to react to something with precise timing (e.g. to trigger different parts of a scene). Even when you have a good reason, avoid setting the quest delay time to .1, or any other low number, for the duration of a quest. Under almost all circumstances, set the quest delay to a low number for a specific purpose, and when that purpose is finished, set it back to the default. | Functions (GECK); Quest Functions; Script Functions
SetQuestFlag | JIP | SetQuestFlag(quest:form, flagID:int, newValue:0/1) | Sets/un-sets the specified flag (determined by <i>flagID</i>) for the specified 'Quest' form. | Functions (JIP); Quest Functions (JIP); Quest Functions; Flag Functions
SetQuestItem | FOSE1 | ref.SetQuestItem(QuestFlag:bool, Item:Object?) -> none | Sets the quest item flag for the specified BaseForm. If only a calling reference is passed, its BaseForm is used. This change is save baked. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Quest Functions; Functions (FO3); Savebaked Functions
SetQuestObject | GECK1 | SetQuestObject(object:form, questObjectFlag:int) | Sets the "quest item" flag on an object or actor (1 or 0). When this flag is set to 1: * NPC/Creature -- has priority in updates when the player is changing process state. For instance, "quest" flagged NPCs will follow the player through load doors more quickly. Corpses will not be "cleaned up" when the cell expires. * Inventory items -- player cannot drop or sell items flagged as "quest items". | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Quest Functions
SetRace | LU | SetRace(NPC:form, race:form) | Sets the specified 'NPC' form's 'Race'. *Visual update of loaded references, via Update3D, is required. *This function may have unanticipated side effects. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions
SetRadiationExtraData | ShowOff | reference.SetRadiationExtraData(newVal:float) -> bool | Sets the reference's Radiation ExtraData, if it has any. If it has no Radiation ExtraData, it is added and created. The changes are savebaked. This data is mostly used for Radiation Markers, where it determines the amount of rads gained while standing in the marker's radius. | ExtraData Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

SetRadius | LU | reference.SetRadius(radius:float, light:baseform) | Sets the radius of a reference (any type) or Light BaseForm. If used on a reference, this will modify that reference's Radius ExtraData. If it does not exist, it is created. Note that it may not be saved with the reference, depending on the reference's type. | Functions_(JIP); Light_Functions_(JIP); Light_Functions; ExtraData Functions
SetRandomizerSeed | ShowOff | SetRandomizerSeed(seedValue:UInt?) (alias: SetSeed) | Sets the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Random_seed randomizer seed] (unsigned int) for the calling mod, and resets any progress made with that seed. If no seedValue is specified, or if it is set to 0, will set the seed to the 'default seed' (value of 5489). *'NOTE: since the default seed is constant, it will always generate the same sequence of random numbers.' For use with generating random numbers via RandSeeded / GetRandomPercentSeeded. Does not affect random number generation for any other function. | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

SetRefCount | NVSE | reference.SetRefCount(NewCount:int) | Sets the number of items in a stack of items in the world. Note that while this function will change the count of any type of reference, it may have unpredictable results when used on references like NPCs which are not logically stackable. | Functions_(NVSE); UI Functions
SetReferenceFlag | LU | [ref].SetReferenceFlag(flag:int, value:bool) (alias: SetRefFlag) | Sets a flag on a reference. | Functions_(JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Flag Functions
SetRefExtraData | JIP | reference.SetRefExtraData(slotIndex:int?, value:any?) | Sets the value stored at the specified slot index of the calling reference's Extra Data. *The value may be numeric, a form/reference, or a string. 'See table below for data-type restrictions per slot'. *Calling the function with both arguments omitted will remove 'all' extra-data from the calling reference. | Functions (JIP); Extra Data Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP)
SetRefName | JIP | reference.SetRefName(newName:string?) | Sets the name of the calling reference. Unlike SetName/SetNameEx/LNSetName, all of which change the name of <u>every</u> instance of the 'Base Form', this function assigns a name 'unique' <u>only</u> to the reference. *If the '<i>newName</i>' argument is omitted, the reference's name is reset to the default one. *To retrieve the unique name of a reference (if any), LNGetName should be used; GetName etc will only return the base form's name. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Name Functions_(JIP); Name Functions; String Functions
SetRefrModelPath | JIP | reference.SetRefrModelPath(nifPath:string?) | Sets the file path to the 'NIF model' used by the calling reference. Unlike similar functions, such as SetModelPathEx, the model is changed <u>for the calling reference only</u> and will not affect other instances of the same base form. *The file path is relative to '..\Data\Meshes\'. *Calling the function with the 'nifPath' argument omitted will reset the model to the one defined for the reference's base form. *A call to this function should be followed by a call to Update3D on the reference, in order to apply the changes. | Functions (JIP); Misc Reference Functions (JIP); Model Functions
SetRefVariable | NVSE | ref.SetRefVariable(VarName:string, NewValue:form, Parent:Object?) -> none | Sets the value of the 'ref' variable specified by name for the calling reference or specified scriptable object. | Functions_(NVSE); Script Functions
SetRepairList | FOSE1 | itemRefr.SetRepairList(repairFormList:baseform, itemForm:baseForm?) | Sets the repair list for the specified item. If itemForm is omitted and a calling reference is passed, will use that reference's BaseForm. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE)
SetRestrained | GECK1 | [actor:ref].SetRestrained(restrainedFlag:int) | Set the actor to a restrained state (or clear the state). Restrained actors will not move from their current position, or enter combat, but will continue to "think" (pick packages, run detection so they could yell alarms) and go into dialogue. | Functions (GECK); AI Functions

SetScale | GECK1 | reference.SetScale(scale:float) | Sets the scale of the calling object to the specified amount: 1.0 being "normal size", 2.0 being twice normal size, etc. This change is savebaked. | Functions (GECK); Scale Functions; Savebaked Functions

SetScript | FOSE1 | reference.SetScript(NewScript:baseForm, ScriptableObject:baseForm) -> previousScript:baseForm | Sets the specified Script onto the specified BaseForm, and returns any previous script. If ScriptableObject is omitted and a calling reference is provided, that reference's BaseForm is modified. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Script Functions
SetScriptDisabled | JIP | SetScriptDisabled(scriptOrList:ref, disable:0/1) | Disables/re-enables the specified 'Script' (or, alternatively, every script in the specified 'Form List'). *While disabled, the script will 'not' be processed by the game (including any and all block-types). *'Variables' defined in the script remain accessible, and may still be read/modified remotely, from another script. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script_Functions
SetScriptEventDisabled | JIP | SetScriptEventDisabled(anyFormOrList:ref, eventNames:string, disable:0/1) | Disables/re-enables the specified script-'Event Block' (i.e. <i>OnActivate</i>, <i>OnAdd</i>, <i>OnTrigger</i>, etc.) in the 'Script' (if any) of the specified form(s). *A disabled 'Event Block' will <u>not</u> run when its matching game-event occurs and gets registered in the containing 'Script'. There are three options for the first argument (<i>anyFormOrList</i>): *An 'Object Reference' - In which case, the effects will apply <u>only</u> on that one, specific reference. *A 'Base Form' - In which case, the effects will apply on <u>every</u> instance of that form in the world. *A 'Form List' of object references and/or base forms. The second argument (<i>eventNames</i>) is a string containing the event names. Multiple event names may be specified, separated by a space. | Functions (JIP); Script Functions (JIP); Script_Functions
SetSecuritronExpression | VEGAS1 | SetSecuritronExpression(Target:reference, Face:String, Expression:String) (alias: SetSecExp) | Used to set a Securitron reference's expression on their monitor. Requires a reference and two strings. | Functions (Vegas); Texture Functions; Savebaked Functions
SetSeedUsingForm | ShowOff | anyReference.SetSeedUsingForm(seedForm:anyForm?) (alias: SetFormSeed) | Sets the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Random_seed randomizer seed] (unsigned int) for the calling mod using the specified form's FormID, and resets any progress made with that seed. If no valid form is passed, will set the seed to the default seed (value of 5489). *'NOTE: since the default seed is constant, it will always generate the same sequence of random numbers.' For use with generating random numbers via RandSeeded / GetRandomPercentSeeded. Does not affect random number generation for any other function. | Math Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
SetServiceFlag | LU | SetServiceFlag(NPC:form, flagID:int, doSet:1/0) | Sets/un-sets the specified buy/sell/service flag for the specified 'NPC' form. | Functions (JIP); Actor Functions (JIP); Actor Functions; Flag Functions
SetShowOffDebugMode | ShowOff | SetShowOffDebugMode(isDebugEnabled:int) | If set to a non-0 value, toggles debug print messages for certain ShowOff NVSE functions. Not particularly useful for most modmakers; this exists more-so to aid in ShowOff's development. | Debugging; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)
SetShowOffOnCornerMessageEventHandler | ShowOff | SetShowOffOnCornerMessageEventHandler(setORremove:1/0, handlerScript:ref, flag:int?) (alias: SetOnCornerMessageEventHandler) | Sets/removes the specified 'UDF Script' as a handler that will be invoked whenever a corner message (like ones from MessageEx) 'is appended to the corner message queue'. For example, it runs when a message is added via MessageExAlt, which is not necessarily when it is displayed. When invoked, the script gets passed 5 arguments: #The message text. #The icon type, used for preset icon paths. Only used internally by the engine, otherwise left at 0. #The icon path. #The sound path (unknown when this is used, usually null). #The duration of the message. *If any of the strings are null, they are passed as '"<void>"' to the UDF. Unlike ShowOff:OnQueueCornerMessage, this will run even if the message being queued will be ignored and immediately removed from the queue. For example, if you have a Karma loss message currently displayed, then the game won't actually queue another one unless something else is in the queue, as it detects the redundancy and eliminates it. | Functions (ShowOff NVSE); Event Handlers (ShowOff NVSE); Event Handlers; Message Functions
SetShowQuestItems | GECK1 | SetShowQuestItems(showQuestItemsFlag:int) | This is used to show or hide quest items in the player's inventory (normally the player cannot drop or sell quest items). When flag = 1, quest items will be displayed in the player's inventory (default functionality). When flag = 0, quest items will be hidden. Obviously this function has to be used in very controlled circumstances to prevent the player from permanently "losing" all his quest items. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Quest Functions
SetSize | GECK1 | [actor:ref].SetSize(size:float) -> int (alias: CSize) | Shrink/Grow an actor. Sets the actor size to specified radius (0.0 resets the actor to its original radius). | Functions (GECK); Miscellaneous Functions

SetStageAlt | JIP | SetStageAlt(quest:ref, stage:int) | Works exactly as SetStage. The only difference is that this function will set the specified quest's highest completed stage (i.e. the value returned by GetStage) to the specified number, even if it is lower than the current stage. | Quest Functions; Functions (JIP); Quest Functions (JIP); Savebaked Functions

SetTerminalUIModel | JIP | SetTerminalUIModel(terminalOrList:ref, modelPath:string?) | Sets the UI art file that will be used for the specified 'Terminal' in the computers/hacking menu. *The first argument may be either a 'Terminal' form, or a 'Form List' of terminal forms. *If the second argument is omitted, the art file will be reset to the default one. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Model Functions; File Functions
SetTextInputExtendedProps | JIP | SetTextInputExtendedProps(offsetX:float, offsetY:float, minLength:int?, maxLength:int?, switches:int?) | Sets various extended properties of the currently active 'Text Input Menu'. The function takes up to five arguments: *'<i>offsetX</i>' - Override the horizontal (x) origin of the text input window to the specified value. If zero, use the default origin (center of the screen). *'<i>offsetY</i>' - Override the vertical (y) origin of the text input window to the specified value. If zero, use the default origin (center of the screen). *'<i>minLength</i> (optional)' - The minimum number of characters the input text must contain before the 'OK' button may be pressed; 'defaults to 1'. *'<i>maxLength</i> (optional)' - The maximum number of characters the input text may contain, after which further input is ignored. If omitted, or if zero, 'defaults to 32,767'. *'<i>switches</i> (optional)' - A bit-mask of switches, as specified below; 'defaults to 0'. <pre>1&#09;Digits only (including point) 2&#09;Pressing 'Enter' closes menu (instead of new line)</pre> | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions
SetTextInputString | JIP | SetTextInputString(textInput:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) | Sets the text string in the currently active 'Text Input Menu' to the specified 'formatted string'. Any text already in the menu will be discarded. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; String Functions; String Functions (JIP)

SetUIFloatAlt | NVSE | SetUIFloatAlt(traitName:string, newValue:float) -> bool | Sets the numeric value of a float UI trait. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns 1 (true) on successful change, 0 (false) otherwise. *Prior to xNVSE 6.2.5, this function did not return anything. Supersedes SetUIFloat, which is bugged for templated children tiles | Functions (NVSE); UI Functions
SetUIFloatGradual | JIP | SetUIFloatGradual(componentPath:string, startValue:float?, endValue:float?, duration:float?, changeMode:int?) | Gradually changes the value of the specified UI component trait, first setting it to '<i>startValue</i>', then gradually increases/decreases it toward '<i>endValue</i>', in '<i>duration</i>' seconds (unaffected by Time Mult (GetGlobalTimeMultiplier)). This enables creating simple animations using UI tiles. *The 1st argument '<i>componentPath</i>' should be of the format "MenuName\TileName\...\TraitName", as defined in the XML hierarchy for the particular menu. *The last (5th) argument '<i>changeMode</i>' is optional, and may be used to toggle "special" change modes, as follows: <pre>0&#09;(Default) From startVal to endVal in duration. 1&#09;From startVal to endVal in duration/6, endVal for duration*2/3, back to startVal in duration/6. 2&#09;From startVal to endVal back to startVal, in duration, repeated perpetually. 3&#09;From startVal to endVal, in duration, repeated perpetually.</pre> | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions; Functions_(ButcherPete FOSE); Functions (FO3)
SetUIString | FOSE1 | SetUIString(traitName:string, newValue:string) | Sets the value of a string UI trait. Trait name is of the format used by GetUIFloat. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); UI Functions; String Functions; Deprecated Functions
SetUIStringAlt | NVSE | SetUIStringAlt(TraitName:string, NewValue:string, <i>Formatting</i>:) -> bool | Sets the value of a string UI trait to a Formatted String. The trait name is of the format "MenuName/ElementName/ElementName/.../TraitName" as defined in the XML hierarchy for the desired menu, i.e. "MessageMenu/NoGlow_Branch/MM_Title/x". Returns 1 (true) on successful change, 0 (false) otherwise. *Prior to xNVSE 6.2.5, this function did not return anything. Supersedes SetUIString and SetUIStringEx, which are bugged for templated children tiles. | Functions (NVSE); UI Functions; String Functions
SetUIStringEx | NVSE | SetUIStringEx(TraitName:string, NewValue:string, <i>Formatting</i>:) -> None | Sets the value of a string UI trait. Trait name is of the format used by GetUIFloat. The path argument has two new capabilities. A path segment of "*" matches any child tile, and a path segment suffix of ":N" matches the N'th child tile. This is useful when accessing list box items, where the tile names are either identical, or unpredictable, or both. | Functions_(NVSE); UI Functions; String Functions; Deprecated Functions

SetWeather | GECK1 | SetWeather(WeatherID:ref, WeatherOverrideFlag:bool?) (alias: sw) | Changes weather using the games normal switching to the specified weather. The transition time is effected by the Trans Delta setting of the weather or if SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride has been used to extend the vanilla maximum of 30 seconds. If OverrideWeatherFlag is 1, the weather will not change again until ReleaseWeatherOverride is called. Otherwise, the weather will change back in the normal fashion according to the weather system. | Functions (GECK); Weather Functions

SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride | JIP | SetWeatherTransitionTimeOverride(transTime:int?) | Sets the amount of time (in real-time seconds) it takes for the game's 'Weather' to make a full transition to a new Weather type. This overrides the <i>Trans Delta</i> setting of <u>all</u> Weather types (which effectively limits the transition time to 30 seconds max), allowing for smoother, more gradual transitions. Calling this function with no arguments (or with 0) removes the override. | Functions_(JIP); Weather Functions (JIP); Weather Functions; Time Functions

SexChange | GECK1 | [actor:reference].SexChange(targetSex:Male/Female?, resetAppearance:bool{Default:0}?) | Changes the gender of the specified actor. The function has an optional parameter indicating the target sex (male, female). If no parameter is passed the actor changes to their opposite gender. If no calling reference is specified, the player is chosen by default. If resetAppearance is set to true (1) AND if the affected actor is the player AND if targetSex is set to change the actor's current gender (rather than keep the same one), then the player's appearance will be set to the default preset for their race+gender. This default preset can be seen when opening ShowRaceMenu and changing the race. | Functions (GECK)

If numSkills is specified, will override the amount of skills that could be allotted once the menu is open. StartCombat | GECK1 | [actor:ref].StartCombat(targetActor:ref) | Forces the actor to start combat with the specified ActorID. If the target actor is already in combat, the function will cause the attacker to target their combat group rather than that actor specifically. If the target is not immediatly visible to the calling actor reference, the calling actor will slowly make their way towards the targets position in a search like behavior. | Functions (GECK); Actor Functions; Combat Functions
StartConversation | GECK1 | sourceActor.StartConversation(targetActor:ref, topicID:baseform?, speakerLocationRef:ref?, targetLocationRef:ref?, headTrack:int?, allowMovement:int?) | Forces a conversation between two NPCs, optionally starting with a specified topic. The calling actor will seek out the target actor in order to have a conversation, no matter where the two NPCs are currently. This can also be used to have an NPC "force greet" the player, by setting the player as the TargetActor. The specified topic will be used instead of the normal Greeting topic, so the NPC can start the dialogue with any topic (use Greeting to simply have the normal greeting). | Functions (GECK); Dialogue Functions; Actor Functions
StartRadioConversation | GECK1 | [radiostation:ref].StartRadioConversation(topicID:baseform?) | StartRadioConversation will trigger a conversation over the specified radio station. The topicID parameter is optional. If a parameter is given, the conversation will begin with the specified topic. If no topic is specified, the station will play a topic according to normal conversation rules. | Functions (GECK); Radio Stations; Radio Functions

Stats and their values are stored in the save. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Stat Functions; Savebaked Functions

stolenItemOrContainer | JohnnyGuitar | thiefActor.stolenItemOrContainer(stolenItemOrContainer:refr, scanInventory:int(0/1)?) | Sends an alarm to the owner of an item or a container reference as if it was stolen. If the actor is not being detected by the owner of an item, it will result in a karma loss. <br><br> When using a container reference, optional argument can be used for alternative behavior - when set to 1, function will first scan calling actor's inventory for items stolen from the container's owner and dispatch the alarm if any were found. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); Crime_Functions

Sv_Compare | NVSE | Sv_Compare(Formatable:string, <i>Formatting</i>:|Optional = y?, ToCompareWith:string_var, CaseSensitive:bool?) -> int | Compares a formatable string to a string variable. Returns 0 if the strings are equal, 1 if the string variable occurs alphabetically before the formatted string, -1 if the string variable occurs alphabetically after the formatted string, or -2 if the comparison fails. Comparison is case-insensitive by default, with the optional flag reversing this. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Construct | NVSE | Sv_Construct(Literal:string, <i>Formatting</i>:) -> string_var | Returns a string variable constructed from the specified string and up to 20 formatting arguments. If no formatting is required, this function is not necessary, as the Let command can be used to assign string variables. Note that <b>Let</b> supports concatanation and the ToString($) function which can usually achieve the same result as formatting. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Count | NVSE | Sv_Count(toFind:string, 20xOptional:formatting?, within:string_var, startPos:int?, numChars:int?, caseSensitiveFlag:int?) -> int | Returns the number of occurrences of some sub string within a string variable. By default it is case insensitive, but an optional flag is available to change this. The first argument is a string, which may have optional formatting information thereafter. This is what you want to count. The second argument (ignoring the optional formatting) is a string variable within which to search for the first string. There are three optional int arguments at the very end: a start position and end position, which allow searching of a substring of the string_var, and finally, a boolean flag to enable case sensitivity. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Destruct | NVSE | Sv_Destruct(ToDestruct:string_var, optional-1:string_var, ...:, optional-9:string_var) -> None | Destroys the contents of up to ten String Variables, resetting their value to NULL (empty, zero). Unlike other variables, string variables might not be automatically destroyed when their script is, and potentially cause bloat if not destructed. All string variables created by a mod are automatically destroyed when the mod is unloaded, however. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Sv_Erase | NVSE | Sv_Erase(Source:string_var, StartPos:int, <i>HowMany:int</i>) -> none | Erases the specified number of characters from the specified string starting at the specified position. Omitting the HowMany argument will erase all characters from startPos to the end of the string variable. Note that this function modifies a string variable in place, and returns nothing. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Find | NVSE | Sv_Find(toFind:string, 20xOptional:formatting?, within:string_var, startPos:int?, numChars:int?, caseSensitiveFlag:int?) -> int | Returns the position of the first occurrence of a string in a string variable. The first argument is a string, which may have optional formatting information thereafter. This is what you want to find. The second argument (ignoring the optional formatting) is a string variable within which to search for the first string. There are three optional int arguments at the very end: a start position and end position, which allow searching of a substring of the string_var, and finally, a boolean flag to enable case sensitivity. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_GetChar | NVSE | Sv_GetChar(Source:string_var, Position:int) -> ASCIICode:int | Returns the ASCII code (0-127) for a single character in a string variable. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; Deprecated Functions
Sv_Insert | NVSE | Sv_Insert(Insertion:string, <i>Formatting</i>:, Target:string, Position:int) -> none | Inserts a formattable string (or string variable) into a target string variable at a given position. The target string variable is modified in place, and this function returns nothing. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Length | NVSE | Sv_Length(ToMeasure:string_var) -> int | Returns the length of a string variable, which is the number of characters it contains. Returns -1 if the string was invalid. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
sv_Percentify | NVSE | sv_Percentify(Source:string) -> string | Returns the input string with each '%' character replaced by two '%' characters. The argument can be any expression evaluating to a string value. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
sv_RegexMatch | JIP | sv_RegexMatch(targetSeq:string_var, regEx:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> match:0/1 | Returns whether the specified 'string_var' matches the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern. | String Functions; Functions (JIP); String Functions (JIP)
sv_RegexReplace | JIP | sv_RegexReplace(targetSeq:string_var, replacement&#124;pattern:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> modifiedSeq:string_var | Returns a copy of the specified 'string_var', with all matches to the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern replaced by the specified string. The <i>replacement</i> and <i>pattern</i> are both passed as a <u>single</u> formatted string, separated by a '&#124;', i.e. 'replacement&#124;pattern' (see example, below). | String Functions; Functions (JIP); String Functions (JIP)
sv_RegexSearch | JIP | sv_RegexSearch(targetSeq:string_var, regEx:formattedString, formatVars(up to 20):?) -> matches:array_var | Returns an 'array_var' containing all matches in the specified 'string_var' to the specified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern. | String Functions; Functions (JIP); String Functions (JIP); String Functions; Array Functions
Sv_Replace | NVSE | Sv_Replace(Old{{!:, <i>Formatting</i>:, Source:string_var, StartPos:int?, SearchLen:int?, CaseSensitive:bool?, MaxReplacements:int?) -> NumReplaced:int | Replaces occurrences of an old string with a new string within a string variable. Both the old and new text is passed in a single format string argument, separated by a pipe character as in "Old{{!}}New" (when calling from the console, use '@' in place of the pipe character). By default this function replaces all occurrence in the entire string and is case insensitive. Optionally, a range may be specified by start character and length thereafter, a case sensitive flag and a maximum occurrences to replace may be provided. Note that this function modifies a string_var in place, and returns the count of number of replacements as an int, not a new string. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
Sv_Set | NVSE | Sv_Set(Literal:string, <i>Formatting</i>:, Target:string_var) -> none | Sets the contents of a string variable to the passed formattable string. Although ported to NVSE, this function was deprecated in OBSE. It is simpler and better easier to use Let instead. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; Deprecated Functions
Sv_Split | NVSE | Sv_Split(ToSplit:string, Delimiters:string, JoinDelimiters:bool?) -> array | Splits the specified string based on the specified delimiter(s) and returns a regular array (list array) containing all substrings. Each character in the set of delimiters is matched individually, not as the complete string it is passed as. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
Sv_SubString | NVSE | Sv_SubString(Source:string_var, StartPos:int?, HowMany:int?) -> string | Returns a substring of a string variable. Although ported to NVSE, this function was deprecated in OBSE. It is simpler and better to use slice notation instead. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; Deprecated Functions
sv_ToLower | NVSE | sv_ToLower(Source:string) -> string | Returns the input string with all letters converted to lower case. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions

Valid characters include digits, a leading sign, and an '<b>e</b>' to indicate scientific notation. Conversion halts at the first invalid character. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions
sv_ToUpper | NVSE | sv_ToUpper(Source:string) -> string | Returns the input string with all letters converted to upper case. | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions
SwapTextureEx | JIP | SwapTextureEx(objectRef:ref, nifBlockName:string, texturePath:string, texSlotIndex:int) | Similar to the vanilla function SwapTextureOnRef - swaps a texture used by a particular 'Nif Block' on the specified object reference. However, unlike the vanilla version, which <i>only</i> allows swapping the main texture (diffuse map), this function allows swapping <i>any</i> of the six possible texture layers. This function takes the same parameters as the vanilla version, with one extra parameter for specifying the texture slot index (0-5). Both versions are otherwise functionally identical. | Functions (JIP); Miscellaneous Functions (JIP)

targetContainer | VEGAS1 | sourceContainer.targetContainer(targetContainer:reference, retainOwnershipFlag:(0/1), suppressMessages:(0/1), typeCode:int, exceptionFormlist:baseForm) | An extended version of RemoveAllItems. Removes all typed items (except those listed in the exemption FormList) from the calling reference's inventory, <u>except for the following:</u> *Biped forms marked as unplayable (LNIsPlayable), *If the calling reference is the player, Quest Items (IsQuestItem). ==Arguments== If a target container is specified, the typed items are moved to the target container with all equipment items retaining their current health; otherwise the items are destroyed as they would be via RemoveItem. If the retain ownership flag is set to a non-zero value, the items' original ownership is retained; otherwise the ownership is cleared. This is especially notable in the case of retaining ownership on items owned by someone else when transferring them to the player, as this may be counted as stealing. If the suppress messages flag is set to 1, the standard "Item Added" pop-up will not be displayed when this command is used to transfer items to the player. If the typeCode flag is omitted or set to -1, will try to remove all items, essentially doing the same thing as RemoveAllItems. If set to 0, will do nothing. Otherwise, this determines the Form Type ID of items that will be removed. If exceptionFormlist is specified, any form in that FormList will not be removed. {{:Inventory Item Type Codes}} | Functions (Vegas); Inventory Functions; Actor Functions
targetContainer | GECK1 | sourceContainer.targetContainer(targetContainer:ref, retainOwnershipFlag:int, hideMessages:int) | Removes all items from the calling reference's inventory, <u>except for the following:</u>: *Biped forms marked as unplayable (LNIsPlayable), *If the calling reference is the player, Quest Items (IsQuestItem). If a target container is specified, the items are moved to the target container with all equipment items retaining their current health; otherwise the items are destroyed as they would be via RemoveItem. If the retain ownership flag is set to a non-zero value, the items' original ownership is retained; otherwise the ownership is cleared. | Functions (GECK); Object Functions; Actor Functions
TempCloneForm | FOSE1 | reference.TempCloneForm(ToClone:baseForm, indexToCallingScript:bool) -> Clone:baseForm | Clones the specifies BaseForm, returning a new base form. If ToClone is omitted and a calling reference is provided, that reference's BaseForm is used. By default, a cloned form's ID has the 'FF' mod index, like dynamic objects generated during the course of gameplay. If indexToCallingScript is true, it will instead use the index of the mod to which the calling script belongs, taking the first available form ID in it. Either way, this cloned base form will not be persisted in the save game but the ref var containing the temporary form ID will be (which will be invalid on game load) so you must reinitialize the form on game restart. | Functions_(FOSE); Functions_(NVSE); Functions (FO3)

ToggleBipedSlotVisibility | JIP | ToggleBipedSlotVisibility(slotID:int, toggle:1/0?) -> isToggled:0/1 | Toggles the visibility of the specified 'Armor Biped Slot' when in 1st person POV. Normally, 'UpperBody', 'Left/RightHand' and 'PipBoy' are the only biped slots that are visible in 1st person, the rest being hidden. This function enables to override this limitation. *Calling this function will have no immediate effect on items that are already equipped. To apply the changes, simply call ReloadEquippedModels on the player: 'player.ReloadEquippedModels' *The 2nd argument may be omitted, in which case the function will only return the visibility state of the specified slot, without making any changes. | Functions (JIP); Armor Functions (JIP); Armor Functions; Miscellaneous Functions (JIP); Model Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)

ToggleHUDCursor | JIP | ToggleHUDCursor(toggle:1/0?) -> isToggled:0/1 | Toggles the menu mode cursor for the HUDMain menu. While toggled, mouse motion will control the cursor position, instead of the camera direction. Just as in <i>true</i> menu mode, the cursor is capable of activating and interacting with UI components (click/mouseover/scroll/drag etc.) that are part of the HUDMain menu. *Cursor mode is automatically switched off upon opening any game menu. *By omitting the argument, the function can be used to return whether cursor mode is active, without making any changes. | Functions (JIP); UI Functions (JIP); UI Functions

ToString | NVSE | ToString(ToConvert:exp) -> string | Attempts to convert an expression to a string. For numeric expressions, the result is a string representation of the numeric value. For objects, it is the name of the object if available; otherwise it is the formID expressed in hexadecimal notation. For string variables, conversion is only necessary for vanilla and old NVSE functions (eg: It is needed for PrintToConsole, but not for Print) You can also use the shorthand alias '$', which is an NVSE Expression | Functions_(NVSE); String Functions; ID Functions; NVSE-Aware Functions

The reference must be directly reachable to the calling actor, with no teleport doors in-between. Commonly used in traps, other "earth shaking" activators, and inebriation effects. The strength parameter can go up as high as you want, but anything higher than 4 or 5 starts to lose the desired effect. | Functions (GECK); Functions (FO3)

However, unlike <i>UnequipItem</i>, this function will also trigger the 'OnUnequip' script event (where applicable). | Functions_(JIP); Inventory Functions (JIP); Object Functions
UnequipItems | ShowOff | actorRefr.UnequipItems(flagsMask:int?, noEquip:int?, hideMessage:int?, triggerOnUnequip:int?) | Unequips certain items from the calling Actor reference. When called without specifying any arguments, will unequip all equipped Playable Items. Otherwise, filters and effects can be used to determine what items will be unequipped, and how. ==Parameters== 'flagsMask': A bitmask containing filters for what kinds of items to get. If flags is omitted / set to 0, the function will check for every playable equipped item. Otherwise, it can be used to specify which equip slots to check for, and further filters. *If an equipped item occupies multiple equip slots, the function will not count duplicates. 'noEquip': If set to 1, the actor (including the player) will be unable to equip the item. Once the item is equipped with the EquipItem function, the item can be equipped and unequipped as normal. *Disabled by default (if not provided a value). 'hideMessage': If set to 1, the item unequipped message will be hidden from the player. *Disabled by default (if not provided a value). 'triggerOnUnequip': If set to 1, will trigger the OnUnequip script block. *Enabled by default (if not provided a value). | Actor Functions; Inventory Functions; Bit Functions; Functions (ShowOff NVSE)

It is called on a Reference and takes a whopping nine parameters, though the last five are optional: * the weapon * where to stand * the target * how many times to use the weapon * whether to crouch when reloading [optional, default is true] * whether to hold fire when blocked [optional, default is true] * whether to automagically hit the target [for scripted forced kills; optional, default is false] * whether to prevent all damage [for scripted protracted fake combat; otpional, default is false] * where the target needs to stand [optional, default/NULL means "anywhere"] | Functions (GECK); AI Functions; Functions (FO3)

ValidateModIndex | JIP | ValidateModIndex(modIndex:int) -> validatedIndex:int | Attempts to resolve a <i>mod index</i> which was stored during a previous game session to account for any changes to the user's load order. Given the stored mod index for a mod, returns the mod index currently associated with that same mod, or -1 if the index could not be resolved (for instance, because the mod is no longer loaded). | Functions_(JIP); Utility Functions (JIP); File Functions; Functions_(Command Extender); Functions (FO3)
ValidateRegex | NVSE | ValidateRegex(regEx:string, outErrorStrVar:string_var?) -> match:0/1 | Tests whether the specified [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regular_expression Regular Expression] pattern is valid or not. This is <u>only</u> useful to test regex that is dynamically retrieved from a file or other dynamic source. If the regex is known beforehand, consider using an online validator. Possible return values are listed below: <pre> Error_FailedToExtractArgs = -1, NoErrors = 0, Error_Collate = 1, Error_Ctype = 2, Error_Escape = 3, Error_Backref = 4, Error_Brack = 5, Error_Paren = 6, Error_Brace = 7, Error_Badbrace = 8, Error_Range = 9, Error_Space 10, Error_Badrepeat = 11, Error_Complexity = 12, Error_Stack = 13, Error_Unknown = 14 </pre> If an error occurs and outErrorStrVar is set to a valid string variable, then that string variable will be set to contain information explaining the nature of the error. | NVSE-Aware Functions; Functions (NVSE); String Functions

WeaponHasModType | JIP | WeaponHasModType(weapon:form, modType:int) -> modSlot:int | Returns whether or not the passed 'Weapon' (1st argument), which can be either a base form, or a reference of a weapon placed in the world, has an optional weapon mod of the specified type. If it does, the function returns the index (1-3) of the weapon mod. Otherwise, 0 is returned. | Functions (JIP); Weapon Functions (JIP)
while | NVSE | while(Condition:expression) | Whilst a specified condition is met, the code section below, until the <b>loop</b> command, will repeat. Within while loops, the command <b>continue</b> may be used to skip remaining loop code and return to the while line. In the same context, the command <b>break</b> may be used to end the loop immediately, regardless of the while condition. The same effect could be achieved using <b>label</b>, <b>goto</b>, <b>if</b> and <b>endif</b> statements, but this method allows for fewer lines and improved readability. Care must be taken to avoid the possibility of infinite loops- a while condition that never becomes false will immediately crash the game. | Functions_(NVSE); Commands; Scripting; NVSE-Aware Functions
Worldspace | JohnnyGuitar | Worldspace(Worldspace:ref, filepath:string) | Sets map texture for a specified 'Worldspace' using string filepath relative to "Data\Textures". | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE)

If zone argument is omitted, worldspace's Encounter Zone will be removed. | Functions (JohnnyGuitar NVSE); World Space_Functions; Encounter_Zone_Functions
